Title: Lock the layout
Body:
Must needed, because I accidentally holded the apps in drawer that were already placed in pie & completely changed the locations. This also disrupts the directions & rotates the pie that I've set up.  

Layout lock idea is to avoid drag & drops inside drawer. This lock toggle should be enabled/disabled from configuration.  

### PS
Locking the layout might increase steps to re-edit the pie but very useful for those who setup the pie once & for all.

Title: Folders
Body:
I think it'd be really cool if the pie menu could have folders. I'm really digging the idea of having exactly 8 icons (and thus 8 directions) to swipe as it makes it super easy to get to apps without looking at my phone. However, I use more than 8 apps frequently, and adding more apps to the pie menu decreases the activation zones.

Solution: Folders. Just drag two icons on top of each other to create a folder. I have two ideas on how folder selection works.

Idea 1: when user swipes toward folder without releasing, the folder opens and the pie menu becomes the contents of the folder. Releasing without selecting an app closes the folder and the pie menu goes back to root. This could make folder interactions really snappy, but could result in some icons being outside the screen depending on where the user first taps.

Idea 2: the user selects the folder like any other app. When a folder is opened, the next time the user brings up the pie menu, it will be the folder contents. Tapping empty space will go up one folder. This solves the problem from the first option where apps could go outside the screen, tho it may be slightly more clunky. In this case, maybe there should be some sort of indicator to show that the pie menu is currently inside a folder.

Title: Shadows for pie menu icons
Body:
Have you thought about adding shadows to the pie menu icons?
I think it would look quite good since it would elevate the icons above the wallpaper.

There are also situations were shadows would help substantially with the visibility of the icons.
If someone uses a single color wallpaper and has an app with the same color icon it becomes difficult to see the icon since they blend together.

Title: Highlight remove/uninstall areas when dragging app over them
Body:
When dragging an app over the remove and uninstall areas It would be nice if they changed color to highlight what action would be taken when the app is dropped.

Additionally, showing the action name  when hovered over could be nice.

Title: Feature Request: Vibration/haptics setting
Body: Would it be possible to add a vibrate option when changing which app is being hovered over? Example: long press to open pie menu, hover over sms app, haptic activates, switch to hovering over web browser, haptic activates. I'd suggest a fairly short length, like how the vibrate works on an on-screen keyboard, but a customizable setting would obviousy be more flexible. Thanks for the great app!

An horizontal list of "modes" made by the user at the bottom of the home screen. The user would be able to select 1 "mode" and changing the selected mode by touching another. Title: [Feature Request] User Configured Press/Hold Duration, Double Tap instead of single tap
Body:
Accidental presses / brushes launch apps incredibly well in this launcher.

Some suggestions.

Allow the user to configure how long a "tap" needs to last to be considered a tap.

Allow the user to configure how long a "hold" needs to last to be considered a hold.

A slightly longer requirement for holding to register as a hold for the hold & slide selection would be fantastic, as would the capability to opt to use a double tap to open the apps drawer instead of a single tap.

Single tap  could then be repurposed to turn the screen off or go back to the last app / open the active windows chooser etc.

Probably not plausible due to how the app functions / gesture limitations but losing the tap screen to turn screen off gesture and the lack of configurability to reduce accidental touch app launches are the primary reasons  I can't stick with this launcher in its current iteration.

Largely just providing user feedback, have a wonderful day.
Thank you for taking the time to read.

Title: Improve icons
Body:
Hi, you've created a very nice launcher!

One thing that was kind of confusing to me was the fact that two icons didn't make a lot of sense. Here's my proposal:

First icon:
![Screenshot_20210807-223223_Pie Launcher.jpg](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/59611881/128627160-c0c11bc3-21f3-4a68-b797-652f68a08135.jpg)

The plus-icon doesn't seem semantically correct. It doesn't send the user to a dedicated add-app-screen as it seems to suggest. Instead, it sends him back to the app-collection.
Therefore, i suggest either using a back-icon or some kind of apps-icon.
[This one, the one titled "Apps"](https://fonts.google.com/icons?selected=Material+Icons&icon.query=apps) seems perfect.

__

Second Icon:
![Screenshot_20210807-223250_Pie Launcher.jpg](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/59611881/128627335-9eca19b1-009e-49ab-959f-4dae293e1af6.jpg)

The `×` is a great choice, but in my opinion the right icon should be displaced. It doesn't delete the app, but instead opens the app-info/settings. Thus, either using a settings-icon or an info-icon would be best.

I think info would be the most clear choice.

Title: Copy images to own folder
Body: Android has a built in limit of 512 grants to Uris an app can have. If someone wants to select more images, it should ask, if they want the app to copy the images to its app folder, so it has access to more. The images should be shrinked to a resolution fitting for the screen size, so it doesn't bloat the memory up.

Title: Too much RAM consuming
Body:
I tried your app today, and it's really nice. There's only one quite big issue. The background service takes too much RAM. On my device it takes more than 140MB, with 30 images selected. Would it be possible to make it work without a background service? At least, make it use less. It's too much for such a simple live wallpaper.
I use a Nokia 6.2, with stock Android 11.

Title: Open Current Image
Body:
It should be possible to open the image, that is currently displayed, in the gallery. This could happen with the following actions (Still to be determined):
- Some kind of tap on the background, but not only one tap or a long press, because these are used for interacting with the home screen itself. Maybe a double tap?
- A widget that opens the image (Perhaps also displaying some info about the current image)

Title: Multiple slideshows to select from
Body:
Add the ability to create multiple slideshows to select from. To select the slideshow to use, the following items should be available:
- Select one slideshow directly
- Randomly select one of multiple (Is related to the next point)
- Switch the slideshow at specific intervals or times of day

Further research: How can we add the ability to select different slideshows for home screen and lock screen? Ideas:
- Provide two Live Wallpaper definitions in the manifest (Might be confusing)
- Dynamically create a live wallpaper for each slideshow (Is that possible?)
- Select which slideshow to use on home screen and lock screen separately inside the app (Requires set wallpaper permission)

Title: Use share to add images
Body: Add a share option for images, so they can be added from outside.

Title: Select whole folder
Body: It should be possible to add the images of a complete folder, instead of having to select every image inside that folder individually.

Title: Order Images
Body: Let the user move the images in the selection, so the selection order can be changed.

Title: Transitions
Body: Add selectable transitions. When the displayed image changes, this should happen with the user selected transition.

Title: Feature request: Add task switcher support.
Body:
On Android 10 and newer, the [task switcher](https://developer.android.com/guide/components/activities/recents) is directly built into the launcher (the "default" task switcher is gone).
This means that if you try to use TL in a ROM without other launchers, the overview button will do nothing and in some cases, bootloop.
My request is to add a task switcher. You can just use the same layout as the main screen, put an "X" on the right, and add a "---CLOSE ALL---" entry on the top, which closes all tasks.

Title: Improvements to the requests file
Body:
(Continuation of the discussion in the comments of #1822)

Some possible improvements to the requests file:
- Move to a machine readable format to make it easier for scripts to interact with it. My suggestion would be YAML since it has a good tradeoff between human and machine readability. Example of a possible structure:
```yaml
requests:
  - name: OnStream
    appfilter: <item component="ComponentInfo{com.maertsno.m/com.maertsno.m.ui.main.MainActivity}" drawable="onstream"/>
    links:
      - https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.maertsno.m
      - https://f-droid.org/en/packages/com.maertsno.m/
    request_count: 14
    last_request_time: 1700051822
```
- Only include app store links that actually work. This would likely involve a script that looks up the app IDs of new requests in multiple app stores to see if they have the requested app. I have already written code previously that can be used for this (currently checks F-Droid, IzzyOnDroid and Google Play) and I would be willing to write the script for this if desired.
- ~~Remove "Last requested time" to reduce file size if it's not going to be used for anything.~~
I didn't realize it was being used to filter old requests. My bad.

Title: [WIP] show keyboard when display history
Body:
For my user experience this is an improvement. You can only have so many favorites and I like to keep the home screen very minimalistic. I always know what app I want to launch and so I almost always prefer to get to said app by simply typing in the first characters of the app name. The `searchEditText` search bar is a pretty small target compared to the rest of the screen. So, with this change I can click anywhere on the home screen that is not occupied by a widget and get right to typing to search for an app I want to launch. I have found this to be more efficient than having to contort my fingers to reach the search bar at the bottom of the home screen, and searching by name is quicker than scrolling through a long list of history or the whole list of installed apps.

Open to discuss if this would be considered useful for other users and/or should be a new preference option. It is similar in nature to that option: "Show history with keyboard — Show history when software keyboard is visible". Except it's essentially the reverse; to show the software keyboard when history is visible.

Title: Add time of day to history modes
Body:
This feature has been requested a few times that I can see. What this does is add a the time of day as an option for history modes. I used the same style of utilizing a SQLite query to handle the heavy lifting and the resulting code is quite simple, keeping up with the KISS philosophy. It may not be immediately obvious what is happening so I made a desmos graph to show how the one-liner equation performs this here: [desmos](https://www.desmos.com/calculator/g4t1uvisxq). The two sliders represent two of the same pojo records with an adjustable time stamp, the chart is adjusted to show 24 days. The black line is the resulting value for that specific pojo record with time being the x axis. 

I have been testing this for quite a few days now and it has become my favorite history mode. It combines frecency with time of day since more frequently accessed records tend to transform the triangle wave into a line, and recency since accessing the item just once resets the time decay. For any further questions on how it works please ask! If you end up accepting this I will be happy to update the docs and do some translation work (I can't guarantee translations as I would just utilize one of the many tools to blindly translate). 

resolves #2231 
resolves #1523

Title: Memorize specific application usage at specific times
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
No, not a problem but I, for example, do exercises every morning and use a specific application at a specific time but only once a day. So the application disappears after some time. Every morning I have to type one letter to look for the app.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
New feature - write a code so that an application used at a regular time/day appears automatically at the bottom of the list during a time range (without having to type and search).
For example, because I do exercises every morning, the app is proposed between 7h00 and 7h30 and once used is no more shown until the next morning.

**Describe alternatives you've considered, or that are already available within KISS**
Adding as a favorite - no because I don't need the app at any other time.

**Additional context**
This can be useful for persons who use an application to open a gate every morning but do not need it afterwards, or have to pay transport in the 8h15 bus every morning, play music in class after recreation...

**Would you be able to contribute code?**
No, I don't know how to...

Thank you 🙏

Title: Add/filter by phone type
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I have many business contacts, which still have a fax number. I marked all those numbers with the `FAX` tag. It does not make sense to display these on a phone, because I very rarely phone a fax number. Often it is not even possible to decide by inspecting the number, if this is a fax number or not.

Now I need to open the contact to see the proper number.

A similar problem occurs, if you have tagged the phone numbers e.g. with `WORK` or `MOBILE` (see #169)

**Describe the solution you'd like**
The best solution would be:
1. Add a filter to filter out phone numbers by tags.
2. Improve the display of the phone number:
    - Coalesce all numbers under the contact name.
    - If there is a number marked as favourite, display that at the top of the list of phone numbers, e.g. in bold.
    - If at all possible, add the tag (#169 is marked as resolved, but I cannot see anything like it).

**Describe alternatives you've considered, or that are already available within KISS**
You can always open the contact details, if there are more than one number. But that complicates things …

**Would you be able to contribute code?**
Unfortunately not, since my knowledge about programming for Android is virtually null.

In the case of search limit I'd prefer to set it as high as possible so I can have all the results present and if it's something rarely used I'd still prefer to scroll up than to have to search for it some other way.

Title: Explicitly changing the language of the app names to index
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I would like to search app names in a language different from the system language.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
It would be nice to have an option to explicitly select the language (in particular the names of the apps that are indexed for the search) to a different one as the system language, or a flag that disables translation at all.

**Additional context**
Especially if you use a CJK language, you are exposed to problems such as described in
https://github.com/Neamar/KISS/issues/1930.
Unfortunately, some apps I use only work correctly when setting the system language to a specific CJK language, which makes it hard to use the KISS launcher for searching apps.
A solution would be to search app names in English (US), regardless of what language the system language is set.

Possibly related issue: https://github.com/Neamar/KISS/issues/1967

Title: Android 14 update -> Frequent crashes
Body:
Years long and huge fan of KISS <3
**Describe the crash**
With the update to Android 14 on my Google Pixel 6 Pro, KISS seems to frequently crashes when returning to the home screen. I specifically disabled Battery optimization in the hopes, that this was involved, but no, didn't affect anything.
~I can't pin the exact circumstance as to when it happens,~ but it just happens sometimes. Roughly 15 times a day.

The crash looks like this: Upon returning to the home screen, the whole screen goes blank, just the wallpaper seen and KISS re-initializes. The Clock Widget appears half a second later, followed by all the icons a second later.

**To Reproduce**
~Can't pin down a specific cause yet.~

Able to reproduce 100% consistently now, see comment https://github.com/Neamar/KISS/issues/2188#issuecomment-1773697017 below.

**Stack trace**
See https://github.com/Neamar/KISS/issues/2188#issuecomment-1791776081

**Additional information**
 - App version: `3.20.1`
 - Did the crash start happening with this version? -  Yes
 - App installed via: (f-droid, Play Store, debug APK): F-Droid

Title: Keyboard does not appear on start
Body:
**Describe the bug**
Even with _Display keyboard on start_ toggled on in the KISS settings, gesturing home does not automatically pull up the keyboard. I need to make the home gesture a _second_ time, while I am already on the home screen, in order to open the keyboard. But if I change Android settings from gesture navigation to 3-button navigation, the keyboard opens as expected. 

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Open any app
2. Make home gesture to open launcher
3. Once launcher is open, make home gesture a second time in order to actually open the keyboard

**Expected behavior**
Whenever the launcher is opened, the keyboard should be open and ready for me to start typing. 

**Additional information**
 - App version: 3.20.1
 - App installed via: F-droid

**Additional context**
This has been functioning well for years, but I first noticed this change in behavior after updating to Android 14 (the latest GrapheneOS update).

Title: [BUG] extension keeps downloading same image everytime the wallpaper autochanges into previously downloaded artwork
Body:
I am not sure exactly how to word this issue. 
Here's the problem i'm facing. 
FYI, I've chosen recommend mode since it's the most stable mode in the extension. 
So basically, once i choose recomend mode and it downloads a few artworks to display, forcibly changing wallpaper either through gesture or clicking the "next" button, causes the app to redownload the same image. 
This results in multiple duplicates being created + no new images being downloaded since only the old images are being redownloaded everytime. 
Something seems to be fundamentally broken at the moment.

Also, the extension regularly crashes when switching wallpapers quickly (without waiting for the fade transition to play out) though this seems to be a separate issue on it's own.

Title: Fit the artworks to screen
Body:
A user has reported that on their very tall phone, landscape art does not look good requiring a lot of scrolling to see the entire artwork.


This could be implemented by somehow adding letterboxes or pillars to the artwork. I can override openFile() in PixivArtProvider, and apply the fitting there, thus preserving the original artwork.

I can take this a step further by adding a slider for how pillars the app should add. E.g. a slider maxed out would result in a tiny landscape artwork in the center of a portrait screen, which a slider halfway would result in the artwork occoupying mostly the center of the screen with some excess hanging off either side.

An issue would be if the user has rotation enabled on their home screens. I can provide only one copy of the artwork to Muzei (they keep its own local copy), so i can only provide one orientation of scaling

Title: [Bug (Plus)] Unable to download TFLite model
Body:
Android 14 (Pixel 7a and Samsung S23 OneUI 6.0)

I think either after the move from TFHub -> Kaggle, or in a recent release, something has changed in the downloading of models from tfhub.dev - even the EfficentDet-Lite0 model cannot be downloaded

I managed to get the below log from logcat, weirdly I am able to download it from my browser though and it's coming down as `1.tflite` as the filename (at first I was thinking they completely broke the download URLs on the move, but thankfully not)

```
doWork: Error downloading https://tfhub.dev/tensorflow/lite-model/efficientdet/lite0/detection/metadata/1?lite-format=tflite
java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Could not parse file name from response
	at kotlin.ResultKt.createFile(SourceFile:41)
	at kotlin.ResultKt.download(SourceFile:310)
	at com.ammar.wallflow.workers.HelpersKt$download$1.invokeSuspend(Unknown Source:15)
	at kotlin.coroutines.jvm.internal.BaseContinuationImpl.resumeWith(Unknown Source:8)
	at kotlinx.coroutines.DispatchedTask.run(Unknown Source:96)
	at androidx.work.Worker$2.run(SourceFile:31)
	at kotlinx.coroutines.scheduling.TaskImpl.run(Unknown Source:2)
	at kotlinx.coroutines.scheduling.CoroutineScheduler$Worker.run(SourceFile:92)
```

I'll have a go through the source to see if I can spot where it might be going wrong, but thought I'd raise it first

I've tried this on a Google Pixel 7a and Samsung Galaxy S23 and they are both having the same issue (although I was only able to try downloading a 'custom' tflite model instead of the default one on the S23)

I also tried uploading the 1.tflite file to an object storage bucket to download from there and that also didn't work though - same error

Body:
Cannot import wallpaper on Android 13 (Tiramisu) Beta

The permission `Manifest.permission.READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE` is required to import the wallpaper with `WallpaperManager.getDrawable()`. 
On Android 13 with targetSdkVersion set to 33 this permission cannot be requested anymore (if it was requested in an earlier version it does persist though).
Instead of the permission dialog, the user sees nothing and the permission request is automatically denied.
So the `getDrawable()` call would fail with `java.lang.SecurityException: Permission android.permission.READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE denied for package`

https://github.com/cvzi/darkmodewallpaper/blob/9527ba9cdd8b4f81e5befdcdf2f6c237bb1de1d0/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml#L27

https://github.com/cvzi/darkmodewallpaper/blob/9527ba9cdd8b4f81e5befdcdf2f6c237bb1de1d0/app/src/main/java/com/github/cvzi/darkmodewallpaper/activity/MainActivity.kt#L1167-L1173

https://github.com/cvzi/darkmodewallpaper/blob/9527ba9cdd8b4f81e5befdcdf2f6c237bb1de1d0/app/src/main/java/com/github/cvzi/darkmodewallpaper/activity/MainActivity.kt#L156-L168

Upstream report:
https://issuetracker.google.com/issues/237124750

Title: Android 9
Body:
Android 9 has support for system dark mode (even though the API is a bit different maybe, I'm not sure), do you think it would be possible to add support for it too?

(It should be possible because I've seen other app do that, but I'd prefer to use yours because it's without ads, unlike the other.)

Title: Slideshow support
Body:
Hello, thank you for the app
It's great that someone finally came to that idea

Please, if it will be possible, in the future add separate slideshow support for light and for dark themes 
(As example, user chooses one folder that contains images for light theme and the other folder with images for dark theme)

Good luck to you

Title: Lockscreen wallpaper?
Body: I'd like to export my lockscreen wallpaper but I'm not seeing anything for that in the app.

Title: Random isn't so random
Body: I have Muzei set up on three devices and the three devices use the same images, I copy them across to each device using SyncThing. So it's really nice to see the images on all devices, but there's a slight problem. While I love that Muzei goes through the new pictures before going back to being random, it's not actually picking from the whole pool. I'm seeing the same images used on all devices and out of a pool of near 1500 pictures, I shouldn't be noticing the same pictures being used. Is there anyway to improve this?

Title: Allow for filtering/searching on browse screen
Body: Users are able to select a wallpaper directly from the browse screen, but there's no way to filter/search wallpapers. It would be nice if that could be added.

Title: Add confirmation dialog before removing all photos
Body:
Hello,

My photos > 3 dots >  "Remove all photos"
This button is quite brutal and doesn't check for confirmation before deletion
Here is my attempt to change it, it still needs translations and I'm no kotlin dev so feel free to trash it.

Thank you for this cool app ! 👍

Title: [Feature Request] Allow dimming to affect wallpaper color for system themes
Body:
_(I want to preface this request by saying I don't actually know how the internal systems involved here work, so I may be making some off-base assumptions.)_

On Samsung phones with OneUI as the launcher, text labels for apps/folders/etc. are rendered in black or white depending on the overall color/brightness of the current wallpaper.

When Muzei is configured with significant dimming, even solid white wallpapers show up as fairly dark. Unfortunately, the wallpaper still reports as bright white in that case, so text is rendered in black on a dimmed background, which makes it unreadable.

An alternative would be to allow the user to force Muzei to always report a bright or dark wallpaper, perhaps, but I think taking the dim value into consideration (or at least providing an option to do so) is probably the more natural approach.

Title: Different sources for lock and home screen
Body: It would be very nice if it would be possible to select different sources for the home and lock screen.

Title: Support Widget Only Use-Case
Body:
A personal feature request:

I'd like to use the Muzei widget only, whilst using my own wallpaper.

Currently this only partially works, as whilst the Muzei widget will display a picture, Muzei will stop getting new pictures, and if you click the picture it prompts you to setup the live wallpaper instead of showing the picture in fullscreen like usual.

I think it'd be great to be able to use Muzei as just a widget because I am fond of my own wallpaper, and just want to see some new art on the widget once in a while

Thanks for considering
ayaya

Title: Changing dim color (a future to add)
Body: It would be great if there was an option to change the dim color (#XXYYZZ INSTADE OF #000000)...

Release notes

Sourced from fast-xml-parser's releases.

v4

  • Keeping cjs modules as they can be imported in cjs and esm modules both. Otherwise refer esm branch.

  • refactoring to use Object.assign
  • refactoring to remove repeated code

  • support global stopNodes expression like "*.stop"

Title: Black screen when loading wallpaper
Body:
Hi,
I am using a Galaxy Fold 4 with Android 13.
I supplied a folder of images i want to use as a wallpaper, but everytime I unlock my device the wallpaper reloads and I am facing a black background. Is this known or maybe due to the unusual form factor?

Title: Option to treat different folders as separate sources when providing images form local device
Body: The current behavior is that it adds all images from selected folders into the queue. I wish there was an option to treat them as separate sources and choose between displaying pics of one folder at a time.

Title: Add Material You Theming
Body:
It would be nice if when Muzei was set as your wallpaper, the theming of Muzei would be pulled from the wallpaper colors ala [Material You](https://material.io/blog/announcing-material-you) rather than always using a blue color (like it does now).

There's some challenges here (as by default you can't change the color pulled from a theme without reloading the entire activity...which is extremely disruptive in Muzei's case given that the Next Artwork button in the app changes the wallpaper). This may mean that more dynamic theming only possible with [Jetpack Compose](https://developer.android.com/jetpack/compose/) may be a prerequisite for doing this work.

Title: Change wallpaper scroll speed
Body: I would appreciate being able to slow down the speed and distance which the wallpaper scrolls when I switch from one home screen to another.  It just feels a little bit jarring at full speed as it is.

Title: Display specific picture of the current month possible?
Body:
Dear team,

I noticed by checking your default image source on wikiart, that I missed several great paintings.

So I would love to load that missed ones by clicking in them, maybe from a "images of the current month"-gallery.

Or maybe a "restart display of current month's paintings" function.

 Is it possible to implement these features? 

That would be great 🤓.

Kind Regards,

K.

Title: Optionally allow auto-advancing offline
Body:
Currently, Muzei is only able to auto-advance when online, but it is also capable of advancing offline using the "skip" button. I think this option could be added in the menu:

![muzei](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/35870988/115910722-766a4a80-a46d-11eb-88f3-f914842fbaa1.png)

When offline, just like when using the "skip" button, Muzei would show preloaded images if any are available, then fall back to random selection.

Ideally the "last downloaded" timestamp should not be updated, so Muzei can fetch fresh images as soon as the device comes online, even if it shouldn't change the image yet.

Along with this, I think it would be good to preload a few more images than currently, especially for the people who using that option: it would allow to get fresh images even if the device is offline for a small amount of time.

Title: Download current image
Body: Some resources support downloading the current image to a selected directory. There is a separate application that calls Explorer to select where to save the current image (regardless of source). I would like to be able to download current wallpapers natively, without using hacks.

Title: Feature request: 3D parallax effect
Body:
Hello all, 
I discovered this amazing app only recently and I love the ability to configure different effects for home and lock screen. The only thing which I'm missing is 3D parallax effect. I found that this feature suggestion was rejected in 2014 due to possible CPU issues, but maybe could be re-evaluated now. I was using another 3D app extensively during past years and it had no visible impact on the battery, so I'd say that it's safe to use with negligible impact on resources (https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=opotech.image3Dlwp). Could you please re-consider adding this cool feature?

Thanks in advance!

Title: Android Q: Support multiple displays
Body: As per [the documentation](https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/ui/foldables#wallpaper), Muzei should add `android:supportsMultipleDisplays="true"` to our `wallpaper.xml` to ensure we support multiple displays.

Title: Source with lots of artwork
Body:
In my app I have a JSON file with over 600 image URLs. I have two questions:

1. If I add them all using `ProviderContract.Artwork.addArtwork` inside `onLoadRequested` method, will Muzei download all of them right away?
  
2. When I open my source in `BrowseProviderFragment`, Muzei will start to download all the images (over 1GB), which is really bad. Can we add `thumbnail` property to `Artwork` so it will be displayed instead of the full image?
  
Thank you.

Title: Allow Tasker actions to change effects
Body: Allow blur amount, dimming, artwork source or any other setting to be changed from Tasker. Smoothly animated just like current double tap to unblur.

Title: Less blurry blur effect
Body: Even applying the minimal amount of blur effect is too blurry for me, glad if you could reduce the minimum amount of blur I could add. Thanks.

Title: Feature request: Add a skip option
Body:
I use NASA’s APOD source, most of the time the image is a great wallpaper, but sometimes it’s something which does not work well as a wallpaper, for example: https://apod.nasa.gov/apod/ap180314.html

In these cases, I would like to have a skip button, which would put back the last wallpaper and wait for the next one to switch.

Title: Use ExifInterface's getRotationDegrees/isFlipped
Body: Use [getRotationDegrees()](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/support/media/ExifInterface.html#getRotationDegrees()) and [isFlipped()](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/support/media/ExifInterface.html#isFlipped()) to correctly orient images.

Title: Create a Favorites art source
Body:
Muzei should provide a 'Favorites' art source. Users would be able to favorite any artwork from other sources. These favorite images would be saved by Muzei without needing to manually download the image or worry about where it is stored.

Ideally, these favorite images could be associated with your Google account so when setting up a new device you'd have access to your old favorites just by logging in.

See discussion on [this Google+ post](https://plus.google.com/109038422348319759883/posts/bkQiZwQVRsy).

Title: Android O: Evaluate usage of Autosizing TextViews
Body: Evaluate whether it makes sense to switch any of our `TextView`s to be [Autosizing TextViews](https://developer.android.com/preview/features/autosizing-textview.html) to handle differing amounts of text.

Title: Android O: Evaluate Content Provider Paging
Body: [Content Provider paging](https://developer.android.com/about/versions/oreo/android-8.0.html#cpp) could prove useful to Muzei as it uses Content Providers extensively.

Title: Make the Muzei blur, dim, etc part of a larger effects system
Body: It would be convenient to allow developers to ship separate effects that would plug into Muzei. For example, this would allow effects like the [live rain wallpaper](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.andrewou.weatherback) to be used alongside Muzei's art plugin system.

Title: Allow users to select multiple active sources
Body: Similar to how [Multimuze](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.getgoodcode.multimuze) works, it would be nice to be able to select multiple sources at the same time, rotating between them at some set amount of time or on a schedule, etc.

Title: App Timer Icon Doesn't Disappear
Body:
I have app timer icons set in Digital Wellbeing on Samsung OneUI. When an app time limit is reached, Kvaesitso puts a timer icon on the bottom right corner. The next day when the timer is reset, the icon remains. The timer icon goes away only upon reboot or force closing Kvaesitso.

![Screenshot_20240120_182816_Kvaesitso.jpg](https://github.com/MM2-0/Kvaesitso/assets/75694650/19bb1cac-4514-4bef-94d9-9567cd982138)

Maybe the app should reset these icons at midnight each night?

Title: [Feature Request] Calendar widget work profile integration
Body: It would be extremely valuable to me if the calendar widget could be able to support accessing the calendars of the "work" profile as well. At the moment it only detects the "main" profile ones.

Title: [bug] Icon of just uninstalled app still visible, clicking on it crashes Kvaesito

Title: [Feature Request] Disable separation of work profile and main profile
Body:
It would be nice if it were possible to combine the work profile apps and the normal ones into one app drawer, disabling the current tabbed approach.

This makes more sense in the context of using work profile as a way to have multiple copies of one app instead instead of separating personal and work things.

Title: Clock Suggestion
Body:
Tapping on the clock currently opens the alarm Settings, but not the full clock app on my Samsung S9+.

My suggestion: 
Option to choose wich/if any app is opened when tapping the clock

Title: Media widget doesnt open the right app
Body: Long pressing the Media control widget opens spotify even though its showing the title and Album Art of the playing video from YouTube or Podcast from Pocketcast.

Title: Issues and suggestions
Body:
When long pressing on an app, the app has a lot of notifications you can't scroll down at all, i don't understand why the notifications are there in the first place but perhaps it was implemented for a reason, but there should be a way to scroll down from there 

Also a way to change the icon pack icon or change the appearance for a specific icon instead of applying to all and the option to hide text labels, at least in the favourites widget

Also for the web search Google sucks, not the search engine but the app, it's better to use the chrome app for this the way smart launcher does it, so hoping to see that implemented too

Title: Feature Request
Body:
It's a great launcher by far and ability to hide nav bar and status bar is just great however;

• I'll be great if you could add battery percentage on the home screen maybe just beside the date or in any way or style thats awesome 😃

• someway to add swipe-able notifications popups or simply their icons with some cool animations would be a great addition to already good app
Looking forward to it<3
Starbowiii.

Title: Media control for work profile apps
Body:
Hello,

On my phone I have Spotify in the work profile using shelter. Unfortunately I am not able to control it using the media control widget, as only apps from the main profile can be selected as media apps. It would be cool if I was able to select the work profile app as well.

Thanks!

Title: [Feature Request] Add configuration options to calendar widget
Body:
- There is a default hardcoded option to always hide "running events from past days". Even if you click on the event to show it, it will hide it back if you change the date. I'd suggest making this behaviour an option in the calendar widget configuration.
- Imo, calendar widget usability would greatly improve if we could select the number of days we want to display (current is hardcoded to one, adding an option for "Next 3 days" or make it entirely configurable for instance).

Thanks a lot for the hard work, this launcher is, by far, the best I ever tried!

Title: Use Material You Compat on Android 12+ if flag is enabled
Body:
Some customization layers such as One UI or MIUI don't retrieve a meaningful color on wallpaper change (maybe this is just done on manual wallpaper selection, which kinda misses the purpose of automatic theming...), and on previous versions, for example, Android 11, still on One UI, this launcher shows more coherent colors most of the time.

This PR suggests a new settings flag under "Appearance" to ignore system colors on API 31+ and use same color quantization scheme for Android 8+, technically skipping the conditional that asks system its colors.

Could close #524 or related if it is merged.

Title: Allow renaming or hiding the Favorites tab beneath the Favorites list when tags are enabled.
Body: When the Favorites app list is pinned above the app drawer (settings > search > favorites) in combination with tags the _Favorites_ tab/label beneath the Favorites app list takes up a lot of space. I would like to be able to either hide it or rename it in order to allow for additional space for more visible tags without having to search for them.

Title: [FR] Additional lines of favorite apps section options
Body:
Settings to determine the number of lines for the favorite apps section. Currently, from my counting, there should be a total of 10 favorite apps lines (possibly according to this issue: https://github.com/MM2-0/Kvaesitso/issues/534). I appreciate the current 10 lines. However, I have way too many apps which I use in some capacity that will fit in the favorite apps section. I request the ability to set additional lines for the favorite apps section within settings. Something insane such as 50, 100, or even infinite. Actually, perhaps infinite is too much. Maybe even 50 is a little overboard. Still though, I would appreciate something to this degree immensely. 

Sincerely, UnknownAzzwipe

Title: [A13] Actions desync
Body:
Quite frequently (I upgraded to A13 today, had it happen 3 times), the launcher loses sync with what I am doing and does the previous action.

For example, I try to open app A and app B (which is the previously opened app) opens.
This happens for any press action when this bug is triggered, even the hamburger menu.
A force-close of the launcher fixes it.

I am not quite sure how to repro, but it's quite frequent.

Title: Web search doesn't recognise Chromite browser
Body:
When clicking on web search and asked to select a browser, it lists a few but doesn't include Chromite. Is this an issue with Kvaesitso or Chromite itself?

![Screenshot_20231209-111906](https://github.com/MM2-0/Kvaesitso/assets/55948138/dbe91060-fbad-4066-9112-9c471d26134b)

Title: Time, Forcast, and the Weather Widget
Body:
The MET Norway weather widget appears to be listing the Norway time for the forecast rather than the user's local time.  In my case this means it appears to have a several hour gap of no forecasts (though it's accurate for the current time and the next hour, just reported as the wrong time)

If the forecast times can also be time shifted according to the user's location that would be fantastic.

Example:
![sEJN6MN](https://github.com/MM2-0/Kvaesitso/assets/1363931/5466ae4a-ea0e-4ce9-8828-cd686945192c)

Title: [Feature Request] Indicate Work Profile Icons in Tag Editor
Body: When editing tags (Settings -> Search -> Tags) to add additional apps to one, there is no indication if an app is on the main or work profile.  This leads to it displaying things like two browsers with no way to tell them apart here.  Obviously you can still edit the tags in the actual app drawer where the personal/work profiles are clear, but the tag editing view should also display an icon to distinguish them.

Title: [Feature Request] Open (or redirect) phone numbers directly to Whatsapp
Body: [OpenContacts](https://gitlab.com/sultanahamer/OpenContacts) has a cool feature where when I type a phone number in the dialer, it gives me the option to redirect that number to a direct chat inside Whatsapp and this is great because it saves a lot of time. It would be great to have an "Open in Whatsapp" button in Kvaesitso when typing a phone number in the search bar.

Title: [Feature request] Transparent icons
Body: Hello. I'd love to see a feature where I can set a transparent background for dynamic icons, thanks.

Title: Home screen favourites
Body:
I wasn't sure if this would be better as an issue or discussion. If I should change it, please let me know. 

I've used kvaesitso since the very beginning and it is by far my favourite Android home app in the past 15 years. 

However, I feel a little confused about how the favourites work. I really appreciate the ability to not have them on my home screen - which I like to keep really clean. On the other hand, there have been a few new discussions here in GitHub with people asking about different aspects and I've been playing around to understand it better. 

Number 1.
When I have both ```Home screen > Clock > Favourites``` disabled and I have ```Home screen > Edit button``` disabled, and do not have a favourites widget, it looks like this:

Option to Hide Applications in the Listing
Body:
As a frequent user of the app, I've been thinking about a feature that I think would be very beneficial to many of us. I would love to propose the integration of an option to hide applications in the main listing, with the ability to easily access them via the search engine.
The main reason behind this suggestion is to provide users with greater control over their privacy. By being able to hide applications directly from the main listing, users are given the ability to customize their experience and safeguard the visibility of certain applications.

Title: [Feature Request] Plug-in framework
Body:
I think that there are a lot of features that would be great to have; but don't make sense for everyone and don't necessarily fit the vision of the project perfectly.

I am imagining some sort of plug-in system that would allow the installation of applets, roughly analogous to widgets used by other launchers; but with an interface to unlauncher that encourages a minimalist UI and consistency with the rest of Unlauncher. These extensions could be accessed via a carousel pattern by swiping left or right from the app list.

I kinda want to sketch out some ideas of what that could look like.

Properties of the plug-in "applets":
- Minimal UI descriptions
  - Applets should return a description of their desired UI that is composed primarily of text, simple layout information, simple monocolor icons, and a list of events that can be sent back to the template when various bits of text are clicked.
- Strongly event driven
  - Applets will return a text-based layout to UL on launch, and will respond to events sent to them by UL by returning updated layouts.
- Stateless
  - Applets should not maintain their own state. Instead, recreating any state needed on each event.
- Parameterized configuration
  - Applets should respond to a specific event with an object describing configuration options
  - Configuration of Applets should take place in the Unlauncher settings.
  - All  other events sent to applets by unlauncher should include a configuration object in the parameters


Example applets:
-  calendar
  - Displays calendar information in a scrollable widget by communicating with the Android Calendar Provider.  Tapping the title launches the default calendar application.
- Todo.txt
  - Displays, sorts, and filters the contents of a given todo.txt file. Allows filtering based on context and project to be configured. Tasks can be marked/unmarked as done by tapping. 
- App list
  - The home app list could be implemented as an applet. It is configured with up to Six apps, up to six labels for the apps, and three enableable quick-buttons
  - this could serve as a sort of foldering or app/group mechanism by simply adding multiple differently configured instances of the app list applet

Body:
I'm referring to [these](https://developer.android.com/develop/ui/views/launch/shortcuts).

This is a very niche case but it's the option that I'm missing to fully switch to Unlauncher. My bank account doesn't use Google Pay so they force to use their own app to pay with the phone, thing is, the app is atrociously slow, bugged and works half of the time, unless you force a direct pay through the app shortcut, long pressing then selecting "pay with the phone" sends you directly to the pay screen and works flawlessly, while going through the app worked 50% of the time.

There's also cases while using navigation apps like Google Maps which you can start the nav from "home" or "work" directly through the shortcut instead of opening the app and doing the necessary chores or directly opening a chat to your messaging app. I think there's already a long press option already developed for Unlauncher so I don't know how hard or even if it's a priority at this point of development, but I would love to be able to use Unlauncher while having access to my day to day tools, thanks.

Title: Add explanation in empty screen
Body:
The `CustomiseAppsFragment` screen is empty and boring when there are no apps. According to [this source](https://magora-systems.com/empty-screens-in-app-design/), apps should have an explanation when users encounter an empty screen. Taking this into consideration I was thinking to put something along the lines:

![scaffold_rocket](https://github.com/jkuester/unlauncher/assets/3295340/5838c118-06e5-449b-a551-54470f97f328)

With an explanation such as 

> Standing by... 
> Add Apps to your home screen by pressing "Add"

Another idea (since unlauncher's icon is a rocket going down) is to use the downward rocket with earth in the background indicating that it is ready to enter the atmosphere. A text would be something like

> Help the rocket enter the atmosphere by adding apps

These ideas are very unlauncher specific, however I think that the slimlauncher can also benefit from this (with a different image)

Title: Shows and hides keyboard when scrolling the app drawer
Body:
This PR is a UX enhancement when scrolling through the app drawer. 

If the show keyboard option is set, it will minimize the keyboard once users start scrolling down the app drawer. Vice versa, it displays the keyboard when the user is scrolling up again. 

[Show keyboard when scrolling up](https://github.com/jkuester/unlauncher/assets/3295340/b618dcc2-25de-4009-9c0a-1d5505aad7d5)

In order to be smooth, the `MainActivity`'s `android:windowSoftInputMode` had to be set to `adjustNothing`. This however  conflicts with PR #143 , since it would cover the search field if it set to the bottom of the screen. There are fixes for this as seen in [an official android guide](https://developer.android.com/develop/ui/views/layout/sw-keyboard).

Title: Race condition between filtering and refreshing apps
Body:
Long time user, I've noticed that sometimes, when quickly returning to the launcher, opening the drawer and starting to filter the apps, the app list resets to the full, unfiltered list ignoring the filter completely.

Steps to reproduce:
- (After using another app for a long time)
- Return to the launcher
- Immediately open the app drawer
- Start typing to filter the list
- After typing 3 letters, the list is filtered
- Then the list is re-populated with the original, full, app list.

After reading the code, my guess as to why this happens is that when using another app for a long time, the launcher needs to be re-created in Android, which, in turn, triggers a re-fetch of the system's App List.
However, if the user already started filtering before the results of this fetch arrive, they will override the apps in the adapter and disregard the current filter.

Solution proposal - probably around this area:
https://github.com/tanujnotes/Olauncher/blob/3411bc2028b3510faf183c81769190cefbfbaa97/app/src/main/java/app/olauncher/ui/AppDrawerFragment.kt#L177

After setting the apps list in the adapter, re-apply the filter so that the new filtered list remains in sync with the query string.

Body:
|Regarding the action:|Current behaviour|Proposed behaviour|Pros of current behaviour|Pros of proposed behaviour|
|-|-|-|-|-|
|Hold app then tap "Rename".|Text box is empty; shows current name as hint.|Text box contains current name, highlighted (selected).|• Won't forget which app is being renamed.|• Won't forget which app is being renamed. • Can make small changes to name without retyping (e.g. fix typo, add tags, etc.).
|Deleting all text in text box.|Hint of current name is displayed.|Hint of default (original) name is displayed.|• Won't forget which app being renamed.|• Won't forget which app being renamed. •Lets the user know the default it'll change to after pressing "RENAME".|

Title: Open-Meteo weather provider
Body:
### Checklist

- [X] I made sure that there are **no existing issues** - [open](https://github.com/iamrasel/lunar-launcher/issues) or [closed](https://github.com/iamrasel/lunar-launcher/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aclosed) - which I could contribute my information to.
- [X] I have already checked the [WIKI](https://github.com/iamrasel/lunar-launcher/wiki) pages and made sure that the feature isn't already exist.
- [X] I have taken the time to fill in all the required details. I understand that the feature request will be dismissed otherwise.
- [X] This issue contains only one feature request.

### Feature description

Hello, I would like to help implement the Open-Meteo API in the launcher, as it requires no API key (hence it's more user-friendly).
I'm currently the only contributor to the [open-meteo-api-kotlin](https://github.com/open-meteo/open-meteo-api-kotlin) library, and I haven't finished it, but I feel like to could benefit this project.
Some "pseudo-code":

 - Create an `Endpoint` with `GeocodingSearch.context`
 - Use the geocoding search endpoint to find one matching place
 - Get the first (and only) result coordinates (should be saved in the settings -> add ability to use coordinates only*)
 - Create an `Endpoint` with `Forecast.context`
 - Call the endpoint with `Forecast.Query(latitude, longitude, currentWeather = true, /* units */)`
 - Get the response current weather:
   - `response.currentWeather?.temperature` for the temperature
   - `response.currentWeather?.weatherCode?.message` for the printable message
   - `response.currentWeather?.weatherCode?.code` for the wmo int code (see at the end of the [docs](https://open-meteo.com/en/docs))

If you need the weather forecast, I would recommend to instead:

 - Query using the invoke operator: `openMeteo(...)`
   - `ForecastHourly.Temperature2m`
   - `ForecastHourly.Weathercode` (convertible with `WeatherCode.from(it)`)
     - Get a printable name (`message`)
     - Get a `Short` code to map icons (`code`)
   - `ForecastDaily.Sunrise` and `ForecastDaily.Sunset` (convertible with `Time(it)`)
     - To know if it's day or night (please note that I'm still working on `TimeZone` usage)
 - Cache all the information needed and update every N hours

*Note: if GPS auto-location were to be implemented, it would be easy to update the coordinates (next version will have `var` coordinates)

### Additional information

A nice feature of the API is that it provides hourly/daily values up to a week, easing data caching.
I'd like also to receive some inputs on how I could make the library more accessible to other developers (I know that the documentation and tests are completely missing in version 0.2.0-alpha, please have mercy), in order to ease the transaction to it.

1. a "pin" button in search bar (only shown if search bar is not empty). it will add input text to pin area when clicked
2. when search result shown. long press a item to add it to pin area
3. pin area will only shown if search bar is empty

Title: Feature request: Tasker (Locale?) trigger integration
Body:
Recently found and fell in love with this app concept. Really quick to use and looks so smooth; it's now my default assistant app. There are probably a lot of little things that could be added to this app, but the big one to me would be the ability to enter commands as tasker triggers!

I saw a previous post about tasker so to be clear, I actually do not have/use tasker! I use macrodroid instead, and I find it to be very powerful for overhauling my phone's functions, or creating small 'applets'.

Very enthusiastic about this environment but not very experienced/knowledgeable, so bear with me 😅 but from what I understand it's an API call, and works the same for both Tasker and Macrodroid (and more apps). It's based on [Locale](https://www.twofortyfouram.com/developer) and is essentially used by a bunch of random apps to activate features inside eachother.

I imagine this would work similarly to the already present custom commands, and when the "enter" key is hit, it would launch the API call to trigger whatever actions I had configured in macrodroid.

I really like this concept for this app in particular, since it would allow it to remain mostly permission-less and lightweight, but allow for really powerful functionality for anyone who wanted it. Anyways, very cool app whether you do or don't add this :)

Title: Project status
Body:
Hello,

Long story short, due to lack of time and motivation, no more new features or options will be implemented in Discreet Launcher.
Only maintenance releases to fix bugs and maintain compatibility with new Android versions will continue.

You can find more details below.

*If someone is willing to create a more active fork, no problem as long as it is rebranded to avoid confusion (different name and icon). The list of unimplemented features can be found with the label [won't do/fix](https://github.com/falzonv/discreet-launcher/issues?q=label%3A%22won%27t+do%2Ffix+%28see+closure+comment%29%22).*

---

In April 2022, I announced (https://github.com/falzonv/discreet-launcher/issues/210) that due to changes in my professional life, I now have very little time to work on Discreet Launcher.
This is still true, and it seems unlikely to change in the coming years.

Having less personal time and because my daily use of Discreet Launcher has always been very basic, I am also no longer enthusiastic about reducing this personal time further by adding features that I will have to maintain but will most probably never use.

From my point of view, this project is already completed and much more advanced than anything I had initially planned.

Many people (including myself) use Discreet Launcher every day so I will of course continue to fix bugs and maintain compatibility with new Android versions so that it keeps working well.
Translations can still be added and updated, and some minor improvements may also be done to existing features.

I know this announcement will deter some people from using Discreet Launcher.

- Basically, if you are happy with Discreet Launcher right now, it will not change anything for you as it will continue to be maintained across Android versions (it just won't grow any further).
- However, if you are looking for new features such as widgets, multiple home screens, changing a single app icon, or anything not currently in Discreet Launcher, you will probably need to find another launcher (there are dozens of them in F-Droid and literally hundreds in Google Play).
Or maybe someone else will have the time and motivation to create a more active fork and publish it to F-Droid and Google Play.
I have no problem with that as long as this fork is rebranded to avoid confusion with this project (different name and icon). In case someone reading this is actually considering to do it, the list of unimplemented features can be found with the label [won't do/fix](https://github.com/falzonv/discreet-launcher/issues?q=label%3A%22won%27t+do%2Ffix+%28see+closure+comment%29%22) and I would be happy to answer technical questions about the Discreet Launcher code base if needed. We could also add the link to your project in the README of Discreet Launcher.

---

To end this already quite long post, a bit of history, the full list of features added in Discreet Launcher and some statistics:

When I started creating this launcher in 2019, my goal was to do something very simple and minimalist, and this progressively evolved into Discreet Launcher v1.0.0 released in February 2021.
It only contained the favorites panel and the app drawer, not even a menu button!

From release to release, more and more features and options were added to the launcher as people started creating tickets in GitHub.
Here is a list of all the things which were added (the ones **in bold** are the ones I actually use, if all others disappeared tomorrow I would still be more than happy with Discreet Launcher):

- Related to favorites:
  - **Custom sort for favorites**
  - Button to sort favorites alphabetically
- Related to the list of apps:
  - **Automatic app list refresh on some events (app install/remove, icon pack change, ...)**
  - **Long press to access app settings**
  - Long press to see app store page
  - Long press to rename app
  - **Long press to add/remove to/from favorites**
  - Long press to add/remove to/from folder
  - Hidden apps
  - **Folders**
  - Number of apps in folder icon
  - Custom folder icon color
  - Visual feedback when touching apps
  - Discreet Launcher app icon as alternative menu button
  - Search
  - Open first search result with Enter
  - Work profiles support (limited to adding work apps to the main list)
  - Web shortcuts support
  - Number of columns according to orientation
- Appearance settings:
  - Theme (light, dark, system)
  - **Transparent status bar**
  - Dark status bar icons
  - Icon size for tablets
  - Option to hide app names
  - Option to hide folder names
  - Option to remove margins if no app names
  - Text color in favorites
  - Text color in app drawer
  - Background color in favorites
  - Background color in app drawer
  - Clock
    - Format (24h, 12h, 12h+am/pm, analog, date and time, full date and time, date, full date)
    - Color
    - Text shadow color
    - Position (combination of top/middle/bottom and left/center/right)
    - Size (small/medium/large)
  - Icon pack support
  - Fallback icon pack
  - Color filter on icons
- Operation settings:
  - Notification for quick access to favorites
  - **Forced orientation**
  - Option to always show favorites
  - Reverse interface
  - Immersive mode
  - Touch targets
  - Interactive clock
    - Click date to open default calendar app
    - Click time to open set clock app
  - Option to hide menu button
  - Option to hide app drawer
  - **Double-tap to open app**
  - Swipe leftwards to open app
  - Swipe rightwards to open app
- Miscellaneous:
  - **Export and import**
  - Button to set as default launcher
  - Action for [Key Mapper](https://f-droid.org/packages/io.github.sds100.keymapper) to open quick access notification
  - Notch support
  - Android TV compatibility (somewhat limited but basic features and navigation are ok)
  - Also reported to work properly on WSA (Windows Subsystem for Android)
  - Help
  - Changelog

And finally, here are updates to the statistics from the April 2022 announce:

- Discreet Launcher is developed in Java, supports Android 5 to 12 (working on Android 13 compatibility), and is translated into thirteen languages: English, French, Danish, German, Indonesian, Italian, Japanese, Portuguese, Norwegian, Russian, Spanish, Turkish and Ukrainian (+7 translations since April 2022).
- I have no idea on the number of users with F-Droid, but there are currently a bit more than 5400 users with Google Play (+5150 users since April 2022!).
- A single permission is requested in order to get the list of installed apps on Android 11 and beyond.
- The APK file size is still less than 2 MB.
- Although it is probably not perfect, I consider the code base to be sane, well commented and with no major bugs.
- With v7.3.1, a total of 61 versions have already been released (+11 releases since April 2022).
- For those wondering, my total income from Discreet Launcher since the beginning is 186€ (fortunately my real job pays decently).

Thank you for reading all of this and for your interest in Discreet Launcher!

Best regards.

Title: User Experience: First Launch and Accessibility Support
Body:
@falzonv
The main idea of DL is that we have a clean home screen. But it will be confusing for a while for blind users who are using DL for the first time. They end up with a blank screen where the screen reader doesn t say anything, so they mistakenly think something is wrong.
I propose the following concept: after the first launch of DL, the user receives a dialog box with the offer to enable accessibility support, as well as a brief explanation that this is required if the user uses a screen reader such as TalkBack. It also says that even if the user opts out, they can turn it on later in the settings. And, accordingly, the Ok and Cancel buttons.
I think this is the best solution, because if DL will detect if the user is using a screen reader, it can be interpreted as a privacy issue. In addition, not all blind users enable accessibility support.

Title: Translations update
Body:
Hello,

The goal of this permanent ticket is to make translators life easier: I will post here when translations need updates.
If you enable notifications for this ticket, you won't need to check yourself for changes.

Thank you again for your help in translating Discreet Launcher!

Best regards.

### Process

(It is meant to evolve based on your feedback and our collective experience.)

- **When strings need to be updated**
  - They will be posted here with links to the related commits.
  - For the update, you can do a pull request (preferred) or reply in this ticket with translations for me to copy-paste.
- **Other remarks**
  - If the ID of a string changes or if a string is removed, I will update translations myself.
  - Android Studio doesn't like missing strings in a language => new strings will be added in English everywhere, waiting for translation (a comment here will inform you).
  - Once translations are integrated, the related comments will be hidden to keep this ticket easy to read.

Title: Bump com.google.android.material:material from 1.9.0 to 1.11.0
Body:
Bumps [com.google.android.material:material](https://github.com/material-components/material-components-android) from 1.9.0 to 1.11.0.
Release notes

Sourced from com.google.android.material:material's releases.

1.11.0

Important

Elevation Overlays within default component styles have been replaced by the new Tonal Surface Color system. Instead of blending the Primary color with the Surface color based on an elevation value, components now use a specific Surface color role that can be more easily understood and customized.

Documentation for the affected components and which Surface color roles they use has been updated in https://github.com/material-components/material-components-android/commit/2114a11378fd801b32cd7431bb8a2c94de4f462c.

What's new since 1.10.0

  • New Tonal Surface Color system! Check out the design guidance and the commit with documentation updates for more details.
  • New Carousel variants! 1.11.0 introduces three new Carousel variants: center-aligned hero, uncontained, and fullscreen. Check out the Carousel documentation for more details.

Dependency Updates

None.

Note: Earlier versions of 1.11 used other versions of the androidx.activity 1.8.0 library dependency, but there is no change in the dependency from the last stable MDC library version (1.10.0).

Library Updates

  • Badging
    • Adjusted badge vertical offset for certain components to fit design requirements (78cc54b8c39da437732ff3d7c96de8f345e09348)
    • Added note in attachBadgeDrawable method docs about menu item views being re-used when there is a structural change in the menu. Since badges are attached to the views, this may change the menu item that the badge is intended for (5e6ea582801ebfe2af8457261581a2ad43c8051a)
  • BottomNavigationView
    • Set badges to be null instead of removing them from the sparse array so that removing badges before restoring badge states will not override the current state (9f2e6864d2170d633709e7aa66b9e8e178352ef0)
  • BottomSheet
    • Fix sheet corners animation (de271320e4ba046c73e35e18f687ee4637f3a6ac)
  • Carousel
    • Ensure that extra small size is not larger than the large size (17baf71972016cf4608d9c83b902bb2debee585b)
    • Fixed crash when recyclerview has a size of zero. (26c31292012868d7e9889f6ecee7559c61caa5b2)
    • When navigating with keyboard, scroll focused item to nearest focal keyline, not the first focal keyline (fb9c1c6edfbdbe4791043a128c03a7fad5fd99f0)
    • Force hero strategy to be start-aligned if there are not enough items to make it center-aligned (9a2347bda5312dd6dc3ab7818a6f71297dcf018b)
    • Add logic for multibrowse strategy to change strategy when number of items is less than the number of keylines (cbb380df61f8eb66273043b437582c8058df7088)
    • Fixed focus order when using keyboard navigation for hero carousels. (0356f24a63b5c47ee23cbf5c0fca4d817e4d6acd)
    • Cleaning up multi-browse strategy and removing compact arrangement (ed4647d5dfa42876063521546de2dbcf71914e01)

Title: Add error reporting
Body:
**Description**
Sometimes the app crashes and the developer is not able to reproduce the issue. Users can't be expected to collect and send crash reports. See #434

**Solution**
Use [ACRA](https://github.com/ACRA/acra) to collect crash information.
Allow user to send report by mail.

**Alternative solutions**
Collect and send crash reports without user intervention. This needs a backend and internet permission.

Title: Feature Request: Search across all Contacts fields
Body:
**Description**
 Contacts can hold a ton of information, not limited to name or phone number, such as address, note, website, email, Company, Job Title, etc. 

**Solution**
It would be wonderful to be able to search across all Contacts fields, like email, notes, website, address, Company, Job Title, etc. 

This would enable me to find someone I met who works for Company ABC without remembering their name or Number.

Title: Feature Request: Tag Contact Results with Data Field
Body:
**Description**
Currently, all Contacts results just show the number and a button for Message and Call. 

It is possible to hold many different numbers per contact, such as Mobile, Home, Work, Dax, etc

**Solution**
Show the field the contact entry is from, be it Home, Mobile, Work, Company, Other, etc. 

**Alternative solutions**
Potentially, use a graphical icon or emoji to signify which contact field the result is from.

**Additional context**
I am just starting to break my app-centric habits and using the search providers, and I'm now seeing that for contacts with extensive details, I can't tell which result is the one I want.

Title: Feature Request: Addition of View/Contact Card for Done Key Contact Action
Body:
**Description**
Currently, we can only choose to call, message, or send when we click enter on a contact result. 

Contacts can also hold tons of other information, such as physical address, notes, important dates, website, etc. 

**Solution**
It would be really great to have an option to launch the contact card instead of forcing a call or message activity.

Title: Feature Request: Option to Group and Sort by parent app for Activities
Body:
**Description**
I would love to see activities grouped and sorted by their parent app in the activities list, instead of how they are all currently intermixed and sorted alphabetically globally. 

**Solution**
This would mean all activities for Element would come before all activities for Fedilab. 

It would also keep the alphabetical ordering of activities beneath each parent app.

Title: Feature Request: External Border color and width
Body:
**Description**
Sometimes I find I would like to have more visual separation of TinyBit Launcher elements like search, results, and dock, while keeping them visually consistent. 

**Solution**
I would love to see the ability to set an external border, with settings for color and width, to have this visual separation. 

**Additional context**
Pop-up menus in TinyBit already have this, so it would integrate well, I think.

Title: Feature Request: Optionally Propagate App Tags to App's Shortcuts
Body:
**Description**
Tagging is very manual and has a lot of touch-time to accomplish, and as a brand-new user, I wasn't aware that Shortcuts could/should also be tagged for organization. With upwards of 300 apps installed... Yikes.

**Solution**
An option to propagate the App's tags to the App's shortcuts would be a great... shortcut to getting them organized in a sensible way, though some folks may wish to further refine a few here and there, or opt out of this entirely.

Title: Feature Request: "Tag" Shortcuts with App/App Tag
Body:
**Description**
The Shortcuts list is Alphabetical by Shortcut, and can quickly become hard to manage/read.

**Solution**
Having the "subtitle" tags beneath each Shortcut like how it is displayed for Apps with a Tag would help differentiate the Shortcuts and help the user identify with certainty which app the Shortcut belongs to.

Perhaps it could show something like this (%appname% - %tag%):  
```
#irchannel
Element - Communications 
```

Title: Feature Request: Combine Widget and Search desktop
Body:
**Description**
As a new user to TinyBit (Microsoft Launcher) who has some passing familiarity with things like KISS/Lunar/Niagara/etc, I am finding that I wish I could have the Search Bar be "sticky" as a configuration option, much like the Dock.

**Solution**
A user-settable toggle to have the Search Bar be sticky like the Dock would be a great way to have this familiar feature, especially since we can already adjust their layout/order.

**Alternative solutions**
A possible alternative would be to have the Search Bar added onto the Dock in such a mode.

**Additional context**
It would look, I imagine, much like KISS in this respect.

Title: On Enter, wait for filter to complete, then start first app
Body:
# Description #
<!-- Describe the bug. A clear and concise description of what the bug is. -->
Whenever I want to start an app, I type in 2 letters and press Enter on the keyboard. I know beforehand which app is the first (well, visually the last) in the result list and as such should be started when pressing Enter.

However, I find that if the list reorganizing is still busy (I'm not sure of the reason, perhaps because of the animation, perhaps because of the filtering process itself) when I press Enter, _another_ app starts.

As an alternative explanation, it might also be that the filter misses out on the _first_ letter I type, immediately after the keyboard shows, but I think that's less likely.

## Steps to Reproduce ##
<!-- It is important to let developer know how to replicate the problem without re-reading the description -->
To reproduce the behavior:
1. Optionally turn on Battery Saver to slow down the device a bit
1. Go to Search
1. Type in a few letters and press Enter quickly after
1. See another app started, instead of the app that should be started according to the result list

## Expected behavior ##
<!-- A clear and concise description of what you expected to happen. If you think it would help, provide a modified screenshot. -->
The first result (visually last in the list) should be started consistently.

## Device info ##
<!-- please complete the following information -->

  OS: Android 11
  TinyBit Launcher version: 7.4 (and I believe 7.3 as well)

Title: [Enhancement] Add Option for Multiple Apps Lists
Body:
### Checklist

- [X] I will make sure to have the issue written in English with an English title.
- [X] I made sure that there are **no existing issues** - [open](https://github.com/HeCodes2Much/mLauncher/issues) or [closed](https://github.com/HeCodes2Much/mLauncher/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aclosed) - which I could contribute my information to.
- [X] I have already checked the [WIKI](https://github.com/HeCodes2Much/mLauncher/wiki) pages and made sure that the feature isn't already exist.
- [X] I have taken the time to fill in all the required details. I understand that the feature request will be dismissed otherwise.
- [X] This issue contains only one feature request.

### Feature description

I like simplicity, I love how mLauncher is built!! I'm also using many apps. This creates problems for me because I can either keep the App List simple with around 6 app shortcuts visible (in which case I'll have to use the menu to access other apps which in turn decreases productivity) or I can increase the number of app shortcuts (in which case the main sceen would become too cluttered). So I kept wondering, how can you make this more powerful but keep simple at the same time? What I came up with is Multiple App Lists. For the record when I say Multiple I mean up to three.

In the Settings / Homescreen area you could insert a new setting called `Number of App Lists` with possible values: 1, 2 or 3 and under it you could insert settings called `App List 1 No of Shortcuts`, `App List 2 No of Shortcuts` and `App List 3 No of Shortcuts` with the usual values. These App Lists could be accessed by swiping left and right.

### Additional information

What do you think developer and community? Do you like this idea or not? Do you think it could be implemented or not? Would you change something (and if yes then what) about the way I think this could work?

Title: An improper locking bug due to the unreleased lock
Body:
Hi, developers, thank you for your checking. In below code, when `!parent->parallel_threads`, the lock `parent->mutex` could be unreleased in the child thread. I note the comment that `/* I don't think this is necessary anymore. */`  and thus do not whether it can happen.

https://github.com/Zygo/xscreensaver/blob/a7be2fd2981da28322be0872b11e1059d6571e6d/utils/thread_util.c#L429-L446


Best,

Title: First key typed is ignored and other input related issues.
Body:
The first character entered as an unlock password is always ignored. For example, if the first character is "A", actually the shift key activates the unlock dialog, but the "A" key is ignored and must be entered twice.

But if you press "Shift", wait a little and then press "A" it is accepted. IMHO, this have something with the window initialization process, which seems to be  very slow. 

Sometimes when you type the password the whole window flashes (seems like becomes invisible and then back visible).

In addition, the auto-repeat of the keyboard is not working at all. So, when you type the whole password and realize that the first key is ignored, you should press backspace many times in order to correct it. Other editing tricks like selecting all and then delete by the "Del" key are not working as well.

Because of the above misbehavior, after updating XScreenSaver to v6.0 I was never able to type the password correctly from the first time. The only reason why I am still not downgrading is because of the implemented display of the current keyboard layout, which was not available on the 5.x versions.

Title: Please add support for Progressive Web Apps
Body:
There are website that support a kind of  standalone mode (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Progressive_web_application). On mobile devices one can add these websites to the home screen* and when started they appear and act like regular apps, without the browser UI. As an example: https://www.bmw.com/

*) In most mobile browsers there's an entry "Add to homescreen" in the contextual menu, but it may be hidden if the launcher does not support this.

Unfortunately, Olauncher does not offer support for PWAs. I am not an Android developer, but I think that this can't be a big deal, because pretty much every launcher offers this feature...

For more information here's a link to the same request in the original repo: https://github.com/tanujnotes/Olauncher/issues/20

Title: [feature] Allow enabling/disabling features
Body: While clock31 is amazing, it's not a drop-in replacement for cLock or the lineage clock widget. Having two on the desktop is great, but now we have two clocks for example. Being able to turn on/off (must have at least one enabled of course) is useful to use clock31 for the calendar, but omit the clock.

Title: [Bug] Slow frame-by-frame slideshow instead of smooth animation and designed GIF's frame speed
Body:
**Describe the bug**
Slideshow instead of designed GIF's frame speed.
Open the GIF on a PC or in another program on the phone, and the GIF is played smoothly.
Unlike the wallpaper's slideshow. :-( 

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Set some GIF (3+ MB, with small delays between the frames) as the phone's wallpaper.
2. Watch the slow frame-by-frame slideshow.

**Expected behavior**
Smooth animation.

**Smartphone (please complete the following information):**
 - Device: Sony Z5 Compact
 - Android version: 11
 - App version v2.2.0(74)
