Title: Blue bg green font
Body:
Version: 0.1

Environment:
Galaxy S4 SGH-I337
CM11 ROM
TWRP

Description:
Installed Hall Monitor and enabled Root access function + notifications.

Within a day the S-view when closed went from being the standard black bg white font to a bright blue bg with green font (hard to read for color blind).

Only way to resolve issue was to uninstall apk and reinstall.

Title: general discussion: framework
Body:
i have add the branch [framework](https://github.com/habeIchVergessen/HallMonitor/tree/framework) (my ideas about an component framework).

objective target:
- standardized handling of views (clock, phone, camera, etc.)
- loading on demand (visibility)
- shared code base
- distributed functionality (reduce complexity of merges)
- consolidate layout and code (runtime)
- alternative layout implementations

i guess we need something like that.

Title: network connectivity indicator
Body: Show an icon in DefaultActivity for whether mobile data and/or wi-fi are connected

Title: make the DefaultActivity components rearrangeable
Body:
I'm not sure if this is a good idea, but I think it is, because it can simplify the preferences a lot while adding more configurability. But it can wait until after the next release, I think. I just wanted to write it down.

Here is my n-point plan to reorganize the UI preferences:
1. kill all of the "show/hide X" checkboxes
2. kill the current DA layout hierarchy and put everything in a flat AbsoluteLayout (or similar)
3. make a new configuration mode which shows the same content as the DA, as well as a "palette" below with all of the available widgets (clock, torch button, camera button, battery indicator, custom widget, ...)
   - (the palette will obviously change if some of the buttons are moved into hV's radial menu)
   - You can drag things back and forth between the palette and the DA, and also freely rearrange them within the DA. Things left in the palette will of course not be displayed at all when the DA comes up.
   - Maybe if you tap on one of the things, a toast or dialog pops up explaining what it is?
4. ???
5. ~~Profit!~~ Open source!

Title: be more careful about leaking Activity references
Body: We might be fine... but some classes such as Functions and CameraHelper do have references to the DefaultActivity. These may or may not need to be refactored to avoid leaks. Source: idle conversations on IRC and http://www.curious-creature.org/2008/12/18/avoid-memory-leaks-on-android/

Title: S4 Mini issues
Body:
This could serve as a thread for issues that are specific for the S4 Mini for which an S-View Cover exists, as well. AFAIK, there are at least three different versions of this device (LTE/3G/Dual SIM). I have the LTE version which is officially known as the _GT-I9195_ and on Cyanogenmod as [_serranoltexx_](http://wiki.cyanogenmod.org/w/Serranoltexx_Info). I started with CM10.2, but now I am on the CM11 nightlies. It turns out that HallMonitor is really useful on the Mini, too. However, the on and off trigger seems to be firing less reliable than on the big S4. The apk in the master branch is mostly unfunctional, but @Wallace0 has refined the detection so that the hit rate is much better now. Currently I use the [apk in the merge branch](https://github.com/durka/HallMonitor/tree/merge/bin). To come up with some real numbers, I opened and closed the cover 20 times, with these results:
openclose
success14 (70%)7 (35%)
fail6 (30%)13 (65%)
The last change that introduced a further event refire after 500ms and that Wallace0 [mentioned here](https://github.com/durka/HallMonitor/issues/10#issuecomment-29530545) did not affect the situation noticeably. The hit rate is strongly dependent on the opening/closing speed: If i move the cover really calmly, everything is fine. Fast motion is especially difficult on close.

Title: General Discussion
Body:
Thought we could do with a place for general comments. Can also use this to agree division of labour etc.

So, it looks like you are getting busy with the notifications piece and the telephone answering bit.

To my mind that leaves the media player controls bit, and the alarm clock snooze. I think I'll get cracking with the media player bit first. I'll create a new branch for that off from Master - hopefully we won't have too much fun dealing with merge conflicts when we both merge back in!

Title: Air Gestures
Body:
Well, I still have no idea how Air Gestures really work. But, check this out:

```
shell@android:/ # cat /sys/devices/virtual/input/input7/name
gesture_sensor
```

It exists! Nothing is happening on `/dev/input/event7`, whereas other sensors such as `/dev/input/event9` (the proximity sensor) are definitely producing output. So maybe the gesture sensor is not turned on?

Title: Add option to hide values
Body:
Staring at a "bad" number might make one feel like they're failing at their goals and increase use.

There should be a way to "hide today's use" to prevent this guilt trigger while still keeping track of one's health

Title: Add option to remove outliers from statistics calculations
Body:
As a Daily user, I want to be able to exclude data points that are way too different from my average data point.

We can start with taking the 95 percentile and that will already exclude a lot of outliers.

- Went to a party and consumed more than usual
- Consumed using a different method which changes the price or quantity


- [ ] Checkbox to remember my option (which must be persistent)
- [ ] Take 95 percentile to calculate data on stats

Title: Widgets don't update after I leave the screen
Improving the UI Design of the app.
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Hi there, i feel this type of apps should have a strong influence over its users in order to make changes, and an intuitive & engaging UI can help this app to achieve it. I see there is a room of scope in improving the Design of the app.

Is this project open to accept UI design improvements so that i can contribute on them and code them as well

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I would like to improve the design of the app and code it as well, i am very good in UI Design and i've contributed to open source projects in design improvements before.


**Additional context**
Are the maintainers i mean this project is open to accept UI enhancements? please let me know, i can make some improvement mockups as well and share with you.

Title: 60 and 90 days line color in Stats page are too faint and confusable with the background color
Refactor SettingsView code
Body:
SettingsView is currently 658 lines long, and growing.
Most of the code is repetitive, and can probably be abstracted away.

This should be a priority before adding new configuration options.

Title: Add Detekt to pre-commit hook
Body: Waiting for the checks to run is a slow process. We should have detekt as part of the precommit hooks of this repository

Title: Add option to disable an enabled pause
Body:
"pausing a pause".

To prevent self guilt and worsening of a crisis, one should be able to say "hey Petals, I'm on a crisis right now, suspend the pause for a week"

Title: Add widget for use control to BangleJs
Add "buying date" and "buying amount+monetary value" option
Body:
I would want a way to add a secondary option for the "usage" button that adds a prompt of:

- amount bought,
- date,
- and currency value. 

**Problem solved:**
This way, I can track my consumption better and reduce it properly (ex. I smoke more than usual on the day of buying the stuff, so I can take it into better account if I can know when I bought the stuff. 

**Additional sub-feature** 
I would also like to know when I buy it, how much I buy, and for what price etc for average monthly/daily/yearly buying is possible, using the already existing graphs and data info 'screen'.) 


**Describe alternatives you've considered** none

**Additional context**
Really appreciate the work on the app, it really helped me reduce and track my usage, thank you :)

Title: Add K-Safe suggestion on pause screen
Body: Sometimes self-control can be hard. KSafes are good alternatives to take control out of oneself. That tip can be added to the pause feature

Title: Add option to purchase ad free option
Add rewarded ads options to "disable ads for a week"
Create a guide/gif/pdf on how to use the app displaying the main features and their purposes
Body:
In order to present Petals as a healthy choice and to indeed be a healthy choice, we need to show it to the world. One way of doing that is **showing screenshots**, which is static and not enough to visualize everything. Another way is to show to the person **face-to-face**, but that's not scalable and won't reach much people.

We need something designed to show how the app works, why the features are there and as an invite to get to know it better.
We should be able to show this 'brochure' to

- Potential users
- Researchers that want to understand the project
- Media/press

Title: Add code coverage metrics
Body:
Using kotlinx-kover https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx-kover we should try to estimate our current coverage by unit tests.

For example. I know that plenty of the database is covered in unit tests, but UI tests are lacking. How lacking? We don't know.

- [x] Add Kover to the project
- [ ] Create a github action to run it on every push

Title: Reports: Added grouping by task and hint
Body:
Hello TWT Community,

At work, we recently got new time tracking rules, this way I found your app. I am working tickets within a ticketing system, and have different activities to do for each ticket. I use the tasks for each of these activities, and put the ticket numbers in the hint field (some tickets may be quick to complete, so always adding/removing tasks for all of them was not really an option for me). This is the reason, I thought it would be handy, to have an option to export reports with time sums not only grouped by task alone, but by the combination of the task and the hint of an event. This is my attempt to implement this.

There is a new type `TaskAndHint`, which is used to hold the task as well as the hint of an event as a Map Key. This then allows using the new time calculation methods, which are pretty similar to the ones of the "times by task" options, respectively. On top of that, there are new radio buttons for these options, and some additions around the CSV generator, to export this new format. It still works with empty hints, which will be handled as empty strings in this case - so everything of the same task without a hint will also be grouped together. I added all four options: the general one, daily, weekly and monthly, for it to be similar to the existing  "times per task *" grouping options (I am interested in the daily one mostly). I at least tested it with some test inputs within the android studio emulator, but not on a real device yet.

I tried to keep in-sync with the existing code style, but also I do not have any experience with app-development at all. Let me know what you think of the idea and implementation, or if there is anything I can do better.

Thanks for your work on the app and keeping FOSS apps alive :smile: 


Merry Christmas & happy holidays :santa:

Having the ability to change this could have some impact on battery usage.

Update: maybe also an option to have a period when gps is checked less frequently. E.g. I normally start at 7 or 8 and leave around 4. So make the app check for gps at a lower rate between 9 and 3. 

And also it would be great if one could choose periods where it does not scan at all, e.g. at night

Title: [Feature] Add a shift system
Body:
If one wants to track worktime the reason often is to have an easy way of seeing days that differ from usual work shift.

I propose a way to define shift plans inside of trackworktime. Once that is in place trackworktime could easily recognize if the user worked different to usual shifts and calculate the offset.
Another possible future feature could be to automatically round the tracked worktime to match the shift for that day (with a configurable margin)

Such a system should have a few key elements:
- define work schedules for each day of the work-week
- a system to rotate shiftplans (maybe even allow plans to be associated with even/uneven week numbers)
- maybe more?

Title: Make hints easier to discover
Body:
It would be very handy if hints were visible in the week and day views.

Right now it's impossible to see if there are hints for a specific day. Neither the weekview nor the detailed view of a single day have any indicator for present hints. If one wants to use hints for e.g. documenting reasons for starting work late/leaving early this is not very useful in the current state.

So I propose to add a column for weekview to let the user know that there are hints set for that day.
Also add a column in details view for the day that also shows an indicator. (Or even the content, if space allows it)

there could be a reserved space at the bottom half of the screen. if an entry is selected, show details of the entry, including the hint.
Optionally the indicator could show the actual content of the hint, if it's short enough. (e.g. "-0:30" if one worked 30 minutes less)

If the IN and OUT columns in weekview could be squeezed a bit (they are far wider than needed right now) this would easily make room for another column that shows an indicator for hints.

Title: Android Wear support
Body:
There is a possibility to synchronize a notification with a smart watch (see [this commit](https://github.com/mathisdt/trackworktime/commit/4891b84a061b3bb02c8e6d0bf005a05a0d4fb37f)). But it would be nicer to have a watch face complication which both shows the current status (clocked in or out) and can be used to change the status.

The complication is nearly done (see [this branch](https://github.com/mathisdt/trackworktime/tree/wear-os-watchface-complication)), but the communication with the phone app doesn't work yet. Probably the following architecture can help:

1. The main app stays as it is in the branch (two additional broadcasts are sent when clock-in/clock-out status changes), especially it doesn't depend on any proprietary Google messaging library so there's no problem with F-Droid.
2. A new companion app for the phone is created. This receives the broadcast intents from the main app and sends the data on via [Data Layer](https://developer.android.com/training/wearables/data/data-layer). It also can receive clock-in/clock-out requests via Data Layer and forward them as intents to the main app. This app has to include the proprietary Google libraries.
3. The Android Wear complication in the branch has to include the Data Layer libraries and then receives the messages sent by the companion app (which sets the displayed status). It also is able to send a clock-in/clock-out request via Data Layer when the complication is tapped.

Title: multi-employer configuration and time-tracking
Body:
TWT should support an arbitrary number of employers to be configured.

- Each employer can have different settings (so the options have to be employer-bound and can't stay standard Android properties).
- Each employer can have different tasks.
- The employer should be selectable on top of the main screen. Everything below should change accordingly. The app should remember the last selected employer. If only one (the standard) employer is configured, the employer selection should be hidden.
- The events and target times the user enters count for the currently selected employer.
- The notification and the widget should both display the time/data for the employer which was last selected in the app.

This probably will be a big change, especially in the data model.

Title: Selected task not current
Body:
When you change task, and tap "start new period", task changes to previously used task.

I'm not sure if this is the intended behavior, but selected task seems to be one that was previously selected.

Reproduce:
1. Clock-in with task A
2. Change task to task B
3. Start new period
4. Task changes back to A (I would expect it should remain at task B)
5. Start new period
6. Task changes to task B (correct)

Title: Task semantics and home screen widget
Body:
Tasks is a nice feature, it makes for some complications though, so it might merit documentation, thinking out loud:

- I cannot do several things at once :) 
- Clock-In starts work, I can specify a task
- next Clock-In switches task, if specified
- Clock-Out ends work

The app interface is up to the task (pun intended), "Start new period" will use the currently selected task for the Clock-In event. Perhaps the current active task should be highlighted in some way too, e.g. having two stars next to it in the list?

Not so from the home screen widget. I find that mostly useful to quickly clock out for a break and clock in when taking up work again. Is it more likely, that I continue with the last task, or that I start the default/empty task? What if the date changed in-between?

How not to overly complicate the UI? Perhaps, make switching tasks or hints require pulling up the app and make the home screen widget strictly for breaks and having a day (stop tracking); so instead of the "New" button, put in its place a toggle that reads "Break" and "Continue" (like "pause" on a tape recorder?), where "continue" always would continue the task last started, so should be clear semantics and could even be abused for night breaks, while "Start/Stop" can remain not specified regarding tasks.

What do you think of it? That should keep the UI lean, but maybe requires some thinking from user side. (Less so, if the task name was written on the widget header though.) And yes, some finicky programming too ;)

So much for loud thinking!

Title: Home screen widget: Show task name
Body:
Cool app :)

First idea: Have the home screen widget show the name of the task, preferably in place of "worked", perhaps shortened to the first six or so characters.

Second idea: Make a clock-in event from the home screen widget continue the task, that was used last.

Thank You

Title: Remember selected task
Body:
I think it would be nice to persist currently selected task. When you use a certain task, I think there's a higher chance that you will use it again in near future.

For example, if you work on a different project for a week, you would probably want to use same task in that period.

v1.0.7

Title: [Feature request] Rounding of time entries
Body:
I track my working time only with a 15 minute precision, i.e. only with the granularity of a quarter hour. Others might use 5 minutes as the smallest chunk of work time. Currently doing so is quite tedious since when I do not hit start or stop exactly at a multiple of 15 minutes, I have to correct the entry manually afterwards to make it fit that granularity.

Therefore it would be great when there was a configuration option to automatically round the time entries to the next full 15 minutes (or 5 minutes or whatever someone else uses as their minimal time chunk).

Title: Support Direct Boot (alarm before first unlock)
Body:
The alarm clock does not do anything if the device rebooted and it not yet unlocked.

I am not sure if the Camera could work here, so this alarm clock could be fundamentally incompatible.

But if you device reboots, for example on GrapheneOS where auto-reboot is used as a security measurement to clear the encryption keys from RAM or with automatic system updates with reboot, the clock would not go off.

The AOSP clock as well as [new-clock](https://github.com/qw123wh/new-clock) support direct boot, so they display the alarm on top of even the SIM card lock screen after a reboot.

Title: Support for Multiple, distinct Alarms - Scheduling
Body:
QR Alarm should allow for support of multiple distinct alarms.  For example, if I wanted an alarm to be scheduled daily at 8 AM, and nightly at 10 PM, I shouldn't have to reset and change my alarms twice, daily.  

It would be nice to merely have these set as a given schedule, and even nicer if it had advanced scheduling techniques allowing for it to repeat on specific weekdays, etc.

Title: Feature Request: Reminder to set alarm for next day
Body:
Please add a setting which will give the user a notification reminder to set the alarm for the next day. The setting will decide weather this will be enabled and what time in the day the notification will go off.

This way the user will never forget to set their alarm.

Title: individual settings of all colors in the widget
Body: it's a little unusual and uncomfortable for me to see the colors that you have by default. Please make it possible to change all colors (weekend colors, weekday colors, current day color, line color and text color)

Title: Feature request: weekly view on widget
Body: Great app, thanks! It would be really cool if you could also have a view on the widget that shows a weekly view with abbreviated events.

Title: Widget pagination feature request
Body: I would love to have paging buttons on the widget to be able to see future and past months.

Title: Considering elevation for Salah times
Body:
Assalamo alaykom,
Jazakum Allahu khayran for your application.

There is one problem that most Prayer times app does not take into account which is Altitude/elevation above sea level of the location point.
This is really a big problem and can make difference of minutes of prayer time. The app assumes everything is at sea level which is not the case usually.
Consider those 2 locations:

36.34444/30.12013/~35m
36.33877/30.05053/~1650m

Distance between them is around 6300m while elevation difference is over 1600m which diffidently should have much effect on prayer time.

You can watch this video in Arabic language

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yIaVmmRXLcI

I think you should seek help from https://www.astronomycenter.net/ on how to take elevations into account

Title: Earliest/latest sunrise/sunset
Body:
It would be nice to have the dates for earliest and latest sunset and sunrise in the left-right swiping area on the bottom (with Samhain, winter solstice...).

Due to Earth's slightly elliptical orbit, these each fall several weeks apart in middle latitudes, not on the solstices as most people expect.

Body:

would be nice to have the next Apogee/Perigee displayed where the next solstice etc is displayed.. perhaps optionally if it would be too much information for some users.

Likewise, the equinox and solstice isn't everything: for example this year the earliest sunset in the northern hemisphere is December 12th, the latest sunrise usually December 30-31. Would be nice to see that as well.

Title: Days until next moon phase [Feature request]
Body:
Dear Developers,

I hope you're well. I appreciate the lunar phase widget in the app; it's a valuable addition to my daily routine. I suggest improving it for users who follow non-Gregorian calendars, like me.

Currently, the widget displays the lunar phase and a Gregorian date. I propose showing a countdown to the next lunar phase instead. For instance, "Full Moon - 5 days remaining" or just "in 5 days" rather than "Full Moon - October 28." as an option.

This change would benefit a broader audience and enhance the user experience. Thank you for considering this enhancement.

Best regards,
Tommy Fenyx.

Title: Alarm compatibility with Battery Saver mode
Body:
This is a probably not a bug, more a quick support request because I'm getting nowhere.

On Android 12, with Battery Saver mode enabled (normal, not Ultra), SunTimes alarms do not go off. In Settngs>Apps>SunTimes>Battery, I have of course selected "Unrestricted".

Is there a remedy?

I understand it is something to do with the Android system timer. For instance, sometimes, when I turn on the screen, the most recent missed alarm will go off!
  
NB: anecdotally, apps seem to resist Android killing them if they have an active notification. On a previous Android version I remember seeing a persistent "alarm bell" icon when a SunTimes alarm was active. Not on Android 12, even though I have allowed all SunTimes notifications. Is that to be expected?

Thanks so much for advice.

Title: Sun Position Widget superfluous space above and below Basic Map
Body:
**Describe the bug**

Using the Basic Map layout of the Sun Position Widget there shows superfluous space above and below the map. While the widget takes up 3 grid height, the map that is displayed shows as approximately 2 grid height. Scaling the widget down to 2 grid height, the ratio of superfluous space remains, and the map is resized accordingly.

This issue presents itself on one of my two Android devices.

**To Reproduce**

Not sure if this could necessarily be reproduced, as it doesn't happen on my second Android device.

**Expected behavior**

I would expect the map to fill the entire space used by the widget. As it does on my other Android device.

**Screenshots**

Google Pixel 3a:
![Pixel 3 height](https://github.com/forrestguice/SuntimesWidget/assets/63465529/7ffb6267-7c0c-4f74-a41f-2dc14822706d) ![Pixel 2 height](https://github.com/forrestguice/SuntimesWidget/assets/63465529/ad34907d-b485-4866-8f81-8bb53c7468f9)

Samsung A40:
![Samsung 3 height](https://github.com/forrestguice/SuntimesWidget/assets/63465529/450b1e41-c908-4736-8219-78dc209b259c) ![Samsung 2 height](https://github.com/forrestguice/SuntimesWidget/assets/63465529/b53f22b3-b479-4db3-8c1d-4fe4f76abe29)

**Version Info:**

Google Pixel 3a
 - App Version: 0.15.4
 - Android Version: 13  (custom ROM: CalyxOS version 4.10.1)

Samsung A40
 - App Version: 0.15.4
 - Android Version: 11

**Additional context**

The "Swap layouts to fill available space" of the Sun Position Widget settings is unchecked.

The Google Pixel 3a uses an app grid that has a width of 5, the Samsung A40 uses an app grid that has a width of 4.

---

Thanks so much for this amazing app! It's been so nice tinkering with it and through that learning more about the daylight differences throughout the year and how the human time relates to it, etc.

Title: Read time aloud [Feature request]
Body:
Greetings,

This request pertains to the Suntimes alarms. I would like to suggest the following enhancements for the alarms:

1. Implement a feature where alarms read the time aloud once and repeat the announcement after every snooze interval.
2. It would be beneficial to have the option to choose between read time aload only, and with ringtone while respecting audio focus.
3. Of course, it would be an app Philosophy to incorporate language customization for the Text-to-Speech (TTS) engine.

Furthermore, regarding the snooze functionality, it would be environmentally friendly if the screen could be allowed to sleep during the snooze period.

Thank you for developing this well-designed application.

version 0.15.4 and android 12
Best regards,

Title: Reproducible builds
Body:
[Reproducible builds](https://f-droid.org/en/docs/Reproducible_Builds) means that the build process always produces a byte-per-byte identical APK. This is a security measure against a potential compromise at F-droid / Google.

The downside is that occasional bugs in the toolchain have historically made builds non-reproducible, so this is a long-term commitment for the maintainer. That said, F-droid is pushing for repro builds for new apps, whenever possible, so I think the toolchain should be fairly stable nowadays.

Related: #17

Title: Formatting of exported CSV
Body:
Currently, the exported file is a CSV with the name of the counter in rows and each instance in a new column. I think it would be better to have the counters in columns and a new row for each new instance. In other words. to "transpose" the spreadsheet. This is easier to get an overview of since we otherwise get a very long list of columns after using the app for a while. It is also the standard for statistics, at least as I've understood it from other apps and academic datasets.

Then there is a second issue of instances on the same column having nothing to do with each other. Basically, each counter is a list and they just happen to be next to each other in a spreadsheet. I'm not sure if this should be changed, but I can imagine for example having names of counters in columns and dates in rows. Something like this:

|            | Counter 1 | Counter 2 |
|------------|-----------|-----------|
| 2021-04-23 | 2         | 5         |
| 2021-04-24 | 0         | 4         |

The problem with this is that we lose time precision for when something occurred. My actual preferred method might be to have the variables "counter name" and "time" in columns and a single occurrence on each row, like this:

| Counter name | Time                |
|--------------|---------------------|
| Counter 1    | 2021-04-23T08:33:24 |
| Counter 2    | 2021-04-23T11:16:54 |
| Counter 2    | 2021-04-23T19:01:12 |
| Counter 1    | 2021-04-24T09:15:32 |
| Counter 2    | 2021-04-24T10:45:44 |

Using UNIX timestamps (or whatever they're called) like present, would of course also be fine.

Title: FEATURE REQUEST: swipe down to decrease session time
Body:
I love the swiping up to increase time feature. I think it would be nice to have the option to swipe down to decrease session time too. 

Atm when I need a short timer I just swipe right to get a short "break" started. This causes all stats to be messed up, but I still prefer it because I find it faster and more intuitive than to change the pomodoro config just for a short additional task.

I would add an "X" or "SKIP" button next to the label or somehting to skip the current session, this would replace the swipe down gesture. Given the fact that that gesture has been in the app for so long, I guess there should be an option in the settings to preserve the previous behavior for users who prefer it.

If you consider this feature, it would also be interesting to add an alternative long swipe could add/remove 5 minutes instead of 1. Or just let the user configure those amounts in the settings. Overall this could make swiping totally flexible to adjust the session time easily.

An alternative that I see, and that is used a lot by other pomodoro apps, is adding some sort of confimation box before switching to a break. This usually lets you extend the session for +5 minutes. But I feel this breaks a bit the minimalism and may seem more disruptive. I would prefer manually swipe right to skip the break and a few times down to reduce the timer, more engaging.

Anyway sorry for the long info drop, just dropping the ideas.
Thanks a lot for the app, its a treat!

Title: [Feature Request] Adding logs in case the app crashes
Body:
As you might be aware many chinese firmwares quit the apps in background no matter you removed battery restrictions.
I'm on MIUI and same thing happens. By luck sometimes I can complete a session of 2 hours and sometimes the app is force stopped after 80-100 minutes.

The problem here is that you actually don't know how much time you spent on the session if you set a session for like 150 minutes, and you actually don't know the average of the session completed.

So please add a log option(maybe hidden somewhere or in the settings) which would log requests and the session duration along with time (maybe every 2-3 minutes) so that if the app gets terminated, users can calculate the time on their own and add it manually.

For example I started a session at 2:00pm and took few breaks in between, so I actually don't know the time spent without the break(the time I paused the session).
So if the app crashes maybe around 3:08pm and it would have generated a log somewhere about 3:04 or 3:05 or 3:06. And then I'll calculate the remaining time myself and add the session manually.

It will also help us to see how much time we're spending on breaks rather than focusing on our work.
Will be pleased to hear from you soon.

Thank you.

Title: [Feature Request] Separate sound for pre-notification
Body:
First of all, thank you for the app. I've been using it for over a year, and it's been very useful for fighting procrastination. It went through two NaNoWriMo events with me. :)

There are a couple of things that would be nice to have, though. I don't know how much effort it would be to implement, so feel free to disregard me if it's too much.

1. There's a setting that turns on pre-notification at the last minute of work session. It uses the finished work notification sound. Would it be possible to have a separate sound for it? Now you can only set a Start sound and an End sound.

2. Would it be possible to have a pre-notification for a break as well? It could use the same sound as work session pre-notification (so, three sounds overall: work end, break end and pre-notification).

Thank you in advance if you find it possible to implement this some time. And even if you don't, thank you anyway. :)

Title: [Feature Request] Adding a Flow-State Mode in App
Body:
**The Flow-State Mode would allow to:**
1. Take breaks not when the timer runs out, but when we really need a break.
- Oftentimes, the first Pomodoro session can be even 40 minutes long without taking breaks and you still do not feel like taking a break.
- On the other hand, after a few Pomodoro sessions you might even be tired after 14 minutes of work
2. Not distracting yourself from being in the 'zone state' 
- Zone State - is referring to the moment when everything other than what you're doing ceases to exist
- Being interrupted from this state might be frustrating, but a mindful user might decide for themselves when to stop the timer

**The workflow in this would look like that:**
1. the timer would simply count time, 
2. After the user clicked on the timer BREAK would start up (this break could be calculated by TOTAL_MINUTES/4) or divided by the number 5.
3. When the BREAK ends, the WORK timer starts again. 

The toggle settings to enable this Flow-State Mode might be on the general settings tab so that it doesn't interfere with the minimalistic interface

This option is provided by other competitive apps like ~Forest App~ <- I'm trying to not sponsor/advertise anything!!! :pray:
I personally think this option would be even better in Goodtime App!!!

I don't have great mobile development experience, but I would really want to help out!

Obviously the estimated time must be updated from time to time based on breaks.

Title: Request for changes to Pie Graph on Work Distribution
Body:
It's been bugging me lately. I sometimes log certain things for a short amount of time compared to the time in the month. For example, doing a task for only 30 minutes every day for the month compared to like 8 or 9 hours of studying different subjects (all labeled differently). This makes the small task show up as 0.something percentage. (very small percent of the whole month's data). I log a couple of small tasks like this in-app.
I've attached an image below. My suggestion is that tasks with very small percentages be categorized as "Other" altogether. Maybe tasks with less than 1 or 2% or perhaps that percent can be based on user choice.   

![Screenshot_20220315-082458_Productivity](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/30620767/158312671-0fe4dc68-05ef-4826-89cb-57f48ac39831.jpg)
 
As you can see, the small tasks just look crumpled and unreadable. So why not collapse them into an "other category". Another graph could be added below this graph to show the distribution of the other tasks alone in hours perhaps.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/30620767/158312912-0abd0668-2e1c-43f4-8d59-56df40c293c0.png)

Take this for reference. The 1% gases have been labeled altogether as Other. I am aware some would consider this loss of data but keeping it readable would be more suited for minimalism than otherwise. The categorization could also be restricted to only happen if, for example, more than 5 different tasks all have small percentages.

Adding something like this would allow us to use the app to log more and more tasks efficiently.

Title: Request for More Ways to Display Data (ideas provided)
Body:
I've got 2 ideas on 2 different graph varieties.

### Idea 1:
The first one is a monthly Heat Map divided on a week-by-week basis. This one could show the relative productivity on each day in the past month. This could be a much better way to display the productivity data than the current method for productive days for example.

![Screenshot_20220315-081654_ActionDash](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/30620767/158311885-9f71f975-3445-4753-a016-e563f698d4bf.jpg)

### Idea 2:
This Next one shows the number of times different session lengths were used. Could be useful to track if we tend to do longer sessions or shorter ones in the long run. 

![Screenshot_20220315-081756_ActionDash](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/30620767/158311888-6238fb81-7ce1-4b98-8c36-d2678a74a5e9.jpg)

I'll edit this post as I gather a couple more ideas. 
 

### Idea 3:
An Area Graph showing improvements over previous periods of time. Could Replace the current history graph or maybe add on it.

Title: Data loss while using the app (the fragment disappears after double screen rotation)
Body: When I perform these sequences of events, the fragment disappears after double screen rotation: 1. Open the app 2. Tap in Current label (element at the bottom right of the screen) 3. A screen similar to this will appear: Before rotation: 4. Force the pause from the activity and then return to the activity (double screen rotation). Note that the fragment disappears. The expected behavior is the return to the state of the previous image. However, the fragment disappears. After rotation: This bug also occurs in the element About\Privacy. Note: - The pause from activity(1) can be performed in several ways. We suggest using a double screen rotation (starts in portrait, goes to landscape, and back to portrait) with this function previously enabled in the smartphone. - Double screen rotation: It is executed twice to reach a state that should be exactly the same as the screen before if no data loss occurred. - A data loss problem occurs when data is accidentally deleted or state variables are accidentally assigned with default or initial values. Device: - Device: Moto G30 with OS: Android 11 - Version: 2.5.4 - Device language: English 1. https://developer.android.com/guide/components/activities/activity-lifecycle

Title: .ics import doesn't import/use UID field to UID2445
Body:
When importing an .ics, the `UID` field should be imported as [UID_2445](http://developer.android.com/reference/android/android/provider/CalendarContract.EventsColumns.html#UID_2445) so that following updates for this event can be related to the original event.

Original use case + steps to reproduce:

* Invitation for a meeting (`UID:abcd`) is received as .ics over email
* Invitation is imported with Etar. UID is not processed, UID_2445 not set. (If the calendar is synced with DAVx5, DAVx5 creates a random UID and uploads it to the server. But the problem is also there without DAVx5 sync.)
* An update for the invitation (meeting has been moved to another time or location) is received as .ics over email
* Update is imported with Etar. UID is not processed again, so a new event is created (instead of updating the existing event).

Expected result:

After importing the original event + the updated event, there should be only the updated event with the correct UID2445.

Actual result:

After importing the original event + the updated event, there are both the original and updated event in the calendar, both without UID2445.

Title: Add UI to edit EXDATE fields
Body:
A recurring event (one with `RRULE`) can have a list of dates that it doesn't occur on. These dates are stored in `EXDATE` [fields](https://icalendar.org/iCalendar-RFC-5545/3-8-5-1-exception-date-times.html). One field in an ICS file (if that is relevant for Etar) can hold one or more comma separated dates (or datetimes, they need to match the type of DTSTART value).

Please add a UI to manipulate `EXDATE` fields. Make it possible to add any (within reason of course) number of `EXDATE` entries each with any number of dates. If possible, store short notes in `X-REASON` parameter e.g.

```
SUMMARY:The Wednesday Meeting
DTSTART:20240403T085500
DTEND:20240403T100000
RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;UNTIL=20240829T08550
EXDATE;X-REASON="I am on holiday":20240501T085500,20240508T085500
EXDATE;X-REASON="Canceled":20240424T0805500
```

Title: Suggestion: Less surprising event creation for empty days in Month view
Body:
For context: In my calendar, most days have no entries and days that aren't usually only have one or two entries (4 entries on a single day is very, very rare for me). As a result I pretty much exclusively use Month view. When not using Month view, I'd like to use Agenda view.

When in month view, if I tap on a day it goes to the Agenda view for that day (or testing now, I see that if I last used Day view then it goes to Day view instead which might actually be helpful for me, but let's set that aside for now). If the selected day doesn't have any events then Agenda view doesn't really have anything to show, so it "goes to" a different day meaning that if I follow up by tapping the "+" to add an event, it defaults to another day, seemingly whatever the nearest day was that had an event. In hindsight, as a developer I am unsurprised that the implementation ends up working this way. However as a user I find this very strange and confusing (and I cannot imaging that this is a specifically-intended behaviour).

A quick search through other issues reveals that I am not the only one. Issues #1147, #1148 both seem to be for this exact issue, and possibly #1023, #940 and #77.

I know you can long-press on a day to add an event but:
1. That's not intuitive and there's no way of knowing that this is an option. I didn't know you could do this until I came looking for related issues here on GitHub!
2. It doesn't give you the full "New event" form, you can only set a title and you always get an all-day event, which means that very often I'd need to separately go back to edit the event after creation.
3. In much the same way as extra clicks, long-tap is more work/effort
4. It's inconsistent with other views. On "Day" and "Week" view, if I tap an empty slot it turns blue and shows a "New event" button, which I can tap to get to the usual "Create event" screen. This means I can quickly double-tap to create an event.

So my suggestion has two options/forms:
1. Make month view consistent with the Day and Week views. Tapping on a day that has events in it goes to Agenda/Day view (depending on what you opened last, just like it does right now), but tapping on a day that does *not* have events turns the day into a "New event" button which can be tapped again to open the full "Create event" form. This makes the behaviour consistent across views (reducing surprise for users), and makes creating events from Month view significantly less tedious by allowing the form to be pre-filled with the correct date.
2. Alternatively (or possibly as an extension of the above): Make the behaviour consistent across Day, Week and Month view, but remove the 2nd tap to create an event. This would reduce the number of clicks required to create an event with the correct initial date & time from 2 to just 1 and honestly I'm not sure what else I could possibly want when tapping on an empty slot in my calendar if not to create an event. The only justification that I can think of for requiring the extra tap is to avoid navigating away from the current view if you accidentally tap somewhere once. I would expect that to be a pretty rare occurrence though, and the solution is just to hit the back button (or just adjust the datetime if you tapped on the wrong slot), making it 2 taps for the accidental flow and 1 tap for the "correct"/no-mistakes flow (instead of the other way around, which is what it currently is).

Regardless, thank you for all your work on Etar! I've been using it for years now and on the whole I find it excellent for my needs :)

Title: Widget has confusing dates
Body:
The way that dates are presented in the widget is very confusing.  Right now, the header of the widget says on the first line "Thu" and on the second line "Nov 30".  This is in 2023 and the dates are correct.  At the top of the scrolling part, it says "Tomorrow, Thu, Nov 30".  Tomorrow is Dec 1, so I'm not sure what it says this.  There are no events today, which is Nov 30.  Underneath this first line of the scrolling part, it shows an event that is on Friday, Dec 1.  Underneath the event for Friday, Dec 1, it says Sat, Dec 2.  After that, it shows events on each day with the date under the last event for that day.  Yet, there is no "Fri, Dec 1" label under the event that is for that day.  

It would be most intuitive to print each date, that has events, with the events under the date.  The way it works now is a nonsense top label of the scrolling part and an incorrect date label for the first event followed by the date label under subsequent events.  

What I think is happening is that the "Tomorrow, Thu, Nov 30" label at the top of the scrolling part is coded incorrectly.  The "Tomorrow" is apparently referring to the event underneath it, though this is the opposite to the rest of the date labels where the date is under the events, not over.  The "Thu, Nov 30" refers to today, but there is nothing over it because there are no events today.  Whatever it is, it is very confusing.  I would really like to use the widget, but I get confused at which date goes with which event, so I have to open the whole calendar to check.  So, I just turned off the widget even though I'd like to use it.

Title: Notification not updated on last build - Potentially FOUND A FIX
Body:
Hello, Persian Calendar community!

I was working on a custom calendar with the same code base when I noticed that there is an issue with updating notifications.

I found a fix for that and hope for community help to refactor my code and well-design it. Unfortunately, I wasn't able to test  with other apps' functions for the lack of a good laptop running an Android 11+ emulator.  Bad for me :)

The method : 

First of all, I removed a weak reference from the application service class to a normal instance (I actually know this may cause a memory leak  but just for a dirty test).


![application-service-removed-weak-reference](https://github.com/persian-calendar/persian-calendar/assets/83671885/550742f6-3122-4918-9253-d363a9e4d936)



Then, in the Application Service class, move important notifications and the **update()** method to Oncreate() and set the instance to this:


![Application-service-set-instance-this](https://github.com/persian-calendar/persian-calendar/assets/83671885/1e46de45-c85a-4720-8f1d-627a1dd9539f)



In the Update function, I noticed that you used the same channel ID and notification to create notification. I made them different. Below, you can observe the areas where they should differ...


I changed PRIORITY from **LOW  to  NotificationCompat.PRIORITY_DEFAULT** in **Update()** method too.


![same-notification-id-and-channel-id](https://github.com/persian-calendar/persian-calendar/assets/83671885/154d6267-fa27-4c0f-9315-fa1d17b181a3)

![use_channel_id_correctly](https://github.com/persian-calendar/persian-calendar/assets/83671885/d5cf41e5-0dc2-463d-9871-8348039aa5be)


![use_channel_id_correctly2](https://github.com/persian-calendar/persian-calendar/assets/83671885/e8b7175d-7a42-4230-b744-de4fba14d246)




Finally, in Main Activity where you call Function,
startEitherServiceOrWorker() uses the **application context** instead of **this**.

Call **startEitherServiceOrWorker(applicationContext)**.

![MainActivity-set-application-context-instead-of-this](https://github.com/persian-calendar/persian-calendar/assets/83671885/75fe924f-0146-4d39-a5c9-7e0d73226597)





note that on android 8+ foreground service should be started like this :

  if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.O)
                    ContextCompat.startForegroundService(
                        context,
                        Intent(context, ApplicationService::class.java)
                    )else{
                    context.startService(Intent(context, ApplicationService::class.java))
                    }

![Screenshot_2023-07-31_01-44-31](https://github.com/persian-calendar/persian-calendar/assets/83671885/e57e30ee-84e2-4e88-af6c-9236aab4bd42)




Firstly, the problem is that after clearing the app from Recents the process will be executed again for some unknown reason and kill the service. But with the above instructions, you bind service to application, and other things like channel that I noticed may make a difference.

NOTE: Dear community, please confirm these results and check whether they are working or not.

 I noticed that this problem has existed for at least 2 years based on commits. I believe and hope my results will permanently fix this issue. Thanks to all Persian-Calendar community ansesters and contributors. It is my honor to be with you.

یه چند مورد عرض میکنم که توی تقویم های دیگه هست منتهی جای خالیش تو تقویم شما حس میشه

Title: Espresso listen
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
<!-- A clear and concise description of what the problem is. Ex. I'm always frustrated when [...] --
It would be great to have an espresso timer. 
**Describe the solution you'd like**
<!-- A clear and concise description of what you want to happen. -->
Imagine an espresso timer where you start the recipe and the phone starts listening. Once the sound resembling the espresso machine operation is detected, it starts the timer. The timer for the shot pulling could be preset in the recipe options. Once a timer reaches the set number of seconds it turns red or in other way indicates that it reached the set time but not stop. The user should be able to set preinfusion time which would be indicated in the app. I guess as a first version the manual start of the timer would be great as well. However, the benefit of detecting the espresso machine starting the brew is the fact that no time is lost between starting the brew on the machine and clicking the timer.

Title: Copycat/Clone on Google Play

Hi @rozPierog 

I found this app on the Google Play Store: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=org.chicha.cofi from [this developer](https://play.google.com/store/apps/developer?id=TuTT). This guy just took your source code, added some ads in it and put it on the Play Store, without warning this is free software...

As you are the owner of the source code, you can follow [that procedure](https://gist.github.com/Poussinou/86136151f8b939fbaa48650666bf62d6) to remove this copy from the Store, if it's your wish.

Don't forget to tell Google that:

* The app is licensed under GNU GPL v3 license and this guy is violating it;
* He is falsely claiming he made the app;
* Provide some screenshots of your own app.

Title: Entering custom weights in multiplier menu is tedous
Body:
**Describe the bug**
Always requiring an integer part in the weight multiplier makes trying to enter custom weights rather tedious. E.g. adapting the default French Press recipe from 30.0g coffee to 45.0g coffee requires moving the cursor several times.
This happens because the app inserts a 0 as an integer part, if all digits left of the dot are deleted.

**To Reproduce**
1. Go to any recipe and open the multiplier menu (scale button, top right)
2. Place the cursor on the very right in the coffee multiplier field ("30.0|")
3. Repeatedly deleting digits by pressing the backspace button results in:
	1. "30.|0"
	2. "30|0.0"
	3. "3|0.0"
	4. "|0.0"

Alternatively placing the cursor to the left of the dot ("30|.0") and repeatedly pressing backspace results in a zero being inserted in the integer part:
1. "3|.0"
2. "|0.0"

**Expected behavior**
Adapting the weights should be as easy as
1. Deleting the current value
2. writing the desired value

**Screenshots**
none. Will provide if needed.

**Smartphone:**
 - Device: Pixel 4a
 - Android: 13
 - App Version: 1.20.0 (via F-Droid)

**Additional context**
To actually change the 30.0g to 45.0g I have to enter 45 before deleting the 30 which requires moving the cursor multiple times.

Title: Connect to and use Decent Scale
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Connecting to a good bluetooth scale would allow the app to be even greater, since being exact is very important - esp. flowrates are hard to hit (V60)

**Describe the solution you'd like**
The Decent Scale is open source and has a well documented API. Decent even offers a free scale for app developers (https://youtu.be/IpjN_FzjUW8?t=457) and is made for coffee lovers.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
There are other bluetooth scales, but I don't know them well enough to recommend.

**Additional context**
Especially getting shown the flowrates of the water you put into your V60 would be awesome. But also automatically calculating how much water you need for the exact amount of coffee the scale registers, etc - awesome!

Title: Sorting through tags
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

Once you add upwards of 5-6 recipes in the app, the recipe list starts appearing cluttered and it's a little difficult finding the recipe you need.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

The ability to add tags to recipes and filter recipes with tags. This could show up as buttons for various tags on the main screen underneath "Cofi" which once pressed would filter the list by the selected tag(s). The app could include some preset tags based on various brew methods like V60, Aeropress etc. with the additional ability for the user to manually add their own custom tags in addition to a description for recipes.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Maybe adding some form of colour coding for recipes to stand out? Either colour coding the whole recipe block or a portion of it. Although this might increase clutter in a way if done improperly.

**Additional context**

![Tag concept](https://github.com/rozPierog/Cofi/assets/25312934/4ff507c7-78e3-4629-b39d-437ea01edccc)
This is what the tag buttons could look like

Title: Timer to count down
Body: Please have Cofi either count DOWN the timer or make it an option. A count up timer is unintuitive.

Title: Add (option for) total brew timer including draw down time
Body: A lot of recipes have recommended total times, such as "draw down should finish at around 3:30". It would be great to have a timer running during brewing that keeps running after the last step until you hit stop to analyse your total brew time

Title: Don't open keyboard when editing step
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

When I am editing a recipe I sometimes want to get to a specific input field that isn't always the first. Right now it's always focusing the first input, no matter if that's the one I want to edit or not.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
When tapping a step in edit mode, keep the keyboard collapsed until I tap on an input field. This way I can choose which input I would like to edit or even rearrange the step.

![Screenshot_20230104-203715](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/113971170/210635522-bf5a3afb-19f2-478b-98c6-785ec0a6798f.png)

Title: Add an option to set a delay between when you press play and the first step starts.
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
<!-- A clear and concise description of what the problem is. Ex. I'm always frustrated when [...] -->
When I hit play, the first step immediately starts and the timer begins while I am moving my hand from the phone to the kettle. 

**Describe the solution you'd like**
<!-- A clear and concise description of what you want to happen. -->
Add a line in the recipe editor underneath "+ Add description" that says "+ Add start timer". Either the start timer could be a fixed duration or you can enter in your own duration. I think making it fixed, maybe 3 seconds, is simpler. There might not be much to gain by having a customizable start timer duration. The start timer could have a unique beep tone that leads you into the beep for the first step, like an audible countdown so you can tell when the first step is going to start without watching the screen.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
<!-- A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered. -->
There is a simple workaround, which is to add a wait step in the beginning of the recipe. Though I think adding a dedicated start timer feature would be more useful. Some people might experience the desire to have a delay but not think to add a wait step in the begging. An "+ Add start timer" option would provide an quick and easy way to get a delayed start, but also put the idea in their head that, that is an option.

Title: Reorder recipe steps via drag and drop.
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
<!-- A clear and concise description of what the problem is. Ex. I'm always frustrated when [...] -->
Rearranging steps in a recipe is not intuitive.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
<!-- A clear and concise description of what you want to happen. -->
Drag and drop steps in a recipe to change their order.

Title: Option to input cumulative time for steps
Body:
Most of the coffee brewing recipes I've found, like all of James Hoffman's, use cumulative time for steps — this step is finished after 1:30, the next after 1:45, the third after 2:15, etc. — rather than "this step takes 30 seconds, this one 15 seconds" and so on. Converting between them can be a bit error-prone and cumbersome when in a hurry.

It might be nice to have a toggle towards the top of the recipe creation screen that allows the user to swap the "Duration (s)" field with a "Completed after" field that allows MM:SS time entry.

Title: Move dependencies to a Gradle version catalog
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Gradle version catalog (https://docs.gradle.org/current/userguide/platforms.html#sub:central-declaration-of-dependencies) is a somewhat new feature where dependencies, versions, bundles and plugins are declared in a .toml file and then referred to in the build.gradle file as, for example` libs.kotlin` and` libs.plugins.kotlin.kapt`.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Move dependencies in build.gradle to a libs.versions.toml file.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Having dependencies in buildSrc is one option. Version catalog is more of a "native" Gradle feature.

**Additional context**
The version catalog may not be supported by automated dependency update bots yet.

Title: Better store screenshots
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

Currently Cofi store listing is pretty drab. It would be good to create some more inviting screenshots and hook it up to currently existing screenshot generation scripts.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
- use `./bin/createScreenshots` to create app screenshots on AVD for tablets and phones and copy it to host PC
- use https://github.com/IdeasOnCanvas/ScreenshotFramer CLI to run a script that would compose those screenshots into some frames, and colors
- save it to fastlane folder

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
- manually editing screenshots - that goes against the spirit of automating release process

Title: Widget is not placed and does not work in huawei launcher
Lack of Ability to set a (future) Start Day for taking a Medication
Body:
This feature would be VERY useful especially when you like to taper or substitute a drug.

In that cases, you have planned changes of the amount or the kind of a medication, i.e. you know at a special day you have to lower (or to rise, when the dose must be increased) the dose of a medication, for example for one week 30mg, next week 20mg, the following week 10mg, then a substitution by another drug.

To be able to map this, a planned start for a medication would be necessary, which unfortunately currently isn't possible.

Anyway, RXDroid is a great app - I really like it!

Title: [Enhancement] Store DB on WebDav/Cloud Storage
Body:
Have an option to store and sync your database on a personal cloud file storage. This way if a phone dies unexpectedly, is lost, or stolen, you don't have to start from scratch.

Personally, I use Nextcloud, so I'd like to see WebDav first. I suspect doing a Google Drive integration would be a pretty popular option too.

The current method backing up to off device is a bit cumbersome and more problematically manual. 

- manually backup database
- click +
- select method of backup
- click acknowledgement for method

This should be automatic. This should a set-and-forget-it option. Because honesty, who remembers to manually remembers to back all the things. Once you setup the automatic backup it should work unless outside issues occur. Rxdroid already self-prunes the database which is good. 

Anytime a changing event happens on the DB, it should initiate an non-blocking upload afterwards.

As for polling for updates, I would poll 5 minutes before each time slot. Also have an option in settings to force a poll/sync for changes in time slots and testing.

Also, normal usage should run from a locally cached copy so an internet outage doesn't block the normal function of the app.

Title: Activity memory leak caused by the anonymous thread
solve issue of low contrast
Body:
The original text color of the component is '#FFFFFF', and the contrast between the text color ('#FFFFFF') and the background color ('#FF5722') does not meet the standard of WCAG 2.1. In other words, this color cannot be easily seen by everyone. So, to solve this problem, our team designed a tool that can automatically detect and repair such problems. The test report and recommended replacement colors ('#282828') are as follows:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/101503193/167430688-b94fb1fe-17c1-4e2b-8132-88e26ce790e3.png)
The figure on the left is the original page, the figure on the right is the repaired page, and the figure below is the problem detection report.
If you think it is useful, please give me feedback. Your feedback is very important to me. We sincerely hope to receive your suggestions and opinions as an app developer.

Title: Show "as needed" drugs on all days, even if not scheduled
Body:
If a medication is set for repetition every x days, but also marked take as needed, it should be shown even on days when there are no scheduled doses. Otherwise it's not possible to keep track of extra doses taken on these days.

Thanks for the app by the way, it's very useful for me.

Title: Unable to set current day as the end
Body:
Example situation:
A user forgets to mark the end of taking medications (or can't as they could not know it initially) and on the last day they would like to save the progress instead of marking the medication as 'inactive' thus hiding (or erasing) it from the log.
Expected behavior:
They can set current day as the end.
Actual behavior:
They can only set the next day as the end (which is counted as still taking meds).

Title: Feature Requests
Body:
I see you're just working on fixing bugs, but I'd like to record some of the things/ideas to maybe, implement on rxdroid.

* Description

> A complete description of the drug. To be used to write down the company name and information about it

* Quick description

> To help the user remember what that pill is for. A quick description like "In case of headache", "Joint pain", "Fever"; "Supplement"

* Expiration date

> Some pills like those for headaches are only taken when you have a problem and it would be nice to have an alarm warning when those are about to expire or if they have expired.

* Photos

> To have a picture of the drub box so it is easier to buy it when you go to the pharmacy

* Auto-Backup

> An auto-backup feature to save the user database everyday

Title: Minor enhancement
Body:
Hello! Really love how detailed you can make your schedule in this app! Though I have a couple concerns. First, I could not find a save button or an OK button to save my medication to the list. The back button did the trick, though I find it a bit inconvenient, especially for old people. 

Additionally, when selecting pill amounts for quantity and refills, I feel like a text input, prompting the num-pad style keyboard would make for good accessibility as well. Selecting my amount at 270 was a bit difficult and I tapped off the prompt a couple times.

Title: start day of a period
Body:
I didn't find a possibility to set a start day for a medication (for example 7 days or one month). I can define of the period but not the starting day. The starting is always the day when you add a new treatment.
The option for setting the start day too, would be (at least in some cases) a very useful option.
Thank you for good app on f-droid, it's helping me a lot.

F-Droid has a [new UI](https://f-droid.org/posts/a-new-ux-6-years-in-the-making/) and thus allows applications to show screenshots and e.g. release notes for updates. It would be nice if you could use this possibility and embed them for your application.

First thank you for your app.
I believe for people with bad eyes, it would be better to make the pale gray symbols are a bit darker for better readability. (The same in the dark mode, they should be lighter). More contrast helps a lot, if one has with problems with his eyes.

Title: Add a CSV to ICS helper tool
Body:
I wrote this to extract the schedule from the website of the EuroBSDCon: https://2023.eurobsdcon.org/program/

Copy-paste the tables into a spreadsheet (like LibreOffice Calc), export as CSV (, as delimiter, " as quote).

It expects:
- lines that start with the event type and day
- lines starting with an empty cell that lists the tracks,
- lines that start with start_time (eventually end), then each talk.

Lot of hardcoding and debug output to cleanup.

Title: title and day dates not displayed
Body:
when I add [cosin's schedule](https://pretalx.cosin.ch/cosin2023/schedule/export/schedule.xml) to gigitty, neither the title of the event nor the date of the days is displayed. (only the name of the day, like "Sun"). But in the xml the corresponding tags are there, with reasonably-looking values.

I diffed cosin's xml with [GPN21's](https://cfp.gulas.ch/gpn21/schedule/export/schedule.xml) - there is no difference in structure for the event subtree - they have the same tags. 

This may not be an issue with giggity but with frab, but I have the feeling it's most easily solved by someone who knows how giggity digests the data.

Title: add notification when a new event is added
Body:
Hi,

I think it is a good thing adding a notification on new event added to giggity.

cheers

Title: Bug: updating an ics does not change event name
Body:
Hi,

I imported a custom .ics file from a link, then noticed the X-WR-CALNAME entry was wrong (as in, the title of the entire file).

I can refresh the ics all I want, it doesn't change the title of the thing anymore. (Also, as others have noted, I can't delete my own imported .ics anymore, which I would like to do, for older events I added)

Title: Feature request: Remove imported .ics-files
Body: I've been experimenting with how to make a good .ics for giggity and the menu is now full of garbage. I'd like for an easy way to get rid of them (like a delete-action in the click-and-hold popup menu), but I'd settle for a workaround doable via termux or similar.

Title: Feature request: Add personal meetings
Body:
During a conference one wants to meet with some person(s) besides the official programm. Morevoer, it is possible that there is something like an exhibision of companies and attendees will make appointments with some of them. For these use cases, it would be interesting to have a new feature, which makes it possible to add such personal meetings besides the official programm.

If this would be possible, then the different views should include such personal meetings and they should also taken into consideration when something new will overlapp with the things already saved.

Title: Support Sched conferences better
Body:
Some conferences started to use https://sched.com/ for their program website, e.g. https://oat21.sched.com/?iframe=no . They provide also a ICS file which then can be used in giggity as well, e.g. https://oat21.sched.com/all.ics . However, this approach has some drawbacks:
- There is no line break in the description which makes them more or less impossible to read.
- Any additional HTML markup (bold, italics, enumeration) in the description of the talks/session is getting lost.
- The persons are not part of the ics file.

Would a direct support for Sched conferences in giggity be possible? I would assume that
1. the user can enter the sched website like https://oat21.sched.com/,
2. then the software knows where to look for the ics file `+all.ics` and create a giggity schedule from that
3. instead of using the description from the ics file, giggity can then go to each seperate event page (the URLs are part of the ics file) and grab the nicer formatted description as well as the person from that website, e.g. https://oat21.sched.com/event/3e892bad5fcace002664b28783802f05?iframe=no

My feeling is, that most of these is maybe easy to build with the things which are already coded here. However, I don't know the codebase and I am not experience to do anything with Java.

Could this suggested feature become part of giggity?

Title: FR: Delete / edit schedules on the list
Body:
Thank you for Giggity on F-Droid!

I have two wishes:
- I would like to be able to delete schedules from the list, at least when I have added them myself.
- I would like to be able to customize the title of a schedule in the list, e.g. add a name. Then I could better distinguish a friend's or colleague's schedule for the same event in the list from my own.

Title: Multi-days event
Body:
Hello! I'm having some trouble with an event that **last over 24 hours**. I cannot set the `<duration>` value over 24 (_Network I/O problem: parse error. Invalid int value for HourOfDay_).

How can I achieve this?


I tried to split an event in 2 days like this:

```
<day date="2019-11-15">
<room name="ABC">
<title>Eventname1</title>
<event id="5f76dc3808c8677ca3515213db89291ecaca9b2dbf3e6">
<start>10:00</start>
<duration>14:00</duration>
</event>
</room>
</day>

<day date="2019-11-16">
<room name="ABC">
<title>Eventname1</title>
<event id="5f76dc3808c8677ca3515213db89291ecaca9b2dbf3e6">
<start>00:00</start>
<duration>16:00</duration>
</event>
</room>
</day>
```

**But the second event doesn't show up.**

--------------

I've found out that an **event that starts before 06:00 is not recognized and doesn't show up**. For example:

```
<day date="2019-11-15">
<room name="ABC">
<title>Eventname1</title>
<event id="5f76dc3808c8677ca3515213db89291ecaca9b2dbf3e6">
<start>05:00</start>
<duration>04:00</duration>
</event>
</room>
</day>
```
**doesn't work**, but:
```
<day date="2019-11-15">
<room name="ABC">
<title>Eventname1</title>
<event id="5f76dc3808c8677ca3515213db89291ecaca9b2dbf3e6">
<start>06:00</start>
<duration>04:00</duration>
</event>
</room>
</day>
```
**is working**.


Why is that? Any suggestions about this issue?

Thank you so much!

Title: Using images and CSS in descriptions of events
Body:
Hi, I'd like to use Giggity for a conference I am organizing and one of the issues is that I would like to be able to use images in the descriptions of talks (the abstract of the talk itself would be followed by the speaker's bio and picture). Some HTML tags (links, lists, `strong,` etc.) are supported, so I figured I'd try with `img`, but the result is only a blue square, whether I link to an external image or I put it base64 encoded in the tag. There seems to be no CSS support either.
Is this a bug or a feature? Is there a way to display images in the descriptions?

Title: Conference hashtag for sharing on Twitter
Body: Would it be possible to save the conference hashtag and then use it for sharing via twitter? However, currently these text is using the whole abstract which will almost certainly be too many characters. Thus, one would also need to restrict this. Any hint where this is handled in the code?

Title: Display events by time (i.e. now and next display) also before the conference starts
Body:
It is IMO useful to have the events listed by time also for planning your attendance of a conference ahead. Such a list is already done by the "Now and Next" display. However, this is not available on non-conference days.

Can this view of the events by time been expanded such that it becomes also available on days before the conference (and afterwards)?

I would envision to still have the message "No events today" but then below an option to choose a specific conference day and then see all events at that day sort by the time.

Title: wishlist: Merging calendars
Body:
Some events have parallel calendars for different "tracks". For example, at IETF105 there is the [calendar of official events](https://datatracker.ietf.org/meeting/105/agenda.ics), and a parallel [calendar of unofficial side meetings](https://tools.ietf.org/agenda/105/sidemeetings.ics).

It Would Be Nice If there was a way to have giggity display both in overlay (just displaying events from both in a single view, with the event details indicating the source when there are multiple calendars).

A first iteration may not even need capabilities for creating such a composite-calendar event from GUI input, just having multiple calendars from a JSON would be a good starting point.

Title: Handle the GEO iCal property for room location ?
Body:
The iCalendar standard has provision for a [GEO](https://icalendar.org/iCalendar-RFC-5545/3-8-1-6-geographic-position.html) property on events, although it doesn't seem to be usable in most clients, cf. the [ticket for NextCloud](https://github.com/nextcloud/calendar/issues/915), and Thunderbird tickets [583569](https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=583569), [1370160](https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=1370160).

It could be interesting to use those when available to save the work of adding them to the JSON.

Title: Speaker information given as attendee in ICal files aren't picked up
Body:
If you look at events on the FOSDEM schedule (provided from the built-in list of conferences) for example, speaker names are given at the top of the events. So the UI is obviously able to show speaker information.

However, if you import an iCal file by it's URL it doesn't show given attendees as speakers. To reproduce use FOSDEM's iCal file for example: https://fosdem.org/2018/schedule/ical

Title: property recording
Body:
Some talks might or might not be recorded, it would be very handy for planning a conference with the app if that information was easily available. It seems there was no taken care of tags/markup for that purpose and thus the only but less user friendly (vs cam or stroken through cam icon) variant would be to add that to the description.

example: [GPN18](https://entropia.de/GPN18:Fahrplan:XML?action=raw) has tagging for “no recording/streaming”

```
               <recording>
                   <license></license>
                   <optout>false</optout>
               </recording>
```

Title: Add trackname to location for FOSDEM
Body: Please add the track name such as Python, Mozilla etc. to the location on the left. This allows the user to find more quickly what he or she is looking for.

Title: Collaborate with other sites/apps on more input formats ?
Body:
Following #29 , I just found [MozFest](https://guidebook.com/guide/114124/) that uses yet another thing for their program, which uses some [custom JSON format](https://guidebook.com/api/v1/public_event/?guide__id=114124&limit=0).

Last summer I attended a [workshop at RMLL](https://prog2017.rmll.info/programme/esthetique-du-libre-engagement-participation-cooperation/not-all-events-on-the-radar-yet-let-s-change-that?lang=en) where we discussed events schedule curation for websites like [radar.squat.net](https://radar.squat.net/en).
I mentioned Giggity on [the notes we took](https://md.gnom.space/radar?view#).

I think we should collaborate instead of reimplementing the same parsers N times. It's probably simpler to add events to some website supporting known formats and point Giggity to it when the event website doesn't provide a suitable format.
