Title: [Feature Request] Add Option to skip a step in the first/last loop
Body:
I would love the option to skip a step on the first or last loop of a timer. I use the app for my workouts and they often have the structure

* Start step
* Workout
* Pause
* End step

But after I am done with the last workout set I don't want the pause. I considered moving the pause before the workout and using it as my start step but my pauses are considerably longer than the start step. The app [Interval Timer](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=cc.dreamspark.intervaltimer) provides a similar feature.

So currently the above example with a loop count of 3 would produce the following steps.

* Start step
* Workout
* Pause
* Workout
* Pause
* Workout
* Pause
* End step

But if we added the "skip last" option added to the "pause" step it would be

* Start step
* Workout
* Pause
* Workout
* Pause
* Workout
* End step

Skipping the last pause. Similarly with the "skip first" feature added to the "pause" it would look like this

* Start step
* Workout
* Workout
* Pause
* Workout
* Pause
* End step

Although this doesn't make much sense in the example i chose.

I propose adding the feature as an extra option in the step where it is already possible to choose halt, beep, etc. This would enable user to combine both features changing the above example to

* Start step
* Workout
* Workout
* Pause
* Workout
* Timer end

Of course this feature should also enable Users to skip a step in the the first and last loop of a Group. if we use the example

* Start step
* Outer
  * Group, loop count: 3
    * Inner
    * Inner2
* Timer end

With a loop count of 3 and a "skip last" on "Inner 2" this would result in the following execution

* Start step
* Outer
* Inner
* Inner 2
* Inner
* Inner 2
* Inner
* Outer
* Inner
* Inner 2
* Inner
* Inner 2
* Inner
* Outer
* Inner
* Inner 2
* Inner
* Inner 2
* Inner
* Timer end

Another consideration is that these options should not be available on the start step and end step as these only ever get executed one anyway.

Although I have no experience in App development, I am familiar with Java and Kotlin and would be willing to submit a PR adding the functionality if we agree that the feature is useful.

Hi,

Title: [Bug] Changing tag selection of running activity impossible (ver 1.34)
Body:
This bug became instantly visible after F-Droid upgraded the app from 1.33 to 1.34.

Running activities cannot have any (record) tags applied to them. This happens in all of the following circumstances:
- with “show record tag selection“ enabled, a selected tag is not saved
- changing tags on *running* activities is not saved
	- (changing tags on stopped entries does still work)
- when a *running* activity has a tag applied, all get cleared when trying to change the tag selection. To reproduce:
	1. add a tag to a saved, *stopped* entry (or select one with some tags)
	2. “continue” this stopped entry using the edit screen, continuing the timer
	3. now the tags are still saved
	4. edit the now *running* activity, changing the tag selection (tested adding & removing one tag)
	5. see how the running activity looses all tags saved
- “repeating” a former activity with tags does not work either
	- the then running activity has no tags applied
	- (the saved entry is unchanged / keeps its tags)

It is not just a rendering issue as the edit screen for that entry does also display no tags as applied and selecting a tag once in that edit screen works as before.

OS: Android 13 (Lineage OS with microG)
Phone: Fairphone 4
App Version: 1.34
Source: F-Droid

Title: [Bug] Automatic export fails
Body:
The automatic export function sometimes fails to upload the .csv file to Google Drive. I get a 'Upload failed: an error occured' notification at midnight. There have been instances where it has worked for me, so there is a happy flow where everything works as expected. Therefore, I don't expect it to be a permission issue.

I have hooked my phone to logcat and tried to re-create the automatic export event. A few times I have managed to re-create the bug and got the same exception every time:

```

01-28 00:00:00.558  2577  1712 E ContentFileSyncMonitor: 	at java.util.concurrent.FutureTask.run(FutureTask.java:266)
```

My hunch: a file is [opened](https://github.com/Razeeman/Android-SimpleTimeTracker/blob/ab2a6799f81dab321ea82487b4c7305b4e40fd79/data_local/src/main/java/com/example/util/simpletimetracker/data_local/resolver/CsvRepoImpl.kt#L52) on the `automaticExportUri` location. Then, later on, when it tries to write to that file, it cannot access that newly created file, resulting in an error. Does it have something to do with writing to a virtual storage location?

It is worth noting that I am using a Cat S22 Flip with Android 11. It could have something to do with that as well. It could be that my phone is slow. Or the virtual storage address isn't open/ready at time of exporting? That could explain why it sometimes does work.

Title: [Feature Request] Sliding Time Window view for Record History
Body:
### TL;DR
Record History window should have an option to view records from the last N hours.

### Current situation
The pane that shows record history only does so as a daily truncated view, meaning *you're only be able to see records that were created on the selected day* between 0~24hrs. The day can be changed by using the arrows, or selected by using the bottom center button to open a calendar.

### The 'Problem'
It can make viewing history at a glance more difficult for people with atypical schedules. For example: if I wake up at 8pm/20:00 for a night shift, *when the day rolls over after midnight, I'd have to press the left arrow to view 'yesterday'* in order to see the records from when my 'day' started. If there's a record that spans between the days, I have to either press it to see the proper duration, or flip back and forth and add up the times.

### Request
In addition to a truncated day view, I'd really like to see a sliding window view that shows all records for the last N hours. At least an option to *view records from the last 24 hours*. I'd suggest tab bar to the selection calendar, with tabs labeled "Day" for the current calendar UI (which should probably remain the default) and "Time" which has buttons for selecting, or dials for configuring, the timeframe for the sliding window. Ideally this would be a configurable number of hours, or have multiple choices (like 24, 48, 72).

#### P.s.
Admittedly, this is a very minor UX inconvenience, so I'd understand if this request is denied if implementation is non-trivial due to how your app is structured. I thank you for your time and consideration of this request.

Title: Clamp records to time range when filtering by time
Body:
I think there should be a feature to clamp time records to the filtered time range instead of including the full record length. This way, it's possible to get statistics for that specific time range (eg. Activity Durations) instead of displaying the full activity.

Scenarios in which this is useful:
Let's say I have a Work record that typically goes from 900-1700. On one day, the record goes from 900-1900.
If I filter the time range from 1700-2200 then I will see this 10 hour Work record even though I'm more interested in the time range after work and would expect to only see 2 hours of Work.

There are a handful of changes in this pull request:
1. Return RecordBase? instead of Boolean in the filter methods. This allows us to make changes to the records returned.
2. Update the selectedByTimeOfDay method to clamp records to the filtered time range and potentially output multiple records for records that span multiple days.
3. Use mapNotNull and flatten instead of filter in order to convert the List<List<RecordBase>?> to List<RecordBase>

Title: [Feature] Show activity reminder only after goal time reached

When "Show activity reminder" is enabled, I would like an additional option to appear below the "Recurrent" option to only have the reminder show after the activity's session goal time has been reached. Some potential option descriptions are "Only show after session goal" or "Wait until session goal".

This seems like a fairly trivial option to add; I'm familiar enough with writing Kotlin where I could attempt to create a pull request for this, although I'm sure it would take @Razeeman much less time to add.

This app has been very helpful for my productivity, and it's very nice to use. Thanks!

Title: Feature Request : Search in Description
Body:
Hi

Thanks for your work on this app, it may just be what I was looking for.

Till now I was using an excel sheet for logging my activities, I was looking for an app with some automation & tagging capabilities. This seems to cover a lot of what I was looking for. 

Have a few feature requests which I was hoping would be possible -

1) Search in Comments field - I sometimes make extensive notes for a particular activity, it would be nice if you could search through old records to find a particular Comment and then copy that if needed

2) Split Timer - I like to log the entire 24 hours, it would be nice to have a split timer like function where as soon as you press a button one activity ends and the next one begins automatically without having to necessarily specify a tag or category or anything all of which one can go back and fill in later

3) Presets - it would be nice to have a set of presets of tags & description so for eg - if there is a preset of 
Activity : TV
Category : Entertainment
Comments : stranger things
Tags : netflix, entertainment 
When I start watching I can select that preset instead of either having to search for a previous record & then copying it or having to manually enter all the details every time. If this came with some sort of fuzzy search where as soon as you typed "stranger.." it came up as a prompt that would be fantastic

4) if the Comment entry is very big it would be nice to have a collapse all/expand all button so you can view the daily records easier


Some questions - 
1) Are there any app limits in the number of Activities or Categories or Tags etc, as in how big the app data becomes? Because if I switch from excel and much later find there are some limits to how many tags can be created etc it'll be a downer
2) Are you expecting to launch a desktop or web version so it can be used when one is on the pc?

One issue I found with some early testing - When entering Comments which has multiple paragraphs the backup csv file saves every new paragraph in a new line


Cheers

edited to correct Description to Comments

As for example / reference, Timelog app does that.

Title: FR: Minus time
Body:
I use Simple Time Tracker to track my days at work. At my workplace we have flexible working days. That means that I one day can work 9 hours and another 7 hours.

But I have to distinguish between extra time worked over 7,5 hours and minus time in the company HR system. I can accumulate plus time in my time bank.I had to manually calculate ho much time I have in my time bank in the app. I started tagging plus time as an own tag; timebank, but I'm not able to set minus time in the app. Again I have to calculate manually.

Therefore it would be nice to be able to set the start time to say 16:00 and endtime to 15:00. Becuase I left for 1 hour early one day. That way get -1 hour registered on the tag; timebank. The total for week/month/year/overall statistic would be correct and easy to comepare with the HR system. To make sure people don't make a mistake. A setting in the tag could be added to allow minus time on that specific tag or mor generally in the settings page.

I would really appreciate if this feature could be added. 😊

Title: Allow import without clearing existing Records (aka Merge Import)
Body:
Let's say I have this fantastic app installed on my phone and on my tablet. Say I use it most of the time while on my phone and that's where I want to keep the main database. Sometimes I use it on my tablet. I want to be able to save a backup file on my tablet and transfer this backup file over to my phone. Then on my phone, I want to "merge import" this backup file from my tablet without deleting any Records from the phone.

I hope I explained this clearly. I want to be able to use this app on my phone and on my tablet. I want the Records I save on my tablet to be importable into my phone without affecting the Records already existing on my phone.

Title: [Feature Request] Widgets customisation
Body:
I had some thoughts about ProgressBars widgets and how they can be improved:
- Simplest (stop gap implementation) make one more compact widget to use less grid space and another widget that continues to use the current sizes but can be more verbose.
- More involved would be to include options when placing widgets to resize the spacing between the text in the widget and allow the user to customise the colours including fonts and background fill of the widget and transparency.

An alternative or complementary idea (additional to above/existing widgets):
- A new type of widget that can be stretched to a whole page (or just larger) that shows multiple existing in-app ProgressBar timers. (Not sure if the widget versions of ProgressBar are meant to be separate/distinct from the main app ProgressBars timers or if it's a bug.)

Title: [Bug] Multiple ProgressBar Widgets

By doesn't work you can only tap and edit the last widget making the first widget look like it's frozen. A notification should still popup when the timer completes it just can't be edited before/after and looks like it's bugged.

Title: Quickly Add timer by amount of hours from now (Duration instead of precise timings)
Body:
I use progress bar to ensure that I eat the next meal only after 4 hours of eating something before. So, it would be quicker if we could also add progress bars by the duration from now (apart from the present system where we have to give precise date and time).

Also, it would be useful if the app could remember what was the last custom duration set. So that, we could create the next one similarly and quickly.

Or such progress bars could be reactivated at any other time and they could again go on for next 4 hours.

---

# Motivation

What I observe is that the present design is suitable for long term events (like a fixed date and time in future), but not that great for repetitive short term events.

Title: Add an option to sync timers across the network
Body:
This would be really useful for tracking event schedules, but it'd need a way to sync the data across the network.

I think there could be a concept of boards added. A single board can hold multiple timers, can be shared with read-only and read-write access to different users.

This way one could create an event schedule as a board and share it with everyone involved in it (could be a technical team or attendants).

What do you think?

Title: Calendar/Screen time developement over time
Body:
Hi, first of all I am really happy with your app, I must be using it over a year now. I really love it for it's simplicity and that it "just works" (no mental overhead hehe). I do not care about "per app usage" and so on, I think that is really unnecessairy at least for me, I never felt like I need it. Screen time is screen time after all :))

I also love that I can scroll back the clock to see "total time usage".

If there is one feature I keep thinking of, is a possibility to "go back in time" and see the development of my screen time.
That would allow two things:
1. One could go back to a specific day and see the screen time
2. See the development of screen time over a specified amount of days.

No. 1 could be implemented as a calendar, while No. 2 could be implemented as a graph. Although one does not depend on the other necessairily. What they obviously have in common, is that it would log the exact days and time of screen usage (not sure if that is already the case internally or not).

This can/should totally be an optional feature, as it may potentially reveal more data about the user if somebody is concerned about that.

I am not sure whether this is outside of the scope of this app, it would surely require some work on. As this could be totally optional it would hardly bloat the app on the user side though (maybe accessable through a tiny hamburger menu in the top right).

Anyways I thought I would share as 1. for me it would be very useful, and 2. I was thinking about this for a while so I thought I'd just share :)

Thanks again for the great work :)

Title: [Feature request] Export history to CSV
Body:
Why I'm asking for this: I look for phone app for automatic sleep tracking. Easiest way is to define sleep as longest time of screen turned off.
Existing sleep trackers usually require manual start/stop and are overbloated for such a simple purpose. Same for phone activity trackers - they usually include apps usage analytics, time warnings, blocking of overusage, etc.
So you app is very close to a perfect match. Anyone can easily implement any analyzing logic, charts and statistics in a spreadsheet table basing on data from Screen Time.

CSV seems to be an optimal export format, containing just event and its timestamp. The latter might be Unixtime, easiest to do computations with it. So it might be like following:
```
"on"; "1655323662";
"off"; "1655323667";
```

Additional dependency is to save history log for a long time, it is not preserved for now, as far as I see.

I believe all of that should add small overhead to code and UI (just one "export" button).

Couple of unrelated things, I can create separate issues for them if necessary:

1) As far as I see, there is no option to disable notification from Screen Time. I did it in Android system settings, but some people might miss this way.
2) I have "Screen Time is running in background" system notification on Android 8. Not sure why it appears since a lot of apps are working in background without this notification.

Paid version with that features is also an option, especially taking into account it's opensource 👍

Title: FR: Support Direct Boot mode / show on first lock screen
Body:
I tried 2.11 from F-Droid.

The stock AOSP clock supports showing the alarm on top of the first login screen, this is called direct boot.

[More info here](https://github.com/yuriykulikov/AlarmClock/issues/398)

GrapheneOS has auto-reboot as a security method, as a reboot clears the encryption keys from RAM. This happens when the device is not unlocked for x hours, for example at night.

Also it (and hopefully other android versions) had automatic system updates with automatic reboot, here this alarm klock would then also not display.

Could be useful to implement.

Title: Address conflict with Android "swipe back" gesture in dismiss alarm slider
Body:
Now that Google has, for whatever reason, discontinued 2 button mode (my old favorite), I find myself constantly going "back" when trying to pull the dismiss alarm slider to the right... 

In a half-awake state at the start of the day, ending up on your home screen while your dreaded alarm noise is playing, fumbling around trying to switch apps so you can try to dismiss the alarm again - it tends to start you off on the wrong foot.

Mainly I blame Google, of course, because I never had this problem with the design of your app before.

Proposed solutions I've thought of:

1\. Add blank padding space on the left of the screen to avoid the "back" gesture startup hitbox.
~~2\. Reverse the swipe direction.~~ (nevermind, you can "back" from the right side of the screen, too...)
2. ...Make the slider vertical?

Title: [Feature request] Multiple alarm sounds
Body:
Hi
Would it be possible to create a playlist of notification sounds that Alarm Klock could randomly or sequentially pick one? 

For example using the same alarm/notification sound interface to select sounds you could add these to a list and then randomly or remember the number of times the alarm has sounded to select the next alarm sound down the list. It seems like a nice minor/low priority idea that could functionally work as an "Alarm of the day/week" feature (depending on how frequently the user sets their alarm to go off). Also would be nice to be able to drag re-order the playlist if using a file explorer to add songs puts them in the wrong order or the user decides to change the order.

Regarding random alarm sound selection maybe another option would be to allow play-once rather than full random list again. ie instead of some sounds being played more frequently by chance allow unplayed sounds a chance to be played and then when everything has played once per alarm firing off re-allow the already played sounds. More options are generally better (but I guess that's more work for the programmer)!

Title: Feature Request: speak current time and/or alarm label
Body:
It would be really nice to optionally use TTS to read the current time and/or alarm label after one or more plays of the alarm tone.

Example:
* Settings
  * Alarm labeled "Wake Up"
  * Time set to "7:00 AM"
  * Sleep set to "10 minutes"
  * Read time is set on
  * Read label is set on
  * TTS delay is set to 1 play
* Effect
  1. Alarm tone plays once
  2. Alarm label is read aloud
  3. The current time is read aloud: "seven A M"
  4. The alarm tone continues playback as usual
  5. The user hits snooze
  6. 10 minutes elapses and the alarm workflow starts again
  7. Alarm tone plays once
  7. Alarm label is read aloud
  8. The current time is read aloud: "seven ten A M"
  9. Repeat as needed

A good first pass implementation might just be to read the time after a fixed number of tone plays. As a reference, the Samsung clock app only reads the current time after one play of the alarm tone.

I will happily help you test this feature.

PS: do you have a preferred way to reference "alarm tone plays"? That feels really clunky to me.

Title: Session alarm notification not triggered on time
Body:
# Environment
- App version: 1.63.0-FOSDEM-Edition
- Android version: 13
- Device: Google Pixel 4a

# How to reproduce
1. Press the action bar button to program a notification for a session
2. Lock the device screen
3. Wait for the session time

# Observed behavior
- The notification is not triggered on time. It is delayed by up to 30 minutes, which is too late to catch up with the session.

# Expected behavior
- The alarm should be triggered at the exact time, even if the app is not in the foreground or the device is in sleep mode.

# Cause of the issue
The notification is scheduled by calling [`AlarmManager.set()`](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/app/AlarmManager#set(int,%20long,%20android.app.PendingIntent)). Since API 19, this method is only suitable to run deferrable background work (batched with other apps work), not for alarm clock-style alarms shown to the user.

# Solution
The notification must be scheduled using [`AlarmManagerCompat.setExactAndAllowWhileIdle()`](https://developer.android.com/reference/androidx/core/app/AlarmManagerCompat#setExactAndAllowWhileIdle(android.app.AlarmManager,int,long,android.app.PendingIntent)), and only after requesting the [SCHEDULE_EXACT_ALARM](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/Manifest.permission#SCHEDULE_EXACT_ALARM) permission on API 31 (Android 12) and later.
On API >=31, [`AlarmManager.canScheduleExactAlarms()`](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/app/AlarmManager#canScheduleExactAlarms()) should be called to determine if the permission needs to be requested, and the alarm may only be scheduled after it has been granted.
On lower API levels, the permission doesn't exist and the alarm can be scheduled directly.

For reference, you can check [the commit](https://github.com/cbeyls/fosdem-companion-android/commit/53d9d5e60aca8468d84488a217fddb6174ca7121) where I added the permission handling to my app.

Title: Multiple schedule updates (especially "0 added, 0 changed, 0 removed" pseudo-changesets)
Body:
During 37c3 it happened to me that the app had "downloaded" multiple changesets. This resulted in an incomplete list of changes, likely because only the last list of changes was shown and not a merger.

It also happened that I was looking at the list of changes, clicked on an event to look at the event description and when I returned to the list of changes it had changed (or was even gone entirely). 

Part of the problem was that the app loaded "0 added, 0 changed, 0 removed" pseudo-changesets that for some reason happened pretty often.

# Suggestion
1. ignore 0-0-0-updates, at least for the list of changes
2. keep all lists of changes accessible in the UI and not only the latest one, such that a user can at least look at the older ones manually
3. maybe aggregate the changes
4. maybe block updates when the user is looking at the list of changes (even when he opens an event description and returns to the list of changes)

Title: Layout and design of a session card
Body:
Status: :green_circle: Ready to work on

# Current state
- A session card shows the basic information of a session:
  - title, 
  - subtitle, 
  - speaker names, 
  - track name, 
  - languages. 
- The track name determines the background color of a session card. 
- Additionally, the favored state is shown by an optional border and by using a brighter background color.

![session2](https://github.com/EventFahrplan/EventFahrplan/assets/144518/86c2ed84-4faa-4bed-9527-170dc2f09910)

- The card also shows with a bell icon if an alarm is set and with a camera icon if the session is recorded.
- The height of a card corresponds to the duration of the session. Cards of longer sessions have a lot of space to show text and icons. Cards of short sessions lack the space to show any information, cut off text and are hard to tap.

![session1](https://github.com/EventFahrplan/EventFahrplan/assets/144518/e8abc792-c722-4fc3-be9f-b121e641edb6)


# Task / requirements
- Given the current drawbacks the session card design has been rethought.
- The new design must consider the following aspects:
  - colors of track names
  - optional alarm and camera icons
  - planned tagging concept to allow applying various custom tags to a session, see #12
  - very short sessions with a duration of 5 minutes (lightning talks)

# Tools
- The session card design must be prepared in [Penpot](https://penpot.app).

# Hints
- Potential inspiration: Calendar apps deal with a similar challenge. There, grouping and collapsing elements can be seen.

# Related
- [Issue #514: Improve text rendering for session elements](https://github.com/EventFahrplan/EventFahrplan/issues/514)

Title: Rethink user flow and positioning of interaction elements
Body:
Status: :green_circle: Ready to work on

# Preliminaries
- The app aims to provide a modern and commonly accessible user interface and user experience.
- Meanwhile the feature set has grown over time and screens were added and changed.
- At the same time the new devices appeared on the market. Screen dimensions have been increased.

# Current state
- Currently, users can interact through action elements positioned in the top bar, by long pressing a session and through actions placed on a session row item (delete session alarm).
  ![long-press](https://github.com/EventFahrplan/EventFahrplan/assets/144518/fdded881-239c-434c-b708-b4a8136ccc22)
![favorite-items](https://github.com/EventFahrplan/EventFahrplan/assets/144518/3c0aa2d8-489c-4b30-9c61-597ba0fbd579)
- In the top bar action elements, depending on the context,
  - target screens (favorites, alarms, schedule changes, settings, info), 
  - target more screens, not available in all apps (sponsors, map)
  ![topbar](https://github.com/EventFahrplan/EventFahrplan/assets/144518/c69a8dfe-914a-4233-9d17-a7af97e40df2)
  - act in the context of a session, e.g.
   ![session-details](https://github.com/EventFahrplan/EventFahrplan/assets/144518/6aa79f28-5f03-46e7-96dc-6106387faf1a)
    - add to resp. remove from favorites
    - schedule resp. cancel alarm
    - submit feedback and rating
    - add to calendar
    - share
    - close detail screen, 
  - manually requesting an update of the whole schedule, 
  - searching (not implemented yet)
  - deleting individual or all favorites, 
  - deleting all alarms.
- The top bar hosts different action elements on smartphones and tablets due to the main / detail screen layouts on tablets.

# Task
- On large devices top bar action elements cannot be reached when using the smartphone with one hand.
- This is especially important to users who are permanently limited to one hand and people temporarily using one hand. 
- Nevertheless, users who can use both hands will benefit from the redesign.
- To address this shortcoming the user flow and the position of action element must be reevaluated.

# Tools
- The new screen design must be prepared in [Penpot](https://penpot.app).

# Hints
- Mind that SnackBar messages must have their space to appear on screen.
- Horizontal scrolling of action elements of the same hierarchy might be worth looking into. Can be found in the Gmail app (search screen).

Title: Design static graphics for empty screens
Body:
Status: :green_circle: Ready to work on

# Preliminaries
- Screens such as favorites, alarms and schedule updates are empty by default.

![favorites-empty](https://github.com/EventFahrplan/EventFahrplan/assets/144518/9043e5c6-324e-4827-a9b0-da6666c7659d) ![alarms-empty](https://github.com/EventFahrplan/EventFahrplan/assets/144518/d7ee8045-e6c0-4c81-a92a-46cf802e90b7) ![schedule-changes-empty](https://github.com/EventFahrplan/EventFahrplan/assets/144518/f3a447eb-a36b-409f-b09f-cc5faef4af2c)

- A textual instruction is shown to tell users what to expect and how to use the individual feature.

# Task
- Create a static graphic for each screen which illustrates the given information, eases to understand the empty state and makes the screen itself more pleasant or even funny.
- Take other apps such as Gmail, Google Contacts (search) as a source of inspiration.
- List references below or link them if available online.

# Screens
- favorites
- alarms
- schedule updates
- speakers (does not exist yet)
- search results (does not exist yet, see #3)
- initial installation, no schedule available to load yet

# Requirements
- The graphics must be designed to work both on bright and on dark backgrounds and with different colors. Take a look at [existing EventFahrplan apps](https://play.google.com/store/apps/dev?id=6885964602402432210) to see color variants.
- The graphics must use one color to allow [tinting](https://developer.android.com/jetpack/compose/graphics/images/customize#tint-image).
- Use plain SVG. The project file format can be application specific, e.g. Adobe Illustrator, Inkscape.
- The graphics must be themed neutral enough to work for all kinds of events and not themed for a specific event context, e.g. a tech conference, acrobatic gathering.
- Must be licensed as CC BY-SA 4.0. The creator of the graphics will be attributed in the repository of the project.

# Hints
- The project files and the plain SVGs must be stored in the `assets` folder of this repository.

Title: Skip sessions with parsing errors
Body:
# Acknowledgments
Please check the following boxes with an `x` if they apply:
* [x] I have read the recent version of the [contribution guide](
  https://github.com/EventFahrplan/EventFahrplan/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) before creating this pull request.
* [x] I have checked a few other pull requests to see how they are written.
* [x] The feature I want to propose would be useful for the majority of users, not only for me personally.
* [x] I am aware that EventFahrplan is mostly developed by one person in their unpaid spare time.
* [x] I can help myself to get this feature implemented or know someone who wants to do it.

# Description
A small change to skip `<event>` elements with parsing errors. Instead of failing to update the entire schedule, this way users will at least see the sessions that could be parsed without error.

# Before
A single parsing error would lead to the entire schedule file not being used at all.

# After
Parsing errors inside an `<event>` tag will only skip that session. 

If one or more sessions have been skipped, this is currently not communicated to the user. So this change should be considered a hack to get to a mostly usable app. It's not a proper fix.

Related: #559

Title: Ngi0 reuse
Body:
Hello,

By way of introduction, I am Jithendra with the Free Software Foundation Europe, a consortium member of the NGI0 Initiative, of which you have signed EventFahrplan up for participation and funding. Part of what is offered with your involvement with NGI0 is help from us with your project on your copyright and license management.

After a quick check on your repository, I would like to propose some updates regarding copyright and licensing information in your files. Our REUSE specification (https://reuse.software/) intends to make licensing easier with best practices to display legal information through comment headers on source files that can be easily human and machine readable. The REUSE tool makes the process of applying licenses to files and compliance checking much easier.

Instructions on how to install the REUSE tool can be found here: https://reuse.readthedocs.io/en/stable/readme.html#install

You can also check out this screencast for more instructions on how to use the REUSE tool: https://download.fsfe.org/videos/reuse/screencasts/reuse-tool.gif

The changes in this pull request have also been made for you to understand the basic ideas behind REUSE, and how adopting the REUSE practices would look like within your repository.

# REUSE Features:

• SPDX copyright and license comment headers for relevant files.
• LICENSES directory with licenses used in the repository
• Associating copyright/licensing information through a DEP5 file in large directories.
Files Missing Copyright and Licensing Information
I've noticed that several files in your repository already contain information about the copyright and license information for the code in that particular file. That's great! Nevertheless, the idea behind REUSE is that every single file in your repository should have a header that includes this information.

To serve as an example, I added the SPDX headers with copyright and license information to the copyrightable files in a few of the directories (namely all the ".kt" files in the /buildSrc and /commons-testing folders). This should give you an idea of how comment headers should look like in a REUSE compliant repository, and how they should be added to the other source code files in your repository.

Please also check if the personal information in these headers are correct and consistent to your knowledge. In the event that there are more copyright holders, please include them in these comment headers.
LICENSES Directory in the Root of the Project Repository

The LICENSES Folder should contain the license text of every license applicable in your repository. I included in this directory the file that contained the Apache 2.0 and CC0 licenses.

Additionally, I included in this folder the text for the CC0 license. This is because you have files in your project that are not copyrightable, for example configuration files such as .gitignore. As the fundamental idea of REUSE is that all of your files will clearly have their copyright and licensing marked, I have applied the CC0 license to .gitignore, which is functionally identical to putting the file into the public domain.

## Image files

Additionally, I noticed that you have two image files ".svg" in the /assets folder. For image files, we recommend creating a .license file, where you can include the comment headers for the license and copyright information. We've added comment headers to all the .svg files in images folder and listed johnjohndoe as the copyright holder. Please feel free to modify and update this to your needs.

Please also note that the changes made through this pull request are only intended to serve as an example so you can get an idea on how REUSE specification works. You can adopt this feature if you find it useful. 

I hope that you find this useful. Feel free to adopt in case you feel REUSE may help your project with copyright and licensing management. Please feel free to contact me directly at [jithendra@fsfe.org](mailto:jithendra@fsfe.org) if you have any questions.

Best,
Jithendra
(Free Software Foundation Europe

Title: Support scrolling to room column from session details
Body:
# Preliminaries
- When browsing a schedule of a large event scrolling to a specific room (track) can be cumbersome if it is "hidden" among a large number of rooms.
- Given a room (track) covers a specific topic (e.g. Kotlin) the user is interested in then it would be convenient to quickly scroll to this particular room (column).

# Steps reproduce
1. Open the session details of a "Kotlin" session

# Current state
2. You need to "leave" the "Kotlin" session details to manually scroll to the "Kotlin" room (column).

# Target state
2. You can tap the room name.
3. The app scrolls to the "Kotlin" room (column).

# Hints
- Session details must be closed on a phone but can stay open on a tablet in landscape mode.
- Other scroll mechanisms must be checked to still work correctly:
  - `MainActivity`: `shouldScheduleScrollToCurrentTimeSlot`
  - `FahrplanFragment/ViewModel`: `preserveVerticalScrollPosition`, `scrollTo`, `scrollToCurrent`
  - `StarredListFragment`: `preserveScrollPosition`, `jumpOverPastSessions`
- Check former `scrolling` issues to see what functionality is implemented: https://github.com/EventFahrplan/EventFahrplan/issues?q=+label%3AScrolling+

Title: Preserve horizontal scrolling position throughout configuration changes
Body:
# Preliminaries
- Preserving the horizontal scrolling position improves the user experience especially when browsing a schedule with many rooms (columns).

# Steps to reproduce
1. Horizontally scroll to a room (column) which is not the first one.
2. Change the system them from light/dark mode to the other.

# Current state
3. The app scrolls back to the first column.

# Target state
3. The app maintains its horizontal scroll position.

# Hints
- Other scroll mechanisms must be checked to still work correctly:
  - `MainActivity`: `shouldScheduleScrollToCurrentTimeSlot`
  - `FahrplanFragment/ViewModel`: `preserveVerticalScrollPosition`, `scrollTo`, `scrollToCurrent`
  - `StarredListFragment`: `preserveScrollPosition`, `jumpOverPastSessions`
- Check former `scrolling` issues to see what functionality is implemented: https://github.com/EventFahrplan/EventFahrplan/issues?q=+label%3AScrolling+

Title: Improve text rendering for session elements
Body:
# Preliminaries
- What is called a "session element" here is an entry in the main schedule grid screen.
- The visual **height** of a session element depends on the **duration** of the talk or workshop.
- A session always has a **title** and an optional **subtitle** - which are shown on the session element.

# Current state
- Text of session elements which is long can be **cut off vertically** by the limited vertical space provided by the physical box of the element.
- Examples:
  
  ![schedule1](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/144518/193638242-ddaa3abb-e66f-4b02-9a0c-71db8eed2500.png)
  ![schedule2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/144518/193637257-7909db43-f2a5-4635-94e3-ed98227df7e9.png)
  ![schedule3](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/144518/193637136-6e6e1507-c16a-4db5-8373-234a63409de6.png)



# Target state
- A possible solution could be to measure the height of the text at rendering time. This would allow to calculate how much text fits in the available space (session element height).
- Text exceeding the available space is **cut off horizontally** and **ellipsized** (`...`) to indicate that text continues.

# Hints
- Check if the Android text rendering system provides any help.
- Since no research has been done on the topic another solution might be applicable. 
  Propose your idea :bulb: here to be discussed before coding or creating a pull request.

Title: Use MenuProvider API
Body:
# Context
- The `MenuProvider` API appeared to decouple menu related code from an Activity or Fragment.
- Docs: https://developer.android.com/reference/kotlin/androidx/core/view/MenuProvider
- `androidx.core:core` release notes: https://developer.android.com/jetpack/androidx/releases/core
 
# Current state
- Menus are created without `MenuProvider`.

# Target state
- Menus are created using `MenuProvider` if possible.

# Hints
- Document which Android version and device models you tested with
- `MenuProvider` added in [`androidx.core:core-ktx:1.7.0`](https://developer.android.com/jetpack/androidx/releases/core#1.7.0)
- `ComponentActivity` implements the `MenuHost` interface as of [`androidx.activity:activity-ktx:1.4.0`](https://developer.android.com/jetpack/androidx/releases/activity#1.4.0).

Title: Only one session is shown when there is a session scheduled for different times (Pretalx)?
Body:
We use pretalx for event scheduling and have a session being repeated on multiple times in the same pretalx event/session.

The pretalx export uses the same `id` for all these sessions but assigns different `guid`s. EventFahrplan only shows the last of these events, missing all the others.

I guess this might be related to #319 ?

By reading some issues, to me it sounds also unclear how sessions with multiple times should be handled:
- same ID or different per time?
- same GUID or different per time?

Logically, the GUID should be unique, but there should be a possibility to link different instances of the same session together, although I don't expect this from EventFahrplan (different Feature request).

Demo Data: https://workshops.acronia.de/acronia-2022/talk/TEQ8ZB/ (Session with multiple timeslots)
https://workshops.acronia.de/acronia-2022/schedule/export/schedule.xml

EventFahrplan only shows the most recent event, 2022-08-21, 09:00–10:00, Black Forest

Title: Update time column highlight based on current time
Body:
# Preliminaries
- The current date matches the selected day.
- The current time is within the time range of sessions of the selected day.
- On main schedule grid screen the **time column** on the left side shows a **highlight** for the current 15min time block.
- To test: Time moves forward, keep the app on screen.

# Current state
- The time column highlight is **not** updated as soon as 15min have passed.

  ![bad](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/144518/180654412-84ae4daa-7e85-42dc-8906-8489679728d4.png)


# Target state
- The time column highlight **automatically updates** as soon as 15min have passed.
 
  ![good](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/144518/180654539-6c61bc11-8fd0-4636-ab5d-83e919056173.png)



# Hints
- 15min should **not be hardcoded** so it can be changed at any point in the future.
- Jump in here: `FahrplanFragment#fillTimes`, `FahrplanViewModel#fillTimes`

Title: Modernize schedule update mechanism when changing settings
Body:
# Current state
- `onActivityResult` is used in `MainActivity` to listen for changes in the `SettingsActivity`, see https://github.com/EventFahrplan/EventFahrplan/blob/4ac75b1db00970f70efde7be5b2490fc88f9fdb9/app/src/main/java/nerd/tuxmobil/fahrplan/congress/schedule/MainActivity.kt#L286-L313
- the `FahrplanFragment` is recreated when needed
- a request is send to the `MainViewModel` when a schedule redraw is needed
- `onActivityResult` is deprecated

# Target state
- A modern mechanism is used to redraw the schedule when needed similar to when new session data is retrieved.
- Updates to settings are routed through the `AppRepository` being the single source of truth for the rendering.

Title: Android 12+: Stretch overscroll effect breaks schedule screen interaction
Body:
# Environment
- Android version: 12 (API 31) or newer
- Branch: `master`

# How to reproduce
1. Visit the schedule screen at the first column
2. Try to scroll before the first column (the column items stretch a little and the spacing on the right is lost)
3. Try to tap a session in the first column to open it

# Observed behavior
- The session won't open anymore.

  https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/144518/178827021-d241026c-cdb8-4488-8b16-1aa9a5dcb584.mp4

# Expected behavior
- The session details are shown.

# Workaround
- Rotate the device to re-render the schedule screen.

# Related
- [Android 12: Behavior changes / Stretch overscroll effect](https://developer.android.com/about/versions/12/behavior-changes-all#overscroll)

Title: Improve Horizontal Fling behavior for the Schedule Screen
Body:
Currently the number of Columns Scrolled when Flinging often feels kind of unpredictable and inconsistent, especially on Handys in portrait mode. I think this is because the amount is calculated by the Velocity with which the user Scrolls and some values. This could probably be improved by always flinging by the number of Columns displayed, effectively lining by one 'page'. Also, if only one Column is displayed, flinging maybe should just be turned off.

Similar behavior can for example be seen in the Google Calendar App.

Title: Add dates to day selection menu entries
Body:
# Current state
- The day selection menu shows an entry for each conference day.
  
  ![screenshot](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/144518/148251324-aae66f76-9634-49b8-ba3b-a226dd2ccc70.png)

- If the current date matches one of the days in the menu then the relevant menu entry is marked with the `- Today` text.

  ![today](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/144518/148251823-0eab8dd4-5833-4877-aa14-84e949547c1e.png)

# Target state

- The day selection menu shows an entry for each conference day and the date formatted as `DD.MM.YYYY`.
  - Example: `Day 1, 27.12.2021`
- If the current date matches one of the days in the menu then the relevant menu entry is marked with the `- Today` text.
  - Example: `Day 1, 27.12.2021 - Today`

## Consideration
- Alternatively, the week day name could be displayed in its short form, e.g. `Mo`, `Tu`, ...
- Given the space limitations of phones in portrait mode only one of the additional information should be shown.
- For now the date seems to be more useful. Convince me otherwise.

Title: List of favorites scrolls to the top when returning from details screen after delay
Body:
# Environment
- App version: [v.1.53.0](https://github.com/EventFahrplan/EventFahrplan/releases/tag/v.1.53.0)
- Android version: not relevant
- Branch: `master`
- Device: not relevant
- Prepare a list of favorites which is longer than the screen is high

# How to reproduce
1. Set the system date and time to match the first day of the conference.
2. Visit the favorites screen, scroll to the bottom of the list.
3. Tap a session at the bottom of the list which opens the details screen.
4. **Wait** for a few seconds, then press BACK to return to the favorites screen.

# Observed behavior
- The favorites list does **not** preserve the former scroll position but instead scrolls to the **top** of the list.

## Screen recording

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/144518/147690155-3ee950fe-c4b1-45cd-a321-1c7b8b31d879.mp4

# Expected behavior
- The favorites list **does preserve** the former scroll position.

# Where to start
- `StarredListFragment#jumpOverPastSessions` and `StarredListFragment#observeViewModel`

# Related
- [Issue #362: List of favorites scrolls to the top when returning from details screen](https://github.com/EventFahrplan/EventFahrplan/issues/362)

Title: Configure code coverage with JaCoCo
Body:
# Current state
- The project is configured for https://sonarcloud.io via [_gradle/sonarqube.gradle_](https://github.com/EventFahrplan/EventFahrplan/blob/master/gradle/sonarqube.gradle). See https://sonarcloud.io/dashboard?id=eventfahrplan.
- SonarQube is being used from time to time to check the current statistics. This happens by manually executing either `./gradlew sonarqube` on the `master` branch or `./gradlew sonarqube -Dsonar.branch.name=feature-x` on a feature branch.
- SonarQube/SonarCloud does not recognize code coverage:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/144518/114550025-06d0b000-9c62-11eb-8e3e-9a0e7b4bb5e2.png)
- The `com.vanniktech:gradle-android-junit-jacoco-plugin` Plugin is used to generate code coverage reports locally by executing one of the `jacocoTestReport` Gradle tasks.

# TODOs
- [ ] Check if SonarCloud allows that multiple people can push updates.
- [ ] Configure the project so code coverage is tracked every time `./gradlew sonarqube` is executed.
- [ ] Ensure code coverage reports generated locally still work in the browser.
- [ ] Check if code coverage can be generated for all project modules (Java, Kotlin, Android).
- [ ] Check if a merged (single file) code coverage report can be generated for all project modules.

# Playground
The following volatile branches have been used to try out different things. They can be used as an inspiration. Commits on theses branches are explicitely work-in-progress!
- `jacoco-report-path-deprecated`
- `jacoco-experiments`
- `jacoco-coverage`

# Resources
An unsorted list of websites which might be helpful to resolve above tasks.

Title: Feature request: Be able to specify data directory
Body:
I don't sync my taskwarrior with a taskserver. Instead I use syncthing so sync my taskdata directory between my devices. This works great between my computers, but not with my phone. 

I think it would be great to specify the ```/path/to/taskdata``` in the app, and let foreground use that directory.. Since foreground stores the tasks *somewhere* then I think letting the user specify where should be a nice feature.

Title: CA Certificate setting behaving incorrectly
Body:
I have a server with a certificate issued by Gandi. The certificate configured with taskd (and other services I have setup) contains the intermediate certificate in the chain.

I have taskd working with a desktop client. I generated the client CA and certificates using the taskwarrior scripts.

I tried to setup Foreground using the exact same certificates and settings as on the desktop, using the client CA and certificates generated. I get the error "trust anchor for certification path not found".

As soon as I set the "CA certificate" in Foreground to the **Gandi** intermediate CA certificate it works. 

My assumption was that the CA certificate was supposed to be the generated one for taskd clients, just like as configured in taskrc. If that is the case something is wrong with handling the client certificates.

If the CA certificate is instead supposed to be for verifying self signed certificates, Foreground is not respecting intermediate certificates.

Title: Explicit save button or explicit feedback on save?
Body:
It seems to me that anything I start writing is automatically saved, I even saw an empty task being created and then removed, but I also managed to somehow go back and loose my edit at least once.

My issue is that there is no feedback on the action. I often find myself scrolling to the bottom to see if the task was added. I think explicit save/discard buttons would be more easy to follow, but at least some sort of visual feedback whether the task was added or not would be very welcome.

Sorry if I missed something!

Title: Existing taskwarrior tasks are not synced after first setup
Body:
Hello, I set up taskwarrior sync with my personal taskd server.

Foreground says it's syncing correctly, however it does not show any of the 50 tasks that were already there before I set up Foreground.

It does show _new_ tasks, for example if I create a new task in taskwarrior from my laptop, then sync to the server and then sync from Foreground the new task appears correctly.

Might be a duplicate of #14 but I couldn't fully understand the description of that issue. Feel free to close as duplicate if that's the case (but I see there haven't been any reply to that issue in 4 years...)

Title: [feature request] support reordering of saved filters
Body:
When a new filter is created, it's appended as a new entry to the end of the filter list. Currently, it's not really possible to reorder the list entries.

afaik, the only way to reorder the list is by manually removing and re-adding entries to the list in the right order. This is a very tedious workaround, especially is the filter list has many entries.

Therefore, it would be a big improvement if list entries could be reordered in a more user friendly, intuitive way. (For example, by long-pressing on an entry + dragging it to a different list position)

Title: [Feature request] Ability to set the time format (or use phone default)
Body: Currently the app uses am/pm however I'm from denmark and the our default is 24-hour format, so having am/pm often means I get it wrong and set an event for late evening or early in the morning.

Title: [feature request] support "depends" in tasks
Body: The [taskwarrior documentation](https://taskwarrior.org/docs/best-practices/)  suggests using the `depends:` functionality to represent dependencies between tasks. It would be nice to be able to set/modify task dependencies in Foreground

Title: Taskd tx.data file invalid after modifying task with dependencies in Foreground
Body:
If I have a task that has dependencies, and I complete the task using Foreground, my other Taskwarrior clients stop being able to sync to my taskd server, and instead start reporting "Unrecognized Taskwarrior file format or blank line in data."

Looking at the tx.data file, I see the following (whitespace added for clarity):

```json
{
  "description":"test2",
  "uuid":"73ecca6c-f6c2-4abb-ad35-80a5a8884eba",
  "project":"",
  "status":"completed",
  "priority":"",
  "modified":"20230616T214045Z",
  "end":"20230616T214045Z",
  "entry":"20230616T213531Z",
  "tags":["inbox"],
  "annotations":[],
  "depends":"[\"dcc67934-6230-46e2-aa04-846d31efd329\"]"
}
```

Note the `depends` entry; that should be `"depends":["dcc67934-6230-446e2-aa04-846d31efd329"]`.

The above test case was created with the following (the inbox tagging is a custom hook I have):

```
$ taskd add user adam test
New user key: e65cfbf7-9183-424b-8d54-46798c3afe76
Created user 'test' for organization 'adam'

$ TASKDATA=/tmp/task task rc.taskd.credentials:adam/test/e65cfbf7-9183-424b-8d54-46798c3afe76 add test
TASKDATA override: /tmp/task
Created task 1.
Configuration override rc.taskd.credentials:adam/test/e65cfbf7-9183-424b-8d54-46798c3afe76
Tagged test as inbox

$ TASKDATA=/tmp/task task rc.taskd.credentials:adam/test/e65cfbf7-9183-424b-8d54-46798c3afe76 add test2 depends:1
TASKDATA override: /tmp/task
Created task 2.
Configuration override rc.taskd.credentials:adam/test/e65cfbf7-9183-424b-8d54-46798c3afe76
Tagged test2 as inbox

$ TASKDATA=/tmp/task task rc.taskd.credentials:adam/test/e65cfbf7-9183-424b-8d54-46798c3afe76 sync init
Please confirm that you wish to upload all your tasks to the Taskserver (yes/no) y
TASKDATA override: /tmp/task
Syncing with host:port

Configuration override rc.taskd.credentials:adam/test/e65cfbf7-9183-424b-8d54-46798c3afe76
Sync successful.  2 changes uploaded.

<Clear all data from the Foreground app, set it up to sync to the new test user, complete the "test2" task, sync again>

$ TASKDATA=/tmp/task task rc.taskd.credentials:adam/test/e65cfbf7-9183-424b-8d54-46798c3afe76 sync
TASKDATA override: /tmp/task
Configuration override rc.taskd.credentials:adam/test/e65cfbf7-9183-424b-8d54-46798c3afe76
Unrecognized Taskwarrior file format or blank line in data.
```

This seems similar to #110 to me, but that issue has been fixed.

Title: Support Virtual Tags in filters
Body:
A virtual tag is a tag that doesn't actually exist, but the tag filter syntax is used to simulate the tag, providing a very convenient shortcut. After all, composing a filter to match the tasks due today is not straightforward.  (from: https://taskwarrior.org/docs/examples.html#virtualtags and https://taskwarrior.org/docs/tags.html#supported ) 

              Since version 2.2.0, Taskwarrior has supported virtual tags, and
              the list will continue to grow. Here is the full list of supported
              virtual tags:

BLOCKED | Is the task dependent on another incomplete task?
UNBLOCKED | The opposite of BLOCKED, for convenience. Note +BLOCKED == -UNBLOCKED and vice versa.
BLOCKING | Does another task depend on this incomplete task?
DUE | Is this task due within 7 days? Determined by rc.due
DUETODAY | Is this task due sometime today?
TODAY | Is this task due sometime today?
OVERDUE | Is this task past it’s due date?
WEEK | Is this task due this week? 2.3.0
MONTH | Is this task due this month? 2.3.0
QUARTER | Is this task due this quarter? 2.6.0
YEAR | Is this task due this year? 2.3.0
ACTIVE | Is the task active, ie does it have a start date?
SCHEDULED | Is the task scheduled, ie does it have a scheduled date?
PARENT | Is the task a hidden parent recurring task? 2.3.0
CHILD | Is the task a recurring child task?
UNTIL | Does the task expire, ie does it have an until date?
WAITING | Is the task hidden, ie does it have a wait date?
ANNOTATED | Does the task have any annotations?
READY | Is the task pending, not blocked, and either not scheduled,                     or scheduled before now. 2.4.0
YESTERDAY | Was the task due yesterday? 2.4.0
TOMORROW | Is the task due tomorrow? 2.4.0
TAGGED | Does the task have any tags?
PENDING | Is the task in the pending state? 2.4.0
COMPLETED | Is the task in the completed state? 2.4.0
DELETED | Is the task in the deleted state? 2.4.0
UDA | Does the task contain any UDA values? 2.5.0
ORPHAN | Does the task contain any orphaned UDA values? 2.5.0
PRIORITY | Does the task have a priority? 2.5.0
PROJECT | Does the task have a project? 2.5.0
LATEST | Is the task the most recently added task? 2.5.0

These are great, super convenient shorthand, and shouldn't be too hard to implement; there's a limited number of them, they're not that complex, and they're well defined/ documented.

Title: Consider parsing a subset of the Taskwarrior expression syntax and grammar
Body:
There are some existing issues about providing logical OR (https://github.com/bgregos/foreground/issues/112), as well as some that consider doing this via existing Taskwarrior features that are usually set in `TASKRC` (https://github.com/bgregos/foreground/issues/116, https://github.com/bgregos/foreground/issues/108, https://github.com/bgregos/foreground/issues/49).

I won't attempt to illustrate what kinds of expressions users will expect to use in Foreground. Others might have comment.

I did want to make this request explicit, as it seems to be currently implicitly alluded to in a few other issues. The challenges or strategies might begin to be outlined here.

To get a sense of the problem, we might consider the evaluation implementation at <https://github.com/GothenburgBitFactory/taskwarrior/blob/v2.5.3/src/Eval.cpp>.

Without thinking much about it, I assumed we would just be parsing expressions like `+home or +phone`. Or `(+a or +b) and +c`.

But reviewing the above link, I realize it's a bit more complicated.

And reviewing `task show filter` reminds me we'd start adding other filters to parse, like `status:pending`, etc.

The above is just to suggest that the problem might be a bit complicated, and perhaps proves that parsing the Taskwarrior syntax should perhaps not block adding other alternative conveniences to Foreground, for example being able to filter on a union of constraints, without the use of Taskwarrior's syntax for report and context filters.

On the other hand, I had an idea on a library to use once and if work on a parser begins. The PetitParser library might be a good fit, at <https://github.com/petitparser/java-petitparser>.

And, in the title, I suggest covering just a subset of the expression syntax and grammar, but I'm not actually sure if that is practical. Maybe someone can help with ideas about how it could be reasonable to cover only a subset of the grammar, to make the initial work more tractable.

Note, if you hard-code the default reports, you can get around this issue until parsing is added.

Title: Null Pointer Dereference on Project Colour Selection
Body:
## Brief Description

App crashes when trying to select colour for new project.

## Action version

Could use this app only for 15 seconds, because it crashes, when I try to start using it. So, nothing is set up, yet.

## Workflow Configuration

Could use this app only for 15 seconds, because it crashes, when I try to start using it. So, nothing is set up, yet.

## Steps To Reproduce

1. Install App
2. Open App
3. Click "Create new Project"
4. Try to select Project Colour
5. See App Crash

## Expected behavior

Title: Releases on github
Body:
## :frowning_person: Problem Statement

This project doesn't use github to release official apk. As of now the most official sources for apk are Google Play and F-Droid. Neither are recommended for privacy [[1]](https://www.privacyguides.org/en/android/). Further apps in the official F-Droid repository often fall behind on updates [[2]](https://www.privacyguides.org/en/android/#f-droid).

## :grey_question: Possible Solution

Release official apk with github release. We already have the version tags.

This would mean that the most up-to-date apk would always be available via github releases.

## :arrow_heading_up: Describe alternatives you've considered

Forget about privacy and up-to-date releases?

A release, possibly a beta or unstable release. Unstable release could be released as a separate app, or alternatively on a custom fdroid repo.

Title: Serve web app locally
Body:
## Problem Statement

Currently the Android app connects to https://app.super-productivity.com to load necessary source code to function. For an ToDo / time tracking app that can and is intended to function locally - except from optional task syncing - that's an unnecessary step just transferring data that could be leaked and adding attack surface that could be abused.

## :grey_question: Possible Solution

The best option would probably be to serve the web app locally and therefore embed all needed files inside the Android app itself. That would prevent the former existent connection to be manipulated, spied on or abused otherwise by an attacker.

## :arrow_heading_up: Describe alternatives you've considered

Other alternatives would be:

Give users a option to use a self-hosted instance of the super productivity web app. This wouldn't eliminate the problem completely but users wouldn't have to trust a foreign, uncontrollable and unverifiable web app anymore, because users could set up their own instance or use one they trust. Therefore, the problem would be minimized a bit.

The other option would be to inform the users at the first start of the Android app that a connection to your servers are necessary for super productivity to function properly. This wouldn't avoid the problem, but at least users would know and can actively decide if that's okay with them or not.

## :heavy_plus_sign: Additional context

A pleasant side effect would be that bugs like #3 would probably be avoided :-)

Title: App fails to start without internet connection
Body:
# Current behaviour

When I run the Android App, it doesn't work when there is no internet connection. You just get an error page that the website https://app.super-productivity.com couldn't load. So the App seems to load something from your web server and just fails to start if it can't.

My question here would be, what does the app try to load from your webserver? Is the app just a wrapper for the web app and  loads all the html, css, js content from your webserver at each start? Or is it just some update check?

# Suggested behaviour

It would be great if the app would work offline, too.

Apparently the URLs are published in the XML available to app developers: https://fosdem.org/2023/schedule/xml

Ideally it would be possible to leverage WebSub for efficient delivery of information. But until that's available, I guess some kind of frequent polling would have to set up.

I know I have notifications at my disposal but that isn't always as convenient in this situation as it has less info than the bookmarks list.

Therefore I was wondering if a button (e.g. FAB) could be introduced that allows to jump to the current time (in the list).

I imagine that such button could be added both on the bookmarks and on the individual track screens.

Maybe it should be shown only in case the length of the list is like 1.25 times the height of the screen. (Because otherwise it has little to no value.)

Title: [Feature request] Add different calendar views
Body:
I have been having thoughts about moving my tasks to a calendar app.  The advantage is that I have a week view and can see in one screen a schedule of the upcoming tasks and the time which those tasks need to started/ended.  To allow this, ALL tasks must have a date and time whether it be a Start/End Date or a Reminder Date/Time.  However, I like some of my tasks to have no Date/Time.

Currently, the Calendar view only shows the current month with no "summary" of what the tasks are within each day of the month.

Calendar apps (including Microsoft Outlook) generally have the following views:

- A view of the day and the hours within the day
- 7 days display on the screen with the hours within each day
- A month calendar with a compact display of each task within each day

I don't expect the different calendar views to be exactly like calendar apps because SecUSo ToDo is not a a calendar app.

This Feature Request is obviously not easy, but I log this so that it is in the records.

Title: 'isunlock' may always be false when 'onSaveInstanceState' in 'MainActivity'
Body:
I notice that in 'MainActivity', both 'onStop' and 'onSaveInstanceState' use the field 'isunlock'. However, accoding to the Android official documents, the execution order of these two lifecycle callbacks may reverse after Android API 28. 
In this case, if users use devices at or over  API 28, 'isunlock' is always set to false in 'onStop' and afterwards saved into the bundle in 'onSaveInstanceState'.

Now, you might say to remove my custom event and use the Google one, but in the custom one, I use a different image(of both my father and mum) and title (eg.- ma-pa's anniversary) whereas the Google one will show my dad's image and title is "Dad" anniversary.

Title: New Widget Upcoming Events Visibility
Body:

The new widget is gorgeous, but it's got a flaw: upcoming events (below next birthday) are visible even if there's very little space to show them. This means that below the name of the next person whose birthday it is next, there's a grey and scrollable bar, which is actually the bubble that contains the upcoming events when the widget is bigger.

This might have been seen as as design element, but the scroll indicator from Android (which is on the right of the screen and shows scroll progress) is temporary visible when unlocking the phone or going on the home screen, then disappear (see the screenshots).

Title: Multiple events for each contact
Body:
Right now it appears each event is individual.
It would be nice if all events were all listed for the contact when editing or viewing.

Title: Importing individual events
Body:
Currently, there is no way to do this automatically except for importing a backup, which overwrites existing data.
However, there is an option for sharing a pre-formatted block of text that gives essential information about the event, eg.

🎈  Birday Reminder
🥳  Dominik Novosel (Birthday)
🕯  Thursday, May 4, 2023
📅  Wednesday, May 4, 2005

The simplest possible solution (or at least the most user-friendly) would be to have the user copy and paste this into a text entry field, whereupon Birday would analyze it, pull out the relevant data and save it as a new entry. This seems super practical for sharing since it's just text that can be sent through any messaging service.

Drawbacks:
- How would this play with the translations?
- Do Kotlin string methods support emojis?
- Error correction?
- Varying date and name formats
- UI considerations (where to put this option?)

Title: Include user settings in backup file
Body:
Currently, the backup database only restores event entries, but nothing from the settings page is retained, which is annoying if one happens to not remember what their settings were, or simply due to the tedium of changing them all again.

It seems to me that the backups are, at least partially, intended for sharing, which is problematic in and of itself, because all existing entries are overwritten when importing and it's not possible to transfer individual events, and I'll open another issue for this. Regardless, to try to partially accommodate this use case and avoid forcing the user's settings upon someone else, some simple changes to the import logic and interface could allow the user to choose whether to only import the events, the settings, or both.

Title: Date format differences when year is included or not
Body:
Can it be possible to have Birday have a standard format for the birthday.

Currently, `3.1.1 (21)`, Birday displays the date differently based on whether a year is included or not.

e.g. 

- with year:   day month year
- without year:   month, day.


It would be useful to have both instances display the same,  preferably  dd-mm[-yyyy]  and or [yyyy-]mm-dd

Title: Support moving dates
Body:
It would be handy to also track moving dates, i.e. dates that aren't steady every year. For example, today is Mother's Day in many countries in the world. Mother's Day doesn't have a steady date, but is instead celebrated on the second Sunday of May. That will be 14 May in 2023 and 12 May in 2024.

I'm not sure how such feature would be implemented, but it would be nice to have.

If you would be so kind to do that Id love it even more. Thanks for devoting your time to this app. cheers

Title: track unbirthdays too: day of the week + day of month
Body:
Many people dislike the date of their birthday for some reason, for instance: if they were born on Christmas. An alternative birthday to celebrate is the day of the week plus the day of the month. My grandmother was born on Friday the 13th. She sometimes would celebrate Friday the 13th as though it were a birthday. IIRC, I think I was born on Sunday the 28th, so I could celebrate any month when the 28th is also a Sunday. The term "unbirthday" has been used a number of different ways, but while this isn't the most popular use, I think this is the only well-defined and useful definition of unbirthdays that I have come across.

Unbirthdays can occur up to twice a year, but are also absent some years, and also sometimes fall on the birthday itself. This is the kind of thing it would be helpful to rely on an app to track.

Title: Major ICSx⁵ rewrite
Body:
After much consideration, and looking at the current state of ICSx⁵, we have decided to make a major rewrite of the whole app, including the interface, which would be migrated to Jetpack Compose.

The features will be the same, no new things will be added in this movement, but it will, be a way of making sure the app is stable enough.

[v2.1.1](https://github.com/bitfireAT/icsx5/releases/tag/v2.1.1) works fine enough for most people, but the latest beta releases have been quite buggy, and since this will take a while for sure, we recommend using the latest stable version until the new one is released.

If anyone wants to follow the development of the new version, it will be held on https://github.com/ArnyminerZ/icsx5 instead of this repo. Suggestions are welcome, but I can't guarantee that they will be included.

Title: Android 14
Body:
The release of Android 14 is almost here, and I think we can now start thinking about checking if we can safely move to SDK 34. I'll keep an updated list here of all the breaking changes, and if we meet them. If anything needs to be changed I'll try to say so, or open a PR for that. This will also be useful for doing the same with DAVx5.

- [**Behavior changes**](https://developer.android.com/about/versions/14/behavior-changes-14):
  - [Foreground service types are required](https://developer.android.com/about/versions/14/behavior-changes-14#fgs-types) and [Foreground service types are required](https://developer.android.com/about/versions/14/changes/fgs-types-required)
    
    Shouldn't cause any issues right now, but WorkManager [might create foreground services if necessary](https://developer.android.com/guide/background/persistent/how-to/long-running) so I think we should definetely add this. Also it will be required for showing progress on #102 
  - [Enforcement of BLUETOOTH_CONNECT permission in BluetoothAdapter](https://developer.android.com/about/versions/14/behavior-changes-14#enforce-bluetooth_connect)
  
    We do not use bluetooth.
  - [OpenJDK 17 updates](https://developer.android.com/about/versions/14/behavior-changes-14#core-libraries)
    
    Everything should be fine, however we should check for any UUID parsing or Regex issues. Maybe inside all the libraries.
  - [JobScheduler reinforces callback and network behavior](https://developer.android.com/about/versions/14/behavior-changes-14#jobscheduler-reinforces-behavior)
    
    WorkManager should handle this.
  - [Restrictions to implicit and pending intents](https://developer.android.com/about/versions/14/behavior-changes-14#safer-intents)
    
    Should not be problematic.
  - [Runtime-registered broadcasts receivers must specify export behavior](https://developer.android.com/about/versions/14/behavior-changes-14#runtime-receivers-exported)
    
    ICSx5 doesn't use broadcast receivers.
  - [Safer dynamic code loading](https://developer.android.com/about/versions/14/behavior-changes-14#safer-dynamic-code-loading)

    ICSx5 doesn't use DCL.
  - [Zip path traversal](https://developer.android.com/about/versions/14/behavior-changes-14#zip-path-traversal)

    ICSx5 doesn't use Zip files.
  - [Additional restrictions on starting activities from the background](https://developer.android.com/about/versions/14/behavior-changes-14#background-activity-restrictions)

    ICSx5 doesn't launch activities from the background.
- [Schedule exact alarms are denied by default](https://developer.android.com/about/versions/14/changes/schedule-exact-alarms)

  WorkManager should handle this. However, we do not use exact alarms. However, we should still add some flexibility to the workers with [retry and backoff policies](https://developer.android.com/guide/background/persistent/getting-started/define-work#retries_backoff).
- [Grant partial access to photos and videos](https://developer.android.com/about/versions/14/changes/partial-photo-video-access)

  ICSx5 doesn't access photos or videos.
- [Data safety information is more visible](https://developer.android.com/about/versions/14/changes/data-safety)

  Updating Google Play page is enough.
- [Migrate foreground services to user-initiated data transfer jobs](https://developer.android.com/about/versions/14/changes/user-initiated-data-transfers)

  Since we use WorkManager, we don't have control over this (as far as I have found).

### New features
We don't need to add this for migration, but I will link the ones that I think we should add to our apps.

- [Add support for built-in and custom predictive back animations](https://developer.android.com/about/versions/14/features/predictive-back) and [Support for built-in and custom animations for Predictive Back](https://developer.android.com/about/versions/14/features#predictive-back-animations)
- [Per-app language preferences](https://developer.android.com/about/versions/14/features#app-languages)

Depends on #173
Depends on #175
Depends on #176

Title: Add copyright information in About dialog
Body:
I came across this because HUAWEI complained for "missing" copyright information. Missing in the sense that no entity is responsible for the uploaded app (and visible inside the app) - which is not compliant with their app distribution policy (anymore?). I also had to add our business license for DAVx5 last year and did so for ICSx5 now.

Currently we only have the GPLV3 link in the About dialog (for all stores the same) without any legal entity or names. I think we need to add bitfire as copyright owner for this like we do it with DAVx5. But I also think that we can keep the GPLV3 link. So then it is clearly shown that bitfire licenses the software under GPL and we also don't have to make flavors in Android studio to deal with different information for certain stores.

Title: Backup export and import
Body:
Following the discussion #69. Provide an option to export and import the database. This would provide backup functionality for subscriptions, but maybe we want to also move preferences to the database (eg. dark mode toggle) so it's exported together with the subscriptions.

Overall I'd say that the implementation steps would be:
- [x] Create a database import algorithm
- [x] Create a database export algorithm
- [x] Provide an extra option in the overflow menu in subscriptions list. Maybe we can add a "Backup" option, and then show a dialog to choose between import and export, or add a submenu to select this option. However, the second approach would not allow to show a warning to tell the users information about the backup, such as that importing would overwrite the existing data, or that backups are not encrypted.

Title: enabled calendars aren't kept in sync
Body:
The "calendar window" is only used when initially adding calendars (triggered by clicking a checkbox), however the pref implies that calendars are periodically updated. Entries should be periodically added and removed to keep calendars in sync within this window. 

Currently adding or removing entries is a one-time event triggered by the user. They must uncheck/recheck the enabled pref to get calendars in sync. Considering the "window" is specified in years this isn't really much of a problem, but it would be really nice if it was automatic.
